Page 1163
AVEVA AIM Workhub Access Control 
An object is removed from the public domain by associating it to a security access group object with the security 
access group association type ("is in security access group"/"is a security access group for"). The protected 
object is then only accessible by users who are indirectly associated to that object through a security access 
group. 
Note: Several security access groups may be associated to an object, and a user gains access to the object by 
being associated to any of these. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1164
Associations and Templates 
The models illustrate how objects and associations are organized and instanced for specific purposes or 
functions. This is not the limit of associations that can be created or used. The whole notion of ISO 15926 is to 
create a fully flexible model that can be used to represent any data object or association. In most data 
warehouse implementations, this is typically at a very granular level. To improve the ‘human readability’ and the 
system's ‘repeatability/reuse’ of these objects, the idea of templating these objects and associations together is 
finding acceptance as a preferred modelling method. 
Templates in the AIM Workhub are in two categories: 
• Atomic Templates (AT): is indivisible semantically and normally forms the smallest usable building block of 
objects 
• Molecular Templates (MT): is indivisible by business implementation, and represents logical groups of 
objects that are typically familiar to an end user 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1165
The following sections describe typical ATs and the context in which they are used. 
Identification with Uniqueness Context 
Business Usage Semantics (incl Cardinality) 
Identification involves assigning a string or other encoded symbol to an object in order for it to be used 
subsequently to make unambiguous reference to that object. 
Each object registered with EIF is to be captured at first installation using the OBR Template, which implicitly 
includes one embedded instance of the IDC Template. This separate IDC Template is therefore primarily intended 
for assigning additional identifiers (or aliases) to an object already registered. These additional (unique) 
identifiers (with their own contexts) are intended to enable sets of users in these particular contexts/phases of 
the business to navigate and access information via their preferred identification schemes, without losing 
management of underlying unique identity, and associated consolidation. 
For objects which are versionable (essentially all associations and information objects) "version" is an essential 
part of identification. However physically implemented/constructed, identification therefore consists of <Context 
Symbol><Unique Symbol><Version Symbol> 
Implementation Notes/Issues/Suggestions 
An important aspect of EIF implementation is that not only will interfaces exist to external systems which create, 
manipulate and persist identifiers, but middle-ware components may themselves be distributed and integrated 
with other middle-ware systems. Whilst internal to EIF, controlled system numbering (for example, uid’s) is 
expected, it is essential that uniqueness of identification is managed and validated according to the agreed 
standard template at all interfaces. 
Often business context produces standard formatted forms for the unique symbolic string – typically referred to 
as "tags". Often such tags contain embedded encoded information about the context, the type, and other 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1166
attributes of the identified object. It is essential that the "unique identification" aspect and the implicit or explicit 
"encoded information" are handled distinctly. (See separate notes on identification, tags and aliases.) 
Implicit in this template is that the "context" part of the identification is handled distinct from the unique 
symbolic string, whereas the "version" component is embedded in the unique symbolic string. This is an 
acceptable implementation at the interface, since the same template can capture identification, whether the 
object is versionable or not. It is anticipated that versioning/time-stamping/suppression-tree aspects will be 
handled through meta-data in implementation, however additional formal templates may be generated for this 
purpose as necessary. 
Inherent in the EIWM template model is the nested assembly of information sets – which itself provides useful 
context hierarchies, very similar to the construction of XML Namespaces/URL’s/URI’s. it is recommended that 
this fact is exploited in efficient handling of identifiers and contexts. 
Human Naming 
Business Usage Semantics (incl Cardinality) 
An object may be given any number of names as useful human "handles." 
The naming template does not require or exploit uniqueness of the name, and EIF therefore provides no 
management of any such uniqueness. Where uniqueness is intended by the business, the IDC template shall be 
used. 
The Name string is generally descriptive for human interpretation. Naming assumes that the context (compound 
template) in which the name is created makes the name sufficiently intelligible and recognizable self-evidently to 
human users. 
An optional HNA atomic template is part of the OBR registration template on first instantiation of any object. 
Implementation Notes / Issues / Suggestions 
Generally the "Name" is presented for human recognition, often for corroboration or confirmation together with 
other formal IDC identifiers where there is selection ambiguity or in contexts where uncertainty of human 
recognition is possible. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1167
Object Registration (Registry Entry) 
Business Usage Semantics (incl Cardinality) 
Every object of interest, of which the EIF is expected to take some part in its future management, or of 
information about it, must be "Registered" at least once with unique identification and essential classification. 
The "human name" is an optional component for cross checking purposes, particularly but not exclusively where 
the identification string or symbol, whilst strictly unique, is not in a form for useful human interpretation. 
See IDC, CLE and NAM template definitions for individual semantics. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1168
Implementation Notes / Issues / Suggestions 
See IDC, CLE and NAM template definitions for individual implementation notes. 
Templates and Registered Objects in general are distinct objects each with identity in EIF. For instances of the 
OBR Atomic Template, ID string for the real world object is intended to be used as a surrogate for the ID of the 
template instance itself, in order to avoid circular references / enable bootstrapping. 
Essential Classification 
Business Usage Semantics (incl Cardinality) 
Every object handled within EIF shall be essentially classified at least once. (Generally, for objects captured in the 
Registry, this will be achieved once on instantiation via the Object Registration Template) 
Essential classification concerns the nature of (some aspect of) the object being classified (registered / 
instantiated). It is a specialization of the object base entity type (As per the "upper ontology"), independent of 
the business use of information about the entity, and independent of any additional classification for business 
management and access reasons for chunking and navigation. (See also Incidental Classification). 
Example usages: (Hold) 
Mapping guidelines / rules: (Hold) 
Implementation Notes / Issues / Suggestions 
RDL class objects should be referred to by unique ID (just like any other registered object). Typically 
implementations use the non-unique class "name", however whilst a human interpretable name will always be 
captured at time of first registration (of the class), classes will have alternative names (synonyms) and 
multilingual translations / representations. 
Essential classification(s) should be assumed fixed for the life of an object, however objects may receive 
additional essential (and incidental) classifications during their life as a result of increasing knowledge of more 
aspects of the object. Examples of the occurrence of multiple essential classifications include: 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1169
• Discovering additional specialization (common event) 
• Discovering additional specialization of multiple orthogonal aspects (common event) 
• Discovering erroneous earlier classification (hopefully infrequent event) 
At any one time, one of the essential classifications shall be considered the "default essential classification" of 
the object, for display, navigation, search purposes etc. This default may be re-assigned to any one of its multiple 
essential classifications by an "Information Manager" level user with appropriate access rights. (This may be 
achieved by incidental classification of the essential classification template instance, or by creation of a separate 
atomic template for this purpose, or by association meta-data for the relevant essential classification association, 
since it is an implementation artifact to assign this default). 
Recognition needs to be given to the inheritance aspects of classification – wherever calls make reference to 
(say) "pumps", the normal semantic will include "and all subtypes", unless the query is constructed explicitly 
otherwise to exclude sub-types. One corollary of these last two issues is that as well as "base entity type" and 
"lowest level specialization class", each object will probably need to have recognized a default organizational 
node in that hierarchy. Since this is an implementation artifact, a meta-data implementation is acceptable. 
Incidental Classification 
Business Usage Semantics (incl Cardinality) 
Any object may be incidentally classified (and subsequently de-classified) any number of times. 
Incidental classification is used to create manageable groupings of objects according to any circumstantial (i.e., 
non-intrinsic) aspects of those objects – aspects which are concerned with their (temporary) involvement or 
usage in any aspect of the business. (See Essential Classification for classification according to lifelong intrinsic 
aspects). 
Example usages: (Hold) 
Mapping guidelines / rules: (Hold) 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1170
Implementation Notes / Issues / Suggestions 
None currently recognized. 
Assembly/Collection 
Business Usage Semantics (incl Cardinality) 
The assembly relationship applies between two (whole and part) objects, where the individual part plays a 
distinct or systematic role in the whole (It says nothing about physical assembly or joining). 
The collection relationship applies between two (whole and element) objects, where the individual element 
plays no distinct role in the whole. (It says nothing about physical proximity or gathering together). 
Example usages: 
• Pump P102 is a part of System S100. 
• Filter F103 is part of System S100. 
These are assembly relationships, because although both P102 and F103 are "parts" of the system, they are not 
wholly interchangeable in their relationship to the system. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1171
Pump MfrZ2002/02/317/M is an element of Shipment PO192/7 
Filter MfrX2001/04FQP4/23 is (also) an element of Shipment PO192/7 
This instance of Model no BD/PG4010scx is an element of "The set of available 10 bar pressure gauges." 
That instance of Model no BD/PG4010scx is (also) an element of "The set of available 10 bar pressure gauges." 
These are collection relationships, because there is no different significance in the way the distinct elements 
relate to the whole. 
Mapping guidelines / rules: 
If in doubt map whole-part relationships to Assembly. In general Collection tends to be about "discovering" 
useful sets of things post-hoc, and set membership is ultimately indistinguishable from incidental classification. 
Implementation Notes / Issues / Suggestions 
None. 
Template Composition by Ownership and Reference 
Business Usage Semantics (incl Cardinality) 
A "Template" is an information object whose (relevant) schema is defined according to this EIWM Template 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1172
model. They exist as both the Template Schema and Template Instances according to those schema. See the 
EIWM Overview graphic for the relationship between Templates of various kinds (AT’s, MT’s and DT’s). These TCO 
and TCR AT’s define assembly relationships between other AT’s, MT’s and DT’s. 
These template composition AT’s are simply specializations of the ‘Assembly’ AT, applicable when the objects in 
question are Templates. The semantic differences are: 
TCO applies when it is intended that instances share "ownership", that is the part inherits "publish" (create, 
update and delete) rights from the whole instance. A whole can have many owned parts, but a part can have 
ONLY ONE OWNING PARENT / whole at any one time. 
TCR applies when the part is included (by reference) in the whole, but the instances retain independent 
ownership and publish rights, and is effectively a "subscribe" relationship. (Parts can be referred to any number 
of times by any number of parent wholes). 
Examples and Mapping Guidelines: To follow on request. 
Implementation Notes / Issues / Suggestions 
Need to address Template Schema which confer the above semantics on their instances. 
Need to establish minimum essential meta-data content associated with template instances. 
Need to consider whether owned parts should also inherit versioning and lifestyle related meta-data, in which 
case the parts effectively merge into the whole (the whole-part relationships become redundant) for all practical 
purposes for the instances, except for configuration management of changing schema. (Alternative 
specializations of Template Composition relationships are conceivable depending on implementation 
preferences). 
Property with Value/Units 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1173
Business Usage Semantics (incl Cardinality) 
This AT is used to assign a real value quantified property to an object (with units unless the property is 
dimensionless ratio). 
Any registered object may possess any number of properties. (The same property may in principle be possessed 
by any number of registered objects, though this would involve registering and uniquely identifying the property 
itself). 
Implementation Notes / Issues / Suggestions 
It is permitted to use specializations of this AT for specific property types (and / or units) if this provides any 
implementation benefits. (Ex: Possession of Design Temperature in Degrees Centigrade say). 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1174
About Information Linking Groups of ATs 
Business Usage Semantics (incl Cardinality) 
This AT provides the basic "Asset Linking" mechanism to documents or other information objects from any 
objects (including other information objects). Any number of information objects can be "about" any number of 
objects. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1175
Implementation Notes / Issues / Suggestions 
The main issue with this (set) of AT’s is the mapping choice, not just in which specialization to select, but in being 
clear as the most appropriate asset to which to link. Example is a "test procedure" about a specific tagged or 
numbered pump or about a whole class or set of pumps or is a commissioning instruction about a specific item 
or about all components of a system. 
The mapping guidelines will suggest including the "reference" AT for all explicit objects, and a definition or 
description AT for the true subject. Need to beware for example, where a specification "for" an individual pump 
(say) includes a reference to a (say) system or process unit, etc. 
Qualitative Characteristic 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1176
Business Usage Semantics (incl Cardinality) 
This AT is used to assign qualitative information which characterizes (some aspect of) an object. This is 
distinguished from PRU by the fact that the characteristic is not a physically dimensioned property (though it 
may infer one), and its values must be selected from a domain of valid values, rather than any real number value. 
Any registered object may possess any number of characteristics. 
The specialization FEA is used to characterize a shape or form feature such as having "a bevelled end" or having 
"a bolted bonnet." The feature is a design form, not merely a distinct component. 
The specialization MOC is used to characterize the material of construction of an object such as "being made of 
Stainless Steel," or "being made of ASTM A234 WPB". 
Implementation Notes / Issues / Suggestions 
It is permitted to use specializations of this AT for specific characteristic types (and / or units) if this provides any 
implementation benefits. (Ex: Possession of Size according to ANSI mm Nominal Size, say) 
Two particular specializations FEA and MOC are specified. 
Attribute 
Business Usage Semantics (incl Cardinality) 
This AT is used to assign an arbitrary information attribute which characterizes (some aspect of) an object. This is 
distinguished from QAL by the fact that the attribute value is not limited to any domain by the class of attribute. 
This is a fallback AT to be used only where other AT’s are inapplicable or insufficient knowledge exists to select a 
more specific AT. 
Any registered object may possess any number of attributes. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1177
Implementation Notes / Issues / Suggestions 
None. 
Direct/Indirect Connection 
Business Usage Semantics (incl Cardinality) 
These AT’s are used to indicate that objects are connected to each other. 
DCO is used when the two objects are contiguous, physically touching (barring any materials actually used to 
make the connection into a joint). 
ICO is used to indicate that two objects are connected indirectly in some systematic or logical way only. 
Implementation Notes / Issues / Suggestions 
None. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1178
Involvement in Activity 
Business Usage Semantics (incl Cardinality) 
This AT is used to indicate that an object is involved in an activity (and its role in that activity). 
Implementation Notes / Issues / Suggestions 
None. 
Generic Association/Relationship 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1179
Business Usage Semantics (incl Cardinality) 
Used to capture the existence of a non-specific relationship between two objects, only where no applicable more 
specific atomic (binary) template exists in the template library at the time of instantiation, or knowledge about 
the association is insufficiently complete at that point in time. 
As such, this Template offers no other semantic constraints on object types, string contents or cardinalities. 
Implementation Notes / Issues / Suggestions 
It is conceivable that the business may wish to add knowledge to a generic association, by further classification 
of the association instance, until such time as more explicit alternative AT is implemented. 
Fulfillment 
Business Usage Semantics (incl Cardinality) 
This AT is used to indicate that a materialized physical object fulfills the "role" of a logical or functional physical 
object. 
Main examples are: 
The relationship between a "tagged" process item location (ex as defined on a P+ID) and an individual physical 
plant item of a specific serial number and model number installed at that location. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1180
The relationship between a numbered and versioned document or other logical information content object, and 
a rendition of that document in some physical format or representation. 
Implementation Notes / Issues / Suggestions 
None.

Define Units of Measure 
<SystemOfUOMClass> 
<ClassID>SI</ClassID> 
<ClassName>SI</ClassName> 
<Definition>SI</Definition> 
<UOMClass> 
<ClassID>Metre</ClassID> 
<ClassName>Metre</ClassName> 
<Definition>Metre</Definition> 
<Abbrev>m</Abbrev> 
</UOMClass> 
<UOMClass> 
<ClassID>Centimetre</ClassID> 
<ClassName>Centimetre</ClassName> 
<Definition>Centimetre</Definition> 
<Abbrev>cm</Abbrev> 
</UOMClass> 
</SystemOfUOMClass> 
This example defines the SI system of units of measure (UOM), and also defines the two UOM classes – one for 
Metre (m) and another for Centimetre (cm). 
Measure classes are defined as given in this snippet: 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1183
<MeasureClass> 
<ClassID>Length Measure</ClassID> 
<ClassName>Length Measure</ClassName> 
<Definition>Length Measure</Definition> 
<UnitOfMeasure> 
<UOMClassID>Metre</UOMClassID> 
</UnitOfMeasure> 
<UnitOfMeasure> 
<UOMClassID>Centimetre</UOMClassID> 
<Scale>0.01</Scale> 
</UnitOfMeasure> 
</MeasureClass> 
Here, a Length Measure is defined with its base UOM as Metre and another UOM of Centimetre. 
Note: The Scale factor converts Centimetre to the base unit Metre. 
Following is the definition of a PropertyClass that makes use of a UOM: 
<PropertyClass> 
<ClassID>LENGTH</ClassID> 
<ParentClassID>PHYSICAL PROPERTY</ParentClassID> 
<ClassName>Length</ClassName> 
<Definition>Length</Definition> 
<MeasureClassID>Length Measure</MeasureClassID> 
</PropertyClass> 
To import an Object that uses this Property 
<Object> 
<ID>TestObject02</ID> 
<Context> 
<ID>IPE</ID> 
</Context> 
<Property> 
<Name>LENGTH</Name> 
<Value>10</Value> 
<Units>cm</Units> 
</Property> 
</Object> 
A Property can be provided with an empty <Units/> element (or <Units></Units>). If Units are not provided, 
then the Property will not gain the Base Unit, but will be set to Null. If a Characteristic is supplied for an attribute 
that is actually a Property, then it will gain the Base Unit for the Property. EIA SearchResults will return an empty 
<Units/> element for Properties that have null Units to match the EIWM schema. 
The EIWM allows for the creation of Rights and the assignment of those Rights to appropriate Roles and Persons. 
The following is an EIWM example of creating a Right: 
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?> 
<?vnet xslt="NULL" ?> 
<vl:VNETList xmlns:vl="http://www.aveva.com/VNET/List" xmlns="http://www.aveva.com/VNET/
eiwm"> 
<Right> 
<Name>NewRight</Name> 
</Right> 
</vl:VNETList> 
The following is an EIWM example of assigning a Right to a Person and a Role: 
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?> 
<?vnet xslt="NULL" ?> 
<vl:VNETList xmlns:vl="http://www.aveva.com/VNET/List" xmlns="http://www.aveva.com/VNET/
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1184
eiwm"> 
<Template> 
<ID>BootStrapSampleUsers.xml::template</ID> 
<Object> 
<ID>AVEVA\Person1</ID> 
<ClassID>PERSON</ClassID> 
<Association type="has right"> 
<Right> 
<Name>NewRight</Name> 
</Right> 
</Association> 
</Object> 
<Object> 
<ID>Default User</ID> 
<ClassID>ROLE</ClassID> 
<Association type="has right"> 
<Right> 
<Name>NewRight</Name> 
</Right> 
</Association> 
</Object> 
</Template> 
</vl:VNETList> 
Rights can be assigned to either Roles or Persons with the following conditions: 
• Right should be existing before assigning it to a Role or a Person. If it is not existing, then Import Controller 
displays the message: Right not assigned - no such Right '<name of the right>' 
• Associating any object other than Role or Person to the Right is not permissible. If the Administrator tries to 
assign any other object apart from Role or Person to a new or existing Right, the following message is shown 
to the user while importing the XML: Cannot assign '<name of the right>' right to '<name of the 
object>' object. Rights can be assigned only to persons and roles. 
• Rights cannot be deleted if there are some references still existing. Only the unreferenced rights are deleted 
and then the Import Controller displays messages like this for each existing referenced rights: Right '<name 
of the right>' not deleted as it is still referenced. 
Use the Import Controller from the Command Line 
The AIM Import Controller is used to configure the Import Server and to load data and documents into AIM. The 
Import Controller Console is a command-line version of the Import Controller. You can control the Import Server 
using scripts. 
Import Controller Console Commands 
The following sections provide descriptions for the Import Controller Console commands and the corresponding 
acceptable parameters. 
To get help on the available parameters for a command, type [commandName] /Help. For example, encrypt 
password /Help. 
Encrypt Password 
This command encrypts the given password.

Parameter 
Parameter Type 
encrypt password 
-Password:PasswordString 
Password 
Required

Use Microsoft Entra ID 
AIM can be configured to use Microsoft Entra ID for access to the Dashboard website. 
Microsoft Entra ID can also be used to define the groups used in the Access Control Lists (for limiting access to 
Portal IDs) as defined in the web.config file. 
Note: The following integrations are not supported: 
• Integration with AVEVA AIM-hybrid 
• Integration with AVEVA Point Cloud Manager Viewer 
• Integration with AVEVA Engage/Design in Context/DA Connector 
Configure AIM to use Microsoft Entra ID for Accessing the Dashboard 
To configure AIM to user Microsoft Entra ID for access to Dashboard website: 
1. Log in to the Azure portal, for example https://portal.azure.com/#home. 
2. Select Microsoft Entra ID from the side menu bar. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1193
3. Select App registrations from the side menu bar to create an app registration. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1194
4. Select New registration to create a new registration. 
5. Provide a Name, Supported account types and Redirect URI. For example: 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1195
6. When the registration is created, in the Overview section you will see details similar to those below. Make a 
note of the Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID which will be used for the AIM setup. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1196
7. Navigate to Expose an API and set the Application ID URI, scope and client application. 
8. Set the Application ID URI by selecting Set. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1197
9. By default, api://{clientId} will be present, so just add /AIM after {clientId}. Then select Save. For example: 
10. Select Add a scope. The Add a scope window opens. 
11. Enter the details as shown in the following example. 
12. Select Add scope at the bottom of the window. 
13. Navigate to Overview and copy the Application (client) ID. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1198
14. Navigate to Expose an API and select Add a client application. 
15. Paste the value of the copied Application (client) ID into the Client ID field. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1199
16. Select the checkbox under the Authorized scopes section. 
17. Select Add application. 
18. Navigate to Manifest and update the accessTokenAcceptedVersion value to 2 to get the 
preferred_username claim. 
19. Update the groupMembershipClaims value to SecurityGroup or All to get the groups claim. 
For groupMembershipClaims, a value of null excludes all groups, a value of SecurityGroup includes only 
Active Directory Security Group memberships, and a value of All includes both Security Groups and 
Microsoft 365 Distribution Lists. 
20. Select Save. 
Note: Ensure that the user is not in more than 200 groups (for JWT tokens). For more information, see 
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/security-tokens. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1200
21. Configure AIM’s web.config to use Microsoft Entra ID Authentication (Identity Provider). 
22. Add or modify the following settings in the Dashboard’s web.config file. 
23. All the settings must be prefixed with avevanet:. For example, <add key="avevanet:*" value="*" /> 
Parameter 
Description 
authenticationProvider 
Set as Microsoft Entra ID (case-sensitive). 
applicationRootUri 
The root URI of AIM’s Dashboard application. 
For example: https://sample.example.com/
Dashboard 
authorityUri 
The URI of the Microsoft Entra ID authority. 
For example: https://login.microsoftonline.com/ 
{tenantId}/v2.0 
dashboardClientId 
Application (client) ID 
resourceScope 
The resource scope to be passed. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1201
Parameter 
Description 
For example: api:// 
{clientId}/AIM/AVEVA.NET.Workhub 
For example: 
<add key="avevanet:authenticationProvider" value="AzureAD" /> 
<add key="avevanet:applicationRootUri" value=" 
https://sample.example.com/Dashboard " /> 
<add key="avevanet:authorityUri" value=" 
https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenantId}/v2.0" /> 
<add key="avevanet:dashboardClientId" value="{clientId}" /> 
<add key="avevanet:resourceScope" value=" api:// 
{clientId}/AIM/AVEVA.NET.Workhub " /> 
24. To allow communication with Microsoft Entra ID, in the web.config file under <content-Security-Policy 
enabled="true"> and then under <connect-src self="true"> add: <add 
source="https://login.microsoftonline.com/" />. 
Configure AIM to Use Microsoft Entra ID Groups for Restricting User Access based on Portal IDs 
If there is a requirement to use multiple Portal IDs and you want to restrict a Portal ID’s access based on user 
groups, then it can be achieved by creating Microsoft Entra ID groups. 
1. Ensure you have followed the steps in the previous section in the Configure AIM to use Microsoft Entra ID for 
access to the Dashboard topic. 
2. To create an Microsoft Entra ID group, navigate to the tenant page and select Groups. 
3. Select New group. 
The New Group screen opens. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1202
4. Enter the Group Type as Security or Microsoft 365. Add the Owners and Members based on requirements. 
Click Create. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1203
5. The Group will be shown under All groups. Take a note of the Object Id for each required group so that they 
can be configured in the Portal ID’s AccessControlList (in the web.config) to Allow or Deny Groups. 
6. Apply the group for a Portal ID, for example: 
<portal id="portal1"> 
<ConnectionString …. /> 
<AccessControlList> 
<Allow Everyone="False" nodetype="leaf"> 
<Users> <User Value="abc@xyz.com" /> </Users> 
<Groups><Group Value="17462d33-d117-459f-9fbc-88316ee41234" /></Groups> 
</Allow> 
<Deny nodetype="leaf"> 
<Users> </Users> 
<Groups></Groups> 
</Deny> 
</AccessControlList> 
<ContentManagementInteroperabilityServices>…</ContentManagementInteroperabilityServic
es> 
</portal> 
Configure AVEVA AIM Authentication 
Besides the Windows authentication, AIM supports an additional authentication mode that can be used to 
connect the AIM Dashboard with authentication providers not dependent on a domain. The authentication 
mode is: 
• Web Services Federation (WS-Federation), which allows you to use Active Directory Federation Services 
(ADFS) 
To configure this authentication, you must deploy the AIM Dashboard without using the deployment tool. 
To deploy the AIM Dashboard manually: 
1. Open IIS. 
2. Right-click the application on which you wish to deploy the AIM Dashboard. 
3. Select Import. 
You can then enter the authentication details as explained below as part of the web deploy process. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1204
WS-Federation Authentication 
The WS-Federation option allows the AIM Dashboard to be authenticated by any Identity Provider that supports 
the WS-Federation specification. For example, ADFS. This option does not support authentication for the 
following: 
• Identity Delegation to the Workhub API or the AIM Dashboard Viewer 
• Digital Asset Connector (DA Connector) 
Configuring AVEVA Asset Information Management to Use WS Federation Authentication 
To configure AIM to use a WS-Federation enabled Identity Provider, you must disable the Windows 
authentication option and ensure that the AIM runs under the HTTPS protocol. 
All the settings are prefixed with avevanet: and are added or modified in the appSettings section of the AIM 
Dashboard web.config file, that is: 
<add key="avevanet:*" value="*" /> 
Note: It is recommended that you configure all the settings via the Web Deploy Package for AIM Dashboard. 
Parameter 
Description 
authenticationProvider 
Set as WS-Federation 
applicationRootUrl 
The root URL of the application. For example: 
https://mydashboardapps.contoso.net/HelloWorld 
authorityUrl 
The URL of the WS-Federation authority. For example, 
if you have an ADFS instance and its URL is 
https://myadfs.contoso.net/, that URL is your 
authorityUrl. 
dashboardClientId 
An alternative ID to use for the AIM Dashboard client 
application when talking to the Identity Provider. The 
default ID is AVEVA.NET.Dashboard. 
Configuring ADFS 
If you are using ADFS as the WS-Federation identity provider, you must configure AIM as a relying party. 
For that, you must add a Relying Party Trust with the following settings: 
1. Set the Relying Party Identifier for AIM Dashboard as the applicationRootUrl plus /identity. For 
example, if the URL to your AIM Dashboard site is https://mydashboardapps.contoso.net/HelloWorld, then 
while adding the relying party identifier, the URL should be https://mydashboardapps.contoso.net/
HelloWorld/identity. 
2. Set the Relying Party Identifier and add a WS-Federation Passive Endpoint for the application URL plus the 
identity path. For the example above, the site https://mydashboardapps.contoso.net/HelloWorld has Relying 
Party Identifier and WS-Federation passive endpoint as https://mydashboardapps.contoso.net/HelloWorld/
identity. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1205
3. Add the following claims for the AIM Relying Party: 
Name – Used as the name displayed under profile information in the AIM UI. 
Name ID – A unique name identifier that is used internally. 
Both the claims can have the same value. 
Note: If you have an existing AIM installation, the user name used should be the Windows account name 
(that is, CONTOSO\username). The same user is used within the application while switching to the WS-
Federation authentication option. The Name and Name ID claims must be set as Windows account name. 
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
AIM

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 1 - Preface

1

Preface

This chapter gives basic information about this guide, training and support.

1.1

About this Guide

This guide explains how to install and use AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS (after this,

referred to as the Gateway). It includes the following key information:

· Installing the Gateway.
· Running the Gateway from the command line.
· Working with the Gateway to create and load projects using XML files.
· Executing the Gateway using the project settings.
· Defining various types of project settings, output settings and mappings.

1.2

Audience

This guide is intended for anyone who is responsible for extracting data from Document

Management Systems (DMS), such as Documentum, and loading the data into AVEVA NET

Workhub and Dashboard.

This guide assumes that you have the knowledge of the following:

· SQL expression construction.
· XML syntax.
· Configuration of the DMS server in use with concepts of CMIS.

1.3

Related Documents

This section lists the guides which provide you a good starting point to understand the

AVEVA NET Gateways products suite. For more information about AVEVA NET Gateways

products, see the following guides:

· AVEVA NET Gateway for Configuration Tool User Guide
· AVEVA NET Document Indexing Gateway

software improvement suggestion as soon as possible, and notify you about the status of

the incident.

If you are an accredited user of the AVEVA products, you are entitled to use the AVEVA

Helpdesk, which you should have valid login credentials. For any other queries, contact

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

2

Product Overview

AVEVA NET Gateways process and convert data from multiple data sources into a

compatible file format that is loaded into AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard. The data

sources can be Microsoft Access, Microsoft Excel, Oracle, Documentum, Primavera Server

and many more. The output that the Gateways generate is XML format. These XML files,

also known as Engineering Information and Workflow Model (EIWM) files, enable the

import of source data into AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard as a set of objects using

Import Controller. The various datatypes are as follows:

· Structured Data: Proprietary business data formats, including SAP, Primavera and

Microsoft Project

· Unstructured Data: Document formats, such as Word, Excel, PDF and
PowerPoint; Documents held within Documentum or SharePoint

· 2D Data:  2D CAD data formats, including AutoCAD, MicroStation, SmartPlant

P&ID, INTools and AVEVA P&ID

· 3D Data: 3D model formats, such as SmartPlant 3D, PDS 3D, AVEVA PDMS,

AutoPlant 3D, AVEVA Marine and so on

Gateways perform the following major functions:

· Process new information from data sources and add it to the common

information model, which allows AVEVA NET to store a unified and cross-

referenced model of all tags across projects or asset.

· Scan information sources for tagged items, extract them, and automatically build

relationships between them.

· Render accurately the source information and apply hotspots to tagged items,

which allow you to easily navigate related information.

Depending on the processing of the data, Gateways can be segregated into following

types:

· CAD File Processing Gateways: AVEVA NET Gateway for AutoCAD 2D, AVEVA NET

Gateway for AutoCAD 3D and so on

· Document Indexing Gateways: AVEVA NET Document Indexing Gateway
· DMS Gateways: AVEVA NET Documentum Gateway, AVEVA NET Gateway for

CMIS

· Data Processing Gateways: AVEVA NET Gateway for SAP, AVEVA NET Data

Processor Gateway and so on

Release 5.0.10

Page 11

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

2.1

What is AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS?

Content Management Interoperability Services (CMIS) is an open standard that allows

different content management systems to share information over the network.

Specifically, CMIS defines an abstraction layer to control various document management

systems and repositories using web protocols such as SOAP, REST and ATOMPub.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS is a type of DMS Gateway, which performs the following
major functions:

· Processes the document metadata stored in different DMS, such as Documentum
and SharePoint, using CMIS standards and protocols and generates intelligent

EIWM XML files for import into AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard using the

AVEVA NET Import Controller.

· Provides options to download documents and Rendition files from the DMS that

can be further processed by other gateways such as AVEVA NET Document

Indexing Gateway and AVEVA NET Gateway Configuration Tool to extract more

information in the form of EIWM XML files.

The following figure shows a high level flow of the Gateway:

High Level Flow Diagram - AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS

AVEVA Licensing

You must install ALS 4.1 and appropriate license file before you install the Gateway.

The ALS manages the license for the Gateway:

· You must get the server name of ALS Console Server on your network, for

example, 1234@Serv1.

· If you do not have an ALS Console Server, you must install ALS 4.1, which is

available on the customer website under AVEVA Licensing.
· Get the necessary ALS licenses for the installation requirements.
· Set the environment variable AVEVA_LICENSE_SERVER_LIST to point to the

name and locations of the ALS license file.

Note: For more information about ALS 4.1, see http://support.aveva.com.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 3 - Getting Started

3.3

Installing the Gateway

To install the Gateway:

1.

From your installation media, double-click the CMISGatewayInstaller.MSI file.

2. Click Next.

3.

Select I accept, and then click Next.

Note: To print a copy of the License Agreement, click Print.

4.

If you want to modify the default installation Location, click Browse.

Note: To find out the available and required disk space for your chosen features

and available drives, click Disk Usage.

5.

Select Everyone or Just me depending on who you want to be able to access

the application.

6. Click Next.

7. Click Install. The installation process starts.

8. Click Finish after the Setup Wizard completes installing the Gateway.

Release 5.0.10

Page 15

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 3 - Getting Started

3.4

Uninstalling the Gateway
We recommend you to remove any previously installed versions of the Gateway from
your system before installing a new version.

You can select any of the following ways to uninstall the Gateway:

· Using the Control Panel
· Using the Gateway Installer

To remove the Gateway using the Control Panel:

1. Go to Start, Control Panel, and then click Programs and Features.

2.

3.

Select AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS from the list of programs.

Select Uninstall to remove AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS.

To remove the Gateway using the Gateway Installer:

1.

From your installation media, double-click the CMISGatewayInstaller.MSI file.

2. Click Next.

3. Click Remove.

4. To confirm that you want to remove the Gateway, click Remove again.

5.

Follow the on-screen instructions.

3.5

Changing and Repairing the Gateway

You can change or repair the Gateway using the Gateway Installer.

To change or repair the gateway using the Gateway Installer:

1.

From your installation media, double-click the CMISGatewayInstaller.MSI file.

2. Click Next.

3.

Select one of the following:

a. Click Change if you want to modify any optional features installed with the

application.

b. Click Repair if you want to repair the errors in the most recent installation,

repair any corrupt files, or fix any missing shortcuts or registry entries.

4.

Follow the on-screen instructions.

Release 5.0.10

Page 16

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 4 - Running the Gateway from the Command Line

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

4

Running the Gateway from the Command Line

You can run the Gateway from the command line.

Note: When you run the gateway from command prompt, you do not see any inline

output. These inline output is directed to the log file. This allows you to use the command

prompt without waiting for the Gateway to complete the execution.

All the logs before project execution (such as command line parameters validation logs or

project configuration file load logs) are logged into the default log location:

 C:\ProgramData\Logs\CMISGatewayApp_Log_<GUID>.txt.

The logs during project execution are logged to the log folder as provided by you either

from command prompt or configuration file.

At the command line, type the following:
AVEVA.NET.Gateways.CMIS.App.exe -cp <project configuration file path> -rp
<repository name> -un <user name> -pw <encrypted password> -ad
<endpoint address> -sd <start date> -ed <end date> –op <output folder
path> -lp <log folder path> -ol <object list path>
where the parameters are listed as follows:

Parameter

Description

-cp <project configuration file
path>

(mandatory) Path to the project configuration file,
for example, “C:\Doc Gateway Training\
Example Config.xml".

-rp <repository name>

(optional) The name of the CMIS repository.

-un <user name>

-pw <encrypted password>

-ad <endpoint address>

-sd <start date>

(optional) DMS credentials; CMIS user name -
usually same as the Windows Server Login Name,
for example, Administrator.

(optional) DMS credentials; Encrypted CMIS
password - usually the same as the Windows server
password.  Passwords can be encrypted using the
command line option.

(optional) Endpoint address; URL of the CMIS
Service which is connected to the Gateway.
Note: When connecting to a Documentum server
through the Restful AtomPub binding, EMC CMIS
implementation  uses HTTP basic authentication.
As credentials passed in clear text in HTTP can be
captured and misused, we recommend using HTTPS
sessions to provide encrypted communication.

(optional) Extract only items created or modified
after this time. This parameter contains the format
"yyyy-MM-dd hh:mm:ss", for example,  "2014-
01-31T23:59:59".

Release 5.0.10

Page 17

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 4 - Running the Gateway from the Command Line

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

Parameter

-ed <end date>

-op <output folder path>

Description

(optional) Extract only items created or modified
before this time. This parameter contains the
format "yyyy-MM-dd hh:mm:ss", for example,
"2015-01-31T23:59:59".

(optional) To download files to a specific location
on the client machine.

-lp <log folder path>

(optional) Log folder path.

-ol <object list path>

(optional) XML file with list of object Ids.

-ct <Criteria>

(optional) This is used in place of any configuration
that may be in the project configuration file.

Note: If an optional parameter is not provided, the setting specified in the project

configuration file is used.

To Generate an Encrypted Password:

You can generate an encrypted password using the following command at the command

prompt:

Syntax: CMIS Gateway .exe file name  -x  and then space "password"
Example: AVEVA.NET.Gateways.CMIS.App.exe  -x "password"

Object List XML File Format:

If you want to download a set of documents from a DMS using its Object IDs, you must

create a new XML file and provide the list of Object IDs in the format mentioned below:

<ObjectIds>

 <ObjectId>09000065800011bf</ObjectId>
 <ObjectId>09000065800019b7</ObjectId>

</ObjectIds>

You must then pass the complete file path using the following command:

 -ol <object list path>

If the command line option contains the object list path, it overrides the query present in

the project configuration file and fetch and process only the objects detailed in the above

XML file.

Notes:

· If a Start date is not specified, a date and time of 01/01/0001 00:00 is used (that

is, all documents are included).

· If an End date is not specified, the current date and time is used (that is, all

documents are included).

· If the optional arguments are not mentioned as part of command line arguments,

the values in the configuration file are used by default.

Release 5.0.10

Page 18

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 4 - Running the Gateway from the Command Line

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

Command Line Parameters for WHERE clause:

The command line parameter -ct allows you to set the content of a WHERE clause.

 -ct "<Criteria>"

Example:

AVEVA.NET.Gateways.CMIS.App.exe -cp <project configuration file path> -ct
"In_Tree('<cmis:objectId>')"

which results in the following query: select * from cmis:document WHERE
In_Tree('<cmis:objectId>')

Exit Codes

The following command line exit codes are defined for

AVEVA.NET.Gateways.CMIS.App.exe.

Exit Code

0

1001

1002

1003

1004

Definition

Success

License checkout failed due to expiry of license

License checkout failed due to not enough seats

License for the product is missing or not requested

Invalid license server details/unavailable

Note: The exit code returned from the gateway can be captured in the %errorlevel%

variable when executed in batch mode.

Release 5.0.10

Page 19

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Running the Gateway through the Invoker

5

Running the Gateway through the Invoker

The Gateway invoker enables you to extract data in an incremental mode. This is enabled

by storing the last successful scan date and time for a given project configuration in the

text file <projectFileName>_LastScan.txt, in the same location as project

configuration file. When the invoker is used for initial data extraction, all data matching

the query is extracted from the CMIS server and it stores the current date and time in the

LastScan text file. During subsequent data extractions for that project, the stored last

scan date-time is used as a command line input for the Gateway and it gets updated to

the date-time of the most recent scan. You may delete or modify the text file to clear or

update the last scan date-time.

Parameter

Description

-cp <project configuration file
path>

(mandatory) Path to the project configuration file,
for example, “C:\Doc Gateway
Training\Example Config.xml".

Example: AVEVA.NET.Gateways.CMIS.Invoker.exe -cp <project configuration

filepath>

Using the Gateway invoker, you can perform two types of operations in the Gateway:

· Initial load: When you run the Gateway for the first time to extract all the data

you intend to and load them into AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard.

· Incremental load: After each data extraction, you can extract the items that have
been created/modified since the last scan date and time and load them into

AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard to ensure that it has the latest data.

Notes:

· The Last scan date-time is automatically added by the Gateway reading the

Invoker LastScan.txt file.

· When using the invoker with a Gateway, which is connecting to a SharePoint

server, the invoker should not be run more than once a day. This is because the

SharePoint server only passes the Date component and not the Time component

for any changes made to its documents.

Release 5.0.10

Page 20

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

6

Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

The Project Explorer menu enables you to create projects and browse existing projects on

your computer. You can also load an existing project configuration, update a project

configuration using the project settings menu, and execute projects using the Execute

menu.

To start working with Project Explorer menu of the Gateway:

1. Navigate to Start > AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS to start the application.

2. AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS page opens with the Project Explorer and Help

menus in the main window.

Release 5.0.10

Page 21

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

6.1

Creating Projects

To create a project in the Project Explorer page of the Gateway:

1. Click Create Project.

2. Type the project name, and project description (optional) in the respective

Project Name and Project Description boxes.

3. Type the project path in the Project Path box, or you can browse to the folder

using the Browse button, to create the project at the desired location.

4. Click Create and Load.

Note: If you type the required content in Project Name and Project Path boxes,

the Create and Load option gets enabled.

A new project folder is created containing the Project.xml file with the same

Project Name. If the folder does not exist, a new project folder is created,

containing the Project.xml file with the Project Name. When the Project is

created successfully, a message is displayed in the status bar of the window.

6.2

Loading Projects

A Load Project option can be used to load an existing project to fetch data from a DMS.

When a Project is loaded successfully, two other additional menus, Project Settings and

Execute, appear in the menu bar. These allow you to update the project settings and

execute the project respectively.

To load a project in the Project Explorer page:

1. Under Details, type the project details in the Browse Project box. Alternatively,

you can also click Browse to locate the required project folder.

You can verify the details of the currently selected project, as shown under the

Browse Project box. This includes Project Name, Description and path of the

project.

2. Click Load Project to load the project.

If the project is created successfully, a message is displayed in the status bar of

the window. If it fails to create project, an error message is shown in the user

interface.

Release 5.0.10

Page 22

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

6.3

Defining Project Settings

The Project Settings menu is enabled when a project is loaded successfully in the

Gateway. This menu contains the following options:

· Connection Settings: Defines all mandatory connection settings such as URL of

DMS Server, User Name, Password and so on.

· Output Settings: Allow you to define the output file path and log file path along

with mapping information.

· Query Settings: Enable you to define the CMIS Document Type, selection criteria,

and revision settings.

6.3.1

Connection Settings

Connection Settings page enables you to enter the minimum settings required to connect

to the SharePoint, OpenText or Documentum server.

Settings

Descriptions

CMIS Service
URL

URL of the DMS service, is also known as Endpoint Address.
· For SharePoint 2010, the endpoint is: http://

[ServerName]/sites/[SiteName]/_vti_bin/cmis/rest/
[DocBaseID]?getRepositoryInfo

· To find the DocBaseID, click Site Settings, Site Libraries and
Lists, and then right-click Customize [LibraryName]. The

DocBaseID is in the resulting link.

· For SharePoint 2013, the endpoint is: http://[ServerName]:

[PortNo]/[SiteName]/_vti_bin/cmis/rest?getRepositories

· For Documentum, the endpoint is of the the format: http://[host]:

[port]/emc-emis/resources

· For OpenText Content Server, the endpoint is of the format:

http:// [host]:[port]/ot-elib/cmis/atom

Note: When connecting to a Documentum server through the Restful

AtomPub binding, EMC CMIS implementation  uses HTTP basic

authentication. As credentials passed in clear text in HTTP can be

captured and misused, we recommend using HTTPS sessions to

provide encrypted communication.

User Name

Connects to the corresponding DMS server. Type the domain name as
part of User Name if the DMS server is in some other domain.

Password

Password for the User Name.

Release 5.0.10

Page 23

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

Settings

Descriptions

Repository

DMS Repository ID.

Connect
Timeout

60000 ms.

Read Timeout

1200000 ms.

Retry Interval (in
seconds)

Allows the Gateway to wait for a specified duration and retry in case of
a failure. Default value is 120 seconds.

Maximum Retry
Count

Allows the Gateway to retry execution for a specified number of times
before terminating the execution. Default value is 0 (immediate
termination).

Notes:

· For SharePoint 2010, you can get the DocBaseID by navigating to Site Settings,
Site Libraries, Lists and right-clicking the link Customize "[LibraryName]".

· To prevent the failure of the Gateway when the DMS server is not available, an

option allows it to wait a specified duration and retry. If the DMS server is still not

available, execution is stopped with appropriate error log. This option works only

during command line execution. The following example shows the default

setting:

<RetryIntervalSec>120</RetryIntervalSec>

· If the Gateway processes a number of documents and is interrupted (for example,
a network error to the server), the Gateway can continue the processing of the

remaining documents. Use MaxRetryCount to set a value that allows the

Gateway to retry execution for a specified number of times before terminating

the process. When the Gateway retries, it resumes execution from the point it

was interrupted by the last exception, not from the beginning. For example, this

option allows the Gateway to resume execution up to 3 times:

<ConnectionSettings>

<MaxRetryCount>3</MaxRetryCount>

</ConnectionSettings>
Notes:

· The default value is set to 0 indicating that it terminates the execution on

the first exception.

· The RetryIntervalSec and MaxRetryCount options should never be set

as negative.

Release 5.0.10

Page 24

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

6.3.2

Output Settings

Output settings in the Gateway allow you to define the output file path, log file path and

mappings. When you click Output Settings, the Output Settings page appears, as shown

in the following figure:

The Output Settings page contains the following groups:
· General containing the following options:

· Output Path: Type the output file path in this box or click the Browse button

at the end of this box to specify the output file path.

· Log Path: Type the log file path in this box or click the Browse button at the

end of this box to specify the log file path.

Release 5.0.10

Page 25

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

· Continue Execution on Error: If your query identifies a number of document
objects and you select this option, any failed objects are skipped during

execution but the Gateway continues to process the other objects. On

completion, the status of the execution is returned as Success. If you run

the gateway from the command line, it returns an exit code 0.

· FailGatewayIfErrorFound (Optional - configurable only through

project configuration file): If you select the Continue Execution on

Error option, you can set the value of the optional attribute

FailGatewayIfErrorFound as true to return the status of the

execution as Failed with exit code 1000.

The following XML snippet shows how to configure it in the

configuration file:

<ContinueOnError

FailGatewayIfErrorFound="true">true</ContinueOnError>

This option can be helpful in the following scenario:

When running the incremental scan through the invoker, if the Continue

Execution on Error option is selected, last scan date-time gets updated after

the completion of execution. This gets updated even though some of the

objects in the list are not extracted. You can avoid updating the last scan

date-time when an object fails by setting FailGatewayIfErrorFound to

true.

· Log Level: (Optional) When you specify a Log Level, the relevant events are
added to the log file. For example, if you set the Log Level to Information, all

errors, warnings, and informational messages are logged.

Note: The Log Level option is configurable both in the user interface and in

the configuration file.

The following table shows the severity level for different Log Levels:

Log Level

Severity

Error

Level 1: Indicates logs for an error message.

Warning

Level 2: Indicates logs for an error/warning message.

Information

Level 3: Indicates logs for an error/warning/informational
message.

Verbose

Level 4: Indicates logs at all levels.

Note: Performance of the Gateway is impacted if you set the Log Level to

Verbose option as it logs at all levels. This can produce very large files that

contain detailed information useful for debugging, such as mapping

information and input/output information of the CMIS function calls.

Release 5.0.10

Page 26

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

For LogFileRollSizeKB - To avoid issues when the log file is very large (for

example, when you set the Log Level to Verbose), you can specify that the

log file be split into a number of smaller files. For example, if this is set to 10

then when the log file reaches 10 MB (10240 KB), the file (for example,

mylog_2016_02_11_10_52_26.1.txt) is closed and further events are

rolled over to a new log file (for example,

mylog_2016_02_11_10_52_26.2.txt).

Note: This LogFileRollSizeKB option is configurable only in a project

configuration file.

The following XML snippet shows how to add log level and log file roll size in

a project configuration file:

<OutputSettings>

   .....
   ......
   <LogLevel>Information</LogLevel>
   <LogFileRollSizeKB>100</LogFileRollSizeKB>
   ......
   ......

</OutputSettings>

ALSConnectionRetryTime: This optional parameter in configuration settings

specifies the timeout period for the Gateway to wait for a response from the

AVEVA License Server (ALS). When the Gateway requests a license from the

ALS and does not receive a response, for example, if the ALS server or

network is busy, the Gateway process waits for the timeout period and then

sends another license request to the ALS.

If a license is acquired, the Gateway processing will start. Otherwise, the

Gateway stops processing and reports the appropriate license error. The

timeout period can be configured to have values between 0 and 4294967295

seconds.

Notes:

· You should expect a delay of 10-40 seconds for a normal ALS response.
· The Gateway does not wait for the timeout period to expire if the

configured ALS connection details are invalid or the ALS responds with a

license checkout error.

Release 5.0.10

Page 27

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

· Mappings: You can use Mapping to manipulate the output files before they are

produced by the Gateway. Mapping is a transformation process used to generate

EIWM files from a set of extracted records. For more information about mapping,

see Appendix A: Mapping Configuration.

The Gateway includes following mapping types:

· Template ID Mapping: This mapping assigns an ID to the Template in the EIWM

file (for more information, see Template Mapping).

· Class Mapping: This mapping classifies the objects in the EIWM file (for more

information, see Class Mapping).

· Object ID Mapping: This mapping assigns IDs to objects in the EIWM file (for

more information, see Object ID Mapping).

· Context Mapping: This mapping assigns contexts to objects in the EIWM file (for

more information, see Context Mapping).

Release 5.0.10

Page 28

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

· Attribute Mapping: This mapping determines which attributes from the DMS are

included in the output, as Characteristics or Properties, and permits

transformation of names, values, and units (for more information, see Attribute

Mapping).

· Association Mapping: This mapping creates associations in the EIWM file (for

more information, see Association Mapping).

· Rendition Mapping: This mapping fetches a particular rendition or a group of
rendition files from the DMS (for more information, see Rendition Mapping).

Notes:

· For each specific mapping, you can type the path of the mapping file or click
File Selector at the end of the box. To modify the respective mapping files,

click Modify Mapping. To save the paths to the mapping files, the output

path and the log path, click Save Settings.

· Mapping is applied to objects in the order listed above. For more
information on mapping configuration, see Appendix A: Mapping

Configuration.

The Gateway supports the relative path for Log files, Output files and all mappings.

If you want to add relative path in UI or project.xml location, you must type the relative

path for log and output files such as \Logs and \Output. These are then interpreted as

the equivalent absolute paths of D:\Demo\Demo_CMIS\Logs and D:

\Demo\Demo_CMIS\Output. The use of relative paths makes it easier to copy

configurations from one machine/file location to another.

Relative path must start from project.xml location as given below. For example, if project

CMIS_Demo.xml is at location D:\Demo\Demo_CMIS\CMIS_Demo.xml, then D:

\Demo\Demo_CMIS is the project directory.

For mappings, you must start relative path from project directory, for example:

<TemplateMapping>\Mappings\TemplateIdMappings.xml</TemplateMapping
>
<ContextMapping>\Mappings\ContextMappings.xml</ContextMapping>
<AttributeMapping>\Mappings\AttributeMappings.xml</AttributeMapping>
<ClassMapping>\Mappings\ClassMappings.xml</ClassMapping>
<AssociationMapping>\Mappings\AssociationMappings.xml</AssociationMap
ping>
<ObjectIdMapping>\Mappings\ObjectIdMappings.xml</ObjectIdMapping>
<RenditionMapping>\Mappings\RenditionMappings.xml</RenditionMapping>

Release 5.0.10

Page 29

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

You can also add relative path in command line, as shown below:

· Processing through .exe location: You must type full and correct project file path

in -cp field and relative path for log files and output file locations.

C:\Program Files\AVEVA\AVEVA NET Gateways\Gateway For
CMIS>"AVEVA.NET.Gateways.CMIS.App.exe" -cp "D:
\Demo\Demo_CMIS\CMIS_Demo.xml" -un "user.name" -pw
"user.password" -ad "http://inhyddpc111:8080/emc-cmis/resources" -
op "\Output" lp "\Logs"

· Processing through project directory: You must type project.xml file name only

in -cp and relative path for log and output locations:

D:\Demo\Demo_25Feb>"C:\Program Files\AVEVA\AVEVA NET
Gateways\Gateway For CMIS>"AVEVA.NET.Gateways.CMIS.App.exe" -
cp "CMIS_Demo.xml" -un "user.name" -pw "user.password" -ad
"http://inhyddpc111:8080/emc-cmis/resources" -op "\Output" lp
"\Logs"

The Gateway contains the following custom output file path creation settings under

Output Settings tab:

· Custom output folder structure: Enables you to define a custom folder location
where all processed output files are stored. You can fully customise the output

folder structure with no attributes added to the end of the output hierarchy. By

default, the output folder hierarchy is defined as

<FolderHierarchy>DOCUMENT[cmis:name][cmis:versionSeriesId]-
[cmis:versionLabel]</FolderHierarchy>. For more information about

configuring the custom output folder structure, see Creating Custom Output

Folder Structure.

· Custom output file name: Enables you to define a custom file name.

Notes:

· You can create a folder structure or a file name using both fixed strings and
CMIS attributes (shown in square brackets), which are returned from the

DMS server. The attribute names in the square brackets are resolved with

their values while creating the output folder location for a document object.
· If any attribute name is incorrect or unavailable, its value is skipped from the

path.

Release 5.0.10

Page 30

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

6.3.3

Query Settings

The Query Settings tab allows you configure the settings that are used  to fetch the

required documents, as shown in the following image:

Release 5.0.10

Page 31

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

The Query Settings tab contains the following options under Details as described in the

following table:

Parameters

DocumentType

Selection Criteria

Description

Configures the document type to be used to fetch documents
from DMS Server using CMIS standards. The documents can
be of type cmis:document or any document types derived
from cmis:document.

Selects only those document objects that match the criteria
defined in this section.
Notes:
· For more information on how the selection criteria should
be defined, refer to the CMIS standard documentation
available at http://docs.oasis-
open.org/cmis/CMIS/v1.0/cmis-spec-v1.0.html and
corresponding DMS system reference guides.
For example, to identify all the properties not allowed in the
WHERE clause when querying Documentum server, refer to
Query Mapping section of the EMC Documentum CMIS
Reference Guide, version 6.7. available at
http://www.emc.com/collateral/TechnicalDocument/docu3
3434.pdf.

· If you copy the query from sources such as E-mail or

Microsoft Word to Criteria / Query section, ensure that you
replace special characters such as single quotes and double
quotes using the computer keyboard. This prevents the Bad
Request error being thrown during the execution of the
query as the special characters from other sources are
converted as smart quotes.

· For more information about CMIS Query limitation in

OpenText Content Server, see CMIS Query Limitations in
OpenText Content Server.

Revision Settings

Configures which revisions of the selected documents should
be retrieved.

Latest Revision Only

Specifies that only the latest revision of a document should be
included in the output.

   - Include Revision
version in output file
structures

All Revisions

Includes the revision tag in the generated EIWM output file.

Specifies that all revisions of a document should be included
in the output.

   - Single Output File

Groups all revisions to a single EIWM file. By default, each
revision of a document is output in a separate EIWM file.

Release 5.0.10

Page 32

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

Parameters

Description

Include association
relationships

Includes all the relationships of type cmis:relationship
present on a document object as associations in the output
file. These associations can be manipulated using association
mapping before writing into the output file.
Note: The document object should have a base type of
CMIS_DOCUMENT for converting the relationships to
corresponding associations in output file.

Include only first
matching rendition type

Downloads or links with the first matched rendition identified
by the settings, which are defined in the Rendition mapping
file. Refer to the Note below for more details.

Maximum items per
page

Specifies the maximum number of objects to be queried on a
single request to DMS server.

Save Settings

Selects the Save Settings option to save the modified settings
to the project settings file.

Notes:

· If you select the Include only first matching rendition type box in the Query
Settings tab, the gateway downloads or links with the first matched rendition

identified from the rendition mapping settings.

For example, the following are the input files in the DMS server:

· Input file File 1 contains renditions: .doc (primary), .svg, .xlsx and .pdf

files.

· Input file File 2 contains renditions of .doc (primary), .dwg and .dgn files.

The following is the rendition mapping configuration:

<Add ClassID = "*"    RenditionType = "Download+Link"
ExpectedRendition =".dwg"   />
<Add ClassID = "*"    RenditionType = "Download+Link"
ExpectedRendition =".dgn"   />
<Add ClassID = "*"    RenditionType = "Download+Link"
ExpectedRendition =".xlsx"   />
<Add ClassID = "*"    RenditionType = "Download+Link"
ExpectedRendition =".pdf"   />

The Output is as follows:

For File 1: Gateway downloads .xlsx rendition and adds a link to .xlsx file in the

EIWM. This is because .xlsx file is the first matched rendition for this file and

therefore ignores all other renditions.

For File 2: Gateway downloads .dwg rendition and adds a link to .dwg file in the

EIWM. This is because .dwg file is the first matched rendition for this file and

therefore ignores all other renditions.

Release 5.0.10

Page 33

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

· If you do not select the Include only first matching rendition type box in Query

Settings tab, the gateway downloads or links with all matched renditions

identified from the rendition mapping settings.

For example, the following are the input files in the DMS server:

· Input file File 1 contains renditions: .doc (primary), .svg and .dgn files.
· Input file File 2 contains renditions: .pdf (primary), .dwg and .xlsx files.

The following is the rendition mapping configuration:

<Add ClassID = "*"    RenditionType = "Download"  ExpectedRendition
="primary"   />
<Add ClassID = "*"    RenditionType = "Download+Link"
ExpectedRendition =".dgn"   />
<Add ClassID = "*"    RenditionType = "Link"  ExpectedRendition
=".xlsx"   />

The output is as follows:

For File 1: Gateway downloads both .doc and .dgn renditions and adds a link to

the .dgn rendition in the EIWM.

For File 2: Gateway downloads the .pdf rendition because it is the primary

rendition. It also adds a link to the .xlsx rendition in the EIWM.

Configure DateTimeFormat in Query:

When a new project configuration is created, it includes the parameter DateTimeFormat

with a default value of yyyy-MM-dd hh:mm:ss. This is the format used to build the

queries for the date-time parameters such as start date and end date. If the DMS server

uses a different date-time format other than the default format, then the

DateTimeFormat parameter must be updated. Note that the DateTimeFormat

parameter does not support long date formats, for example, DD-MMMM-YYYY.

Note: The dates provided from the command line and the dates stored by the invoker are

in a default format (yyyy-MM-dd hh:mm:ss). Hence, the Gateway converts these dates

to the format specified by the DateTimeFormat parameter.

Release 5.0.10

Page 34

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

Examples:

· If the format in the project configuration file is changed to dd-MM-yyyy

hh:mm:ss to match the CMIS server date-time format and the command line

user provides a start date (in the yyyy-MM-dd hh:mm:ss format) of 2015-01-

31T23:59:59, then the Gateway converts this to 31-01-2015T23:59:59 when

used in the CMIS query.

The following XML snippet shows the way to use the date-time format in the

query criteria.

<DateTimeFormat>dd-MM-yyyy hh:mm:ss</DateTimeFormat>
· The format in the project configuration file is changed to dd-MMM-yyyy to

match the CMIS server date time format and the command line user provides a

start date (in the yyyy-MM-dd hh:mm:ss format) of 2015-01-31T23:59:59,

then the Gateway converts this to 31-JAN-2015 when used in the CMIS query.

The following XML snippet shows the way to use the date format only in the

query criteria.

<DateTimeFormat>dd-MMM-yy</DateTimeFormat>

Configure DateTimePrefix:

The Gateway constructs the incremental extraction queries internally by taking DateTime

parameters such as start date and end date in string format. As per the CMIS

specification, a DateTime parameter inside the query can either be represented as a

literal or be represented as a string:

DateTime literal in the form of TIMESTAMP’<DateTime string>’

<DateTime string> in the form of YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sss[Z | +hh:mm | -

hh:mm]

The representation of the DateTime in a query varies across DMS servers. An optional

configuration entry DateTimePrefix lets you add the “TIMESTAMP” prefix to the start

date and end date whenever the DMS requires a DateTime literal.

When a new project configuration is created, it includes the parameter DateTimePrefix

with an empty value by default. If your DMS server requires a DateTime literal, then the

DateTimePrefix parameter should be set to “TIMESTAMP”:

<DateTimePrefix>TIMESTAMP</DateTimePrefix>

Note: In the previous versions of the Gateway, this effect was already observed when

connecting to an OpenText server because OpenText CMIS server only accepts DateTime

parameters as a literal. The query was already prefixed with "TIMESTAMP" internally

and this functionality is maintained in the current version. Therefore, setting the

DateTimePrefix value to empty or to "TIMESTAMP" will have the same effect when

connecting to an OpenText server.

Release 5.0.10

Page 35

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
