CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

Relationships Extraction:

When "Include association relationships" is cleared, no relationships are extracted

from the DMS server.

When "Include association relationships" is selected, all the relationships defined in

the association mapping file are extracted from the DMS server.

Note: During relationship mapping, mapping ID (TargetID mapping), ClassID

(TargetClassID mapping) and Contexts can either be provided with fixed string or be

derived from its related object but not from its parent object.

Usage example from attributes:

<Association Relationship="peer" >

<TargetID Value="[cmis attribute of related object]" />
<TargetClassID Value="[cmis attribute of related object]" />
<ContextID Value="[cmis attribute of related object]" />

</Association>

Usage example with fixed strings:

<Association Relationship="peer" >

<TargetID Value="001" />
<TargetClassID Value="Document" />
<ContextID Value="IPE" />

</Association>

In AssociationMapping.xml:

· If the “KeepUnmappedAssociations” attribute is set to true, all relations
present in the DMS system for a document are added in the EIWM file.

· If the “KeepUnmappedAssociations” attribute is set to false, only mapped

relations are added in the EIWM file.

Limitations on Extracting Relationships Using CMIS 1.0 Implementation:

· Microsoft SharePoint 2010/2013 does not support extracting relationships

defined between a source object and a target object.

· OpenText Content Server version 16.0.2 does not support extracting relationships

defined between a source object and a target object.

· Documentum Content Server supports extracting relationships defined between
two objects. But it cannot be queried as cmis:relationship is a non-queryable

type in Documentum.

Release 5.0.10

Page 36

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

· Relationships in the Gateway cannot be extracted incrementally because

Documentum Content Server does not provide values of attributes

cmis:creationDate and cmis:lastModificationDate for object type

cmis:relationship.

· Updating a cmis:relationship object does not modify the objects (source and
destination) that are linked to each other using the cmis:relationship object.

Incremental extract does not provide the modified cmis:relationship objects if

the source and destination objects are not modified.

Release 5.0.10

Page 37

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

6.4

Executing Projects

The Execute button allows you to execute the project after you select the required query

settings, output settings and mappings. While executing the query, the Gateway searches

the specified DMS repository and generates EIWM files.

During the execution process, the gateway creates a Summary file present in the Log Path,

as described in Output Settings. The Summary file contains the list of all objects with its

rendition processing status (link and/or download). A warning is logged in the Summary

file if no rendition is found for an object.

To execute the selected project:

1.

In the Project Explorer, click Execute.

2. Refine the query further using the Details box as shown in the following:

· Document Selection Criteria: This field allows you to fetch the required

documents from the repositories based upon a specific date range. This field

has the following sub-options:

· Fetch All Documents: Select this option to fetch all documents from

the repositories.

· Fetch Changed Documents: Select the start date and end date as per
your requirements to fetch specific documents based on a specific

date range.

· Query: View the exact query used to fetch the documents from the
repository. You can validate the accuracy of the query shown in the

Query box and if required you can edit the query, using the Edit Query

option.

· Edit Query: Edit the query if you want to run a more complex query than the
one generated using the settings mentioned. You can use the edited query

also to fetch the documents from repositories when executed. This modified

query is not saved as part of project and is used to scan the documents as a

temporary query.

Note:

· A Bad Request error is thrown during query execution if special

characters from other sources, such as E-mail or Microsoft Word, are

converted as smart quotes. To avoid this, ensure that you delete and re-

key special characters such as single quotes and double quotes using the

computer keyboard.

Release 5.0.10

Page 38

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

· The query displayed in the Edit Query section is not persistent. Every
time you switch the radio button between Fetch All Documents and

Fetch Changed Documents, the query is reset to the saved query

present in the Query Settings. When you modify the Start Date and/or

the End Date on Fetch Changed Documents, the query is modified on

the saved query (present in the Query Settings), and not on the modified

one. The changes done in the Query section of the Execute page are lost.

The new query amends the Project Settings/Query Settings' Selection

Criteria section.

· If you want the query to persist, modify it in the Project Settings/Query

Settings' Selection Criteria section.

· Execute: Click Execute. A Login window opens which allows you to enter the

user name, password and repository details for the CMIS connection.

· CMIS Service URL: Endpoint Address is the URL of the DMS service.
· User Name: Type the user name that is used to connect to the

corresponding DMS server. Type the domain name as part of User

Name if the DMS server is in some other domain.

· Password: Type the password for the User Name mentioned

above.

· Repository: Select the DMS Repository ID from the drop-down list.
· Save Connection Settings: Select this option if you want to

store the used connection settings in the configuration file.

The stored connection settings are loaded while loading the

project and are shown on the login screen by default.
· Options: Click Options to define a specific timeout values used by the

gateway to return timeout exceptions if it fails to connect to the DMS server

through CMIS.

· Type the connection timeout value in the Connect Timeout box
that you can use to establish the initial connection to the DMS

server using CMIS.

· Type the reader timeout details used for subsequent requests to

the server.

· Connect: Click Connect to connect to the DMS via CMIS.
· Show Scan Log: Allows you to view the log files, which the application

generates during project execution.

Release 5.0.10

Page 39

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Running the Gateway using the Project Explorer

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

3. The Gateway queries and processes the data from different DMS servers and

generates the EIWM XML files.

4.

If you select the option Custom output folder structure, the EIWM XML files

are created in the following folder structure:
· Under the Output folder, sub-folders are categorised based on the Class ID

of the document.

· Under the Class folder, sub-folders are categorised based on the Filename of

the document.

· Under Filename folder, sub-folders are categorised based on the Version
Series ID(cmis:versionSeriesId) and Version no. (cmis:versionLabel).

The following is the example of a typical Output folder path with EIWM file:

Output Folder(user specified )\Pump(Class ID of the document )

\22pe155001_Microstation_3d(File name )\090000658000796e-1.0(version

series id – version no)\ 22pe155001_Microstation_3d_null.xml(EIWM File)

If you select the option Custom output folder structure, and want to create a

customised output folder structure, you can see Appendix C: Creating Custom

Output Folder Structure.

5. The query displayed in Edit Query section is not persisted, every time you

switch the radio button between Fetch All Documents and Fetch Changed

Documents, the query is reset to the saved one present in Query Settings. So,

when you modify the Start Date and/or End Date on Fetch Changed

Documents, the query is modified on the saved one not on the modified one.

6.

If you want the query to persist, modify it from Project Settings->Query Setting

section.

Note: It is not possible to view via Dashboard any SVG files stored in a DMS.

Therefore, you must not configure filters to select the SVG renditions as links.

Release 5.0.10

Page 40

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

7

Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

The following sections describe general details about mapping configuration.

Each mapping type is defined in its own configuration file. The core of these files is a set of
Mapping Entries. A simple Attribute Mapping Entry is shown below:

<Attribute Class="Pipe" Attribute="length_value"><Name Value="Length"/>

<Value Value="[length_value]"/>
<Units Value="[length_units]"/>

</Attribute>
The general form of a Mapping Entry is as follows:

· XML attributes that determine which objects in the source the mapping is applied
to are specified as attributes on the entry itself; ‘Class’ and ‘Attribute’ in the
example above. These are referred to below as Qualifiers.

· Values that the mapping may set are specified as XML child elements of the entry

element; ‘Name’, ‘Value’, and ‘Units’ in the example above.

· XML child elements of the value elements specify transformations that may be
applied to the source value to generate the output value. These are examined

later.

Mapping entries are examined in the order in which they are specified in the mapping

configuration file.

Release 5.0.10

Page 41

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

7.1

Common Mapping Concepts

This section details common mapping concepts.

7.1.1

Qualifiers

Mapping entries support three types of qualifiers that determine if the entry should be

applied:

Qualifiers

Description

Class

Attribute

Attribute
Pattern

Specifies the ID of a class. Only objects of this classification matches
the entry. The special value ‘*’ may be used to match any class.
Note: The Class qualifier is not supported for Class Mapping or
Template ID mapping.

Specifies the name of an attribute from the source system. Only
objects that have an attribute of this name match the mapping
entry.

Specifies a regular expression and is used in conjunction with the
Attribute qualifier. Only objects that have an attribute of this name,
with a value that matches the expression matches the entry.

7.1.2

Attribute References

Any Value (with the exception of the name of a Characteristic or Property) may be set

from the value of an attribute read from the DMS. To include an attribute value in the

output, you must specify the name of the source attribute in square brackets.

In the attribute mapping example above, the Value [length_units’ indicates that the

value of the attribute named length_units should be inserted as the units of the EIWM

Property generated in the output.

7.1.3

Timestamp References

You can user a special placeholder value to insert the current date and time into a value.

This placeholder is specified as [DateAndTime].
 <Template>

   <TemplateID Value="[object_name][DateAndTime]"/>

   </Template>

The Template Mapping entry in the above example generates a template ID from the

object_name attribute concatenated with the current date and time.

The format of the date and time inserted into the value is: dd-MM-yyyy HH:mm:ss.

Release 5.0.10

Page 42

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

7.1.4

Lookups

Any Value (with the exception of the name of a Characteristic or Property) may be
specified as a lookup from an external data source. You must specify any value (with the
exception of the name of a Characteristic or Property) as a lookup from an external data
source. This data source can be one of the following:

· A delimited text file for example, a .CSV file
· A Microsoft Excel spreadsheet file
· A Microsoft Access database file
· A Microsoft SQL Server database
· An Oracle database

You must define the data source in the appropriate mapping configuration file. The

following is an example of the definition of an Microsoft Excel data source:

<MSExcelLookupDataSource
 Id="Attribute Value Map"

       File="C:\Mapping\Data Value Lookup\Mapping1.xls"
       Table="Sheet1"
       SourceColumn="[Source]"
       TargetColumn="[Destination]"/>

You can specify any number of data sources in a mapping configuration file.

To make use of a lookup, you must reference the Id of the data source within a mapping

entry:

<Attribute Class="Document" Attribute="name">

<Value>

<Lookup Id="Attribute Value Map">
<FailAction Action = "FixedValue" Value="[name]"/>
</Lookup>

</Value>
</Attribute>

In the above example, an Excel spreadsheet is used to store a list of lookup values. The

value of the name attribute from the source system is passed to the lookup. This value is

compared with the contents of the Source column in the Sheet1 sheet of the Excel file

and if a matching value is found, the contents of the Destination column is returned and

used as the Characteristic value in the output.

Release 5.0.10

Page 43

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

The Sheet1 sheet is constructed as follows:

The FailAction element specifies the following values you can use if no matching values is

found in the datasource:

· FixedValue – the fall-back value to use is specified as the Value attribute of

FailAction.

· EmptyValue – an empty value should be output if no matching value is found.

Lookups may return an attribute name (in square brackets) and have this resolved to the
attribute value in the output (see Attribute References). The following data sources are
supported:

The following are the data sources which are supported:

<CsvLookupDataSource
     Id="CsvLookup1"
     File="C:\Mapping\Data Value Lookup\Mapping1.csv"
     Provider="Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0"
     Separator=","
     SourceColumn="Key"
     TargetColumn="Value" />
 <MSAccessLookupDataSource
   Id="MSAccessLookup1"
   File="C:\Mapping\Data Value Lookup\Mapping1.accdb"
   Query="SELECT [Key], [Value] FROM [Table1]"
   Provider="Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0"
   SourceColumn="Key"
   TargetColumn="Value" />
<MSExcelLookupDataSource
     Id="ExcelLookup1"
     File="C:\Mapping\Data Value Lookup\Mapping1.xls"
     Provider="Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0"
     ExtendedProperties="Excel 12.0; HDR=YES"
     Table="Sheet1"
     SourceColumn="Key"
     TargetColumn="Value" />
<MSSqlLookupDataSource
     Id="MSSqlLookup1"
     ConnectionString="Data Source=host;Initial Catalog=MyDb;User ID=user;Pas
sword=pass;"
     Query="SELECT [Key], [Value] FROM [Table1]"
     SourceColumn="[Key]"
     TargetColumn="[Value]" />

Release 5.0.10

Page 44

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

<OracleLookupDataSource
     Id="OracleLookup1"
     ConnectionString="OLEDB.NET=true;PLSQLRSet=true;User ID=user;password
=pass;"
     Query="SELECT [Key], [Value] FROM [Table1]"
     SourceColumn="Key"
     TargetColumn="[Value]" />

Note: When more than one type of data source is specified, you must specify the data

sources in the order listed above, that is, CSV>Microsoft Access>Microsoft Excel, and so

on.

The Provider and Extended Properties attributes are optional. If not specified, these

default to the values shown above.

7.1.5

Transforms

Any Value in the mapping (with the exception of the name of a Characteristic or
Property), permits transformation of the source value.

<Attribute Class="Pump" Attribute="att1" AttributePattern="^\d{3}_[A-Z]{3}
_\d{3}_[a-z]{3}$" ><Name Value="Attribute 1" />

<Value>

<Transforms>
<Remove Pattern="_[a-z]{3}$" />
<Replace Pattern="_[A-Z]{3}" Value="_XXX" />
<InsertAfter Pattern="\d{3}$" Value="*" />
<InsertBefore Pattern="_XXX" Value="0" />
</Transforms>

</Value>
</Attribute>

The following transformations of the source value are available:

Transformations Descriptions

Remove

Replace

InsertAfter

InsertBefore

Permits parts of the source value to be removed. Pattern specifies a
regular expression used to identify part of the value to remove.

Permits parts of the source value to be replaced. Pattern specifies a
regular expression used to identify part of the value to replace, and
Value specifies the replacement text.

Permits the insertion of text after a position in the value specified by
Pattern. Value specifies the text to insert.

Permits the insertion of text before a position in the value specified
by Pattern. Value specifies the text to insert.

You can include any number and type of transformations for a given Value.

The values specified for the Replace and Insert transforms may reference other

attributes from the source system, for example, [attribute_name].

Release 5.0.10

Page 45

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

If you specify multiple transformations, the transforms are processed in the order listed in

the configuration. Transforms may be combined with lookups. In this case, the transform

is applied first and the resulting value is passed to the lookup as a key.

When using transformations, it is recommended that you specify AttributePattern to

ensure the attribute value is in the format expected by the transformation sequence.

7.1.6

Regular Expression Evaluation Timeout

Evaluation of complex regular expressions can sometimes take a significant amount of

time. Each mapping file may optionally specify a timeout value (in seconds), for evaluation

of regular expressions used in mapping entries specified in the file.

Note: The default value for this timeout if not specified is 60 seconds.

<AssociationMapping RegExTimeoutSecs="10">
<AttributeMapping KeepUnmappedAttributes="false" RegExTimeoutSecs="10">
<ClassMapping RegExTimeoutSecs="10">
<ContextMapping RegExTimeoutSecs="10">
<ObjectIdMapping RegExTimeoutSecs="10">
<TemplateMapping RegExTimeoutSecs="10">

If the regular expression is not evaluated within the time period specified, an error is

raised.

7.2

Object ID Mapping

Object ID Mapping, also known as Identification, determines the ID assigned to an object

in the output. Each mapping entry in the configuration is examined in sequence and the

first mapping entry to match is used to generate the Object ID.

A simple example, taking the ID from an attribute value is shown below:

<Object Attribute="object_name" >

<ObjectID />

</Object>

Since no ‘Value’ is specified on ‘ObjectID’, the value of the attribute specified by

Attribute is used for the ID. The mapping above is equivalent to:

<Object Attribute="object_name" >

<ObjectID Value="[object_name]" />

</Object>

A regular expression may be used to match specific formats of attribute value:

<Object Attribute="object_name" AttributePattern="^[a-z]\d{4}[.][a-z]{2}$" >

<ObjectID />

</Object>

Release 5.0.10

Page 46

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

An example taking multiple attributes with their corresponding patterns to match specific

format of their attribute values:

<Object>

<Conditions>
      <Attribute Name="object_id_prefix" />
      <Attribute Name="object_id" />
</Conditions>
<ObjectID Value="[object_id_prefix]_[object_id]" />

</Object>

Note: All conditions must be met in order for the mapping to be applied.

A class qualifier may be specified to match only objects of the specified class:

<Object Class="Document" Attribute="object_name" AttributePattern="^[a-z]
\d{4}[.][a-z]{2}$" >

<ObjectID/>

</Object>

A class specifier may take a regular expression used to match specific formats of class

value by adding IsRegEx="true". The value of IsRegEx is false by default if the

attribute is not specified.

<Object Class="([A-Z])\w+" IsRegEx="true" Attribute="object_name"
AttributePattern="^[a-z]\d{4}[.][a-z]{2}$">

<ObjectID />

</Object>

Look-ups may be used to obtain the object ID from an external data source.

<Object Attribute="object_name" >Fregex
<ObjectID Value="[object_name]">
<Lookup Id="Object ID Map">
<FailAction Action="FixedValue" Value="[object_name]" />

</Lookup>

</ObjectID>

</Object>

In this case, the value of the object_name attribute is used as a key to look up a value for

the Object ID. If no match is found, the attribute value is used directly. You can use

Transforms to modify the value of an attribute.

Note: If an object does not match with any of the mapping entries, it is not included in the
output. This provides a means of filtering out unwanted objects. In order to avoid this, an
entry should be created at the bottom of the mapping file that matches any object. For
example,

<Object>
   <ObjectID Value="[object_name]" />
</Object>

Release 5.0.10

Page 47

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

7.3

Class Mapping

Class Mapping, also known as Classification, determines the class assigned to the object

in the output. Each mapping entry in the configuration is examined in turn and the first

matching entry is used to generate the object’s Class ID.

A simple example, taking the ID from an attribute value is shown below:

<Class>

<ClassID Value="[object_class]" />

</Class>

You can use a Regular expression to match specific formats of attribute value:

<Class Attribute="object_class" AttributePattern="^[A-Z]+$">

<ClassID Value="[object_class]" />

</Class>

A simple example taking multiple attributes with its corresponding patterns to match

specific format of its attribute value:

<Class>

<Conditions>

      <Attribute Name="object_class" Pattern="MOTOR" />
      <Attribute Name="object_subclass" Pattern="VERTICAL" />

</Conditions>
<ClassID Value="[object_subclass]_[object_class]" />

</Class>

Note: All conditions must be met in order for the mapping to be applied.

You can also use Lookups and Transforms to obtain the Class ID. If an object does not

match any of the mapping entries, it is assigned a class ID of UNKNOWN in the output.

7.4

Context Mapping

Context Mapping determines the ID of the context within which an Object ID resides.

Each mapping entry in the configuration is examined in sequence and the first mapping

entry to match is used to generate the object’s context ID.

An example taking the context from an attribute value is shown below:

<Context Class="DOCUMENT" Attribute="area" >

<ContextID Value="[area]" />

</Context>

A class specifier may take a regular expression used to match specific formats of class

value by adding IsRegEx="true". The value of IsRegEx is false by default if the

attribute is not specified.

Release 5.0.10

Page 48

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

<Context Class="([A-Z])\w+" IsRegEx="true" Attribute="object_name"
AttributePattern="^[a-z]\d{4}[.][a-z]{2}$">

<ContextID/>

</Context>

An example taking multiple attributes with their corresponding patterns to match specific

format of their attribute value:

<Context Class="DOCUMENT">
      <Conditions>
        <Attribute Name="object_context" Pattern="ROOT" />
        <Attribute Name="object_subcontext" Pattern="PARENT" />
      </Conditions>
      <ContextID Value="[object_context]|[object_subcontext]" />
</Context>

Note: All conditions must be met in order for the mapping to be applied.

You can specify multiple levels of context using the ‘|’ character to separate levels:

<Context>

<ContextID Value="ROOT|PARENT|CHILD" />

</Context>
<Context>

<ContextID Value="[organisation]|[asset_code]|[area]" />

</Context>

You can use Lookups and transforms to generate multiple levels of context; for this you

need to specify a set of nested <ContextID> elements:

<Context>

<ContextID Value="ROOT" >

<ContextID Value="[asset]">

<Lookup Id="Context Map"> <!-- Lookup using [asset] value
<FailAction Action="FixedValue" Value="[asset]"/>

-->

</Lookup>
<ContextID Value="[area]">

<Transforms>

<Remove Pattern="_\d{2}$" />
<!-- Remove from [area] value

-->

</Transforms>

</ContextID>

</ContextID>

</ContextID>

</Context>

If an object does not match any of the mapping entries, it is not assigned any context.

To set a default context that will be used if none of the other mapping entries match, an

entry with no qualifiers should be created at the bottom of the mapping file:

<!-- No Class or Attribute qualifiers - this will be applied to all objects
<Context>
<ContextID Value="DEFAULT" />
</Context>

-->

Release 5.0.10

Page 49

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

7.5

Attribute Mapping

Attribute Mapping determines how attributes from the source system are included in the

output. For each attribute from the source system, each mapping entry is examined to

determine whether the attribute matches.

Keep Unmapped Attributes

The Keep Unmapped Attributes setting determines whether attributes that are not

matched are included in the output.

<AttributeMapping KeepUnmappedAttributes="true">

If this setting is true, then attributes with no matching mapping entry are included in the

output as-is with no mapping.

If the setting is false, then only attributes that have a matching mapping entry are

included.

Mapping Entries

The simplest form of attribute mapping entry is shown below:

A class specifier may take a regular expression used to match specific formats of class

value by adding IsRegEx="true". The value of IsRegEx is false by default if the

attribute is not specified.

<Attribute Class="([A-Z])\w+" IsRegEx="true" Attribute="name" >

<Name Value="Document Name" />

</Attribute>

This mapping entry indicates that for objects classified as Document, the Name attribute

from the source system, if present, should be included as a Characteristic in the output.

The Characteristic value will be taken directly from the value of the name attribute from

the source system.

Note: The Attribute qualifier is required in attribute mapping entries.

You may specify an AttributePattern property to match only attributes with a value that

conforms to the regular expression.

Release 5.0.10

Page 50

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

Attribute mapping entries permit the following Values to be specified:

· Name of the Characteristic or Property
· Value of a Property
· Units of a Property

Name

A mapping entry that specifies the name of the Characteristic in the output is shown
below:

<Attribute Class="Document" Attribute="name">

<Name Value="Document Name" />

</Attribute>
Note: The <Name> element does not support attribute references, lookups, or
transforms, only a fixed value as shown above.

Value

Some examples of mapping entries that specify a Value are shown below:

<Attribute Class="Document" Attribute="name" >

<Value />
</Attribute>
<Attribute Class="Document" Attribute="name" >

<Value Value="[name]" />

</Attribute>
<Attribute Class="Document" Attribute="name" >

<Value Value="[name].[ext]" />

</Attribute>

The first two entries above are equivalent – if no Value property is specified within the

<Value /> element then the attribute specified by the Attribute property is used.

The final entry illustrates how to build an attribute value by concatenating two attributes

from the source system; name and ext.

You may use a lookup to obtain the attribute value. You may use transforms to modify the

attribute value from the source system.

Transforms may be combined with a lookup, in which case the transform is applied to the
source value and the resulting value is passed to lookup:

<Attribute Attribute="name" AttributePattern="^[a-z]{3}_\d{3}$" >

<Value>

 <Transforms>

  <Remove Pattern="_\d{3}$" />

 </Transforms>
 <Lookup Id="Attribute Value Map" >

<FailAction Action = "FixedValue" Value="[name]" />

</Lookup>

</Value>
</Attribute>

Release 5.0.10

Page 51

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

Units

You may specify a Units value to create a Property rather than a Characteristic in the

output:

<Attribute Class="Pipe" Attribute="length_value" >

<Name Value="Length" />
<Value Value="[length_value]" />
<Units Value="[length_units]" />

</Attribute>

The above example generates a Property called ‘Length’ on Pipe objects that contain an

attribute named ‘length_value’. The value of the Property will be taken from the

‘length_value’ attribute, and the units of the Property will be taken from the

‘length_units’ attribute.

You can use transforms and lookups in conjunction with the Units value.

Datasets

Attribute mapping may be configured to create Datasets associated with the source

object. Each Characteristic and Property generated in the output may be attached to a

Dataset rather than the source object.

<Datasets>

<Dataset Id="DatasetSAP" >

<DatasetID>

<Transforms>

<InsertAfter Pattern="^.*$" Value=" SAP Dataset" />

</Transforms>

</DatasetID>
<ContextID Value="IPE" />

</Dataset>

</Datasets>
<Attributes>

<Attribute Class="Pipe" Attribute="area_code" >

 <Dataset Id="DatasetSAP" />
 <Name Value="Area" />

</Attribute>

<Attribute Class="Pipe" Attribute="length_value" >

 <Dataset Id="DatasetSAP" />
 <Name Value="Length" />
 <Value Value="[length_value]" />
 <Units Value="[length_units]" />

</Attribute>

</Attributes>

The example above defines a Dataset in the IPE context whose ID is based upon the ID of

the source object with SAP Dataset appended to it. The <DatasetID> and

<ContextID> elements support attribute references, transforms, and lookups. The

Dataset will be assigned a class ID of ‘Dataset’.

Release 5.0.10

Page 52

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

The Id property, DatasetSAP is used to reference the Dataset from within attribute

mapping entries. This indicates that the Characteristic or Property generated in the output

should be attached to the referenced Dataset. Multiple Datasets may be defined.

The example above creates an Area Characteristic and a Length property on the Dataset.

Attribute mapping adds the ability to specify a Class ID for the Dataset, as shown below:

<Dataset Id="DatasetSAP" >

<DatasetID>

<Transforms>

 <InsertAfter Pattern="^.*$" Value=" SAP Dataset" />

 </Transforms>

 </DatasetID>
 <ContextID Value="IPE" />
<ClassID Value="SAP DATASET" />

 </Dataset>

7.5.1

Name Mapping

Name Mapping determines where the EIWM object name is obtained from.

This mapping is included in the Attribute Mapping configuration file. Each name mapping

entry in the configuration is examined in turn and the first matching entry is used to

generate the object’s name.

Mapping Entries

An example name attribute mapping entry is shown below:
<NameAttribute Class="EQUIPMENT" Attribute="DESCRIPTION" >
   <Value Value="[DESCRIPTION]" />
</NameAttribute>

A class specifier may take a regular expression used to match specific formats of class

value by adding IsRegEx="true". The value of IsRegEx is false by default if the attribute is

not specified.

<NameAttribute Class="([A-Z])\w+" IsRegEx="true" Attribute="DESCRIPTION" >
   <Value Value="[DESCRIPTION]" />
</NameAttribute>

This mapping entry indicates that for objects classified as EQUIPMENT, the Description

attribute from the source system, if present, should be used as the object’s name in the

output.

Release 5.0.10

Page 53

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

An example taking multiple attributes with its corresponding patterns to match specific

format of their attribute values:

<NameAttribute Class="Document">

 <Conditions>
      <Attribute Name="first_part" />
      <Attribute Name="second_part" />
 </Conditions>
 <Value Value="AAA-[first_part]-[second_part]" />

</NameAttribute>

Note: All conditions must be met in order for the mapping to be applied.

This mapping entry indicates that for objects classified as EQUIPMENT, the Description

attribute from the source system, if present, should be used as the object’s name in the

output.

Class, Attribute,  and AttributePattern qualifiers may be used in name mapping entries.

Transforms and lookups may be used in conjunction with the Value element.

7.6

Association Mapping

Association mapping allows extracted relationships to be customised in the output. It also

permits new associations to be created in the output by transforming attributes from the

source system.

A mapping entry may apply to a relationship from the source, or an attribute from the

source depending upon whether it is customising an association or generating a new one.

For each object from the source system, each mapping entry in the configuration is

examined and compared against the object attributes. Each of the source object’s

relationships are then examined and compared to the mapping entries.

Keep Unmapped Associations

This setting applies only to extracted relationships.

<AssociationMapping KeepUnmappedAssociations="true">

If this setting is true, then relationships with no matching mapping entry are included in

the output as-is with no mapping. If the setting is false, then only relationships that have a

matching mapping entry are included. The default value if not specified is false.

Mapping Entries

Creating new Associations

An example of an Association mapping entry that generates an association in the output

from an attribute in the source is shown below:

<Association Class="Document" IsRegEx= "true" Attribute="linked_item_id" >

Release 5.0.10

Page 54

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

      <AssociationType Value="is a part of" />
      <ContextID Value="[linked_item_context]" />
      <TargetID Value="[linked_item_id]" />
      <TargetClassID Value="[linked_item_class]" />
</Association>

A class specifier may take a regular expression used to match specific formats of class

value by adding IsRegEx="true". The value of IsRegEx is false by default if the

attribute is not specified.

<Association Class="([A-Z])\w+" IsRegEx="true" Attribute="object_name"
AttributePattern="^[a-z]\d{4}[.][a-z]{2}$">

<ObjectID/>
</Association>

An example of Association mapping entry taking multiple attributes and their

corresponding patterns:

<Association Class="Document">

<Conditions>
      <Attribute Name="object_type" Pattern="DIG" />
      <Attribute Name="object_subtype" Pattern="PID" />
</Conditions>
<AssociationType Value="is a part of" />
<ContextID Value="IPE" />
<TargetID Value="[object_type]_[object_subtype]" />
<TargetClassID Value="EQUIPMENT" />

</Association>

Note: All conditions must be met in order for the mapping to be applied.

An AttributePattern property can be specified in order to match only attributes with a

value that conforms to the regular expression.

Mapping Extracted Relationships

In order to provide mapping for an extracted relationship, a Relationship property can

be specified as a qualifier to match only relationships of a particular type:

<Association Class="Document" Relationship="linked_item" >

<AssociationType Value="is a part of" />
<ContextID Value="[linked_item_context]" />
<TargetID Value="[linked_item_id]" />
<TargetClassID Value="[linked_item_class]" />

</Association>

This entry will match relationships that were extracted from the source with a relationship

type of “linked_item”.

Note: A relationship consists of two objects, the source object that was identified for

extraction by the Gateway, and the target object that it is related to.

All available attributes will be extracted for both objects, so the attributes of the related

object can be referenced in the mapping entry. However, the attributes of the related

object will not be included as part of the association in the EIWM output.

Release 5.0.10

Page 55

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

The Class qualifier refers to the class of the extracted ‘source’ object, as when creating

new associations from attributes. However, attribute references (for example, the

“linked_item_context”, “linked_item_id” and “linked_item_class” references

shown above), are taken from the related object.

Note: Including both ‘Attribute’ and ‘Relationship’ qualifiers on a mapping entry will

generate an error.

Both forms of Association Mapping entries permit four values to be specified; the type of

the association to generate, the ID of the object targeted by the association, the Context

ID of the object targeted by the association, and the Class ID of the object targeted by the

association.

Association Type

The <AssociationType> element specifies the association type to assign. This value

supports attribute references, lookups, and transforms.

Target ID

The <TargetID> element specifies the ID of the object to associate with the source

object. This value is typically taken from an attribute value. However, a fixed value might

be used to add an association to a specific object. This value supports attribute references,

lookups and transforms.

Target Context

The <ContextID> element specifies the context of the object to associate the source

object with. This value supports attribute references, lookups, and transforms.

Target Class

The <TargetClassID> element specifies the class of the object to associate the source

object with. This value supports attribute references, lookups, and transforms.

Multi-Value Attributes

The mapping entry below demonstrates how to create multiple associations in the output

from an attribute that holds multiple values. Multi-value attributes are assumed to store

multiple values delimited with a separator, (for example, comma, semi-colon, and so on).

Each value held in the attribute is assumed to be an object ID, and a separate association

will be created in the output for each value stored in the attribute.

Release 5.0.10

Page 56

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

Note: All associated objects must exist in the same context and have the same

classification. Each generated association will have the same association type.

<Association Class="WORKORDER" Attribute="part_codes" >

<AssociationType Value="is a part of" />
 <ContextID Value="IPE" />
 <TargetID Value="[part_codes]" >
      <MultiValue Separator=";" />

     </TargetID>
    <TargetClassID Value="EQUIPMENT" />
</Association>

Note: The <MultiValue> tag designates the ''part_codes'' attribute as a multi-value

attribute whose values are separated with a semi-colon.

The <MultiValue> tag is only supported for mapping attributes to associations. If this tag

is specified for a mapping entry that maps relationships, it will be ignored.

Expand Mapping Attribute

The Expand mapping feature lets you create multiple associations in the EIWM output

from an attribute value containing text delimited with a separator (for example, comma

and semicolon). If this attribute value matches the pattern from the mapping files, object

values can be expanded to multiple tags. This is based on the expansion character and

interpolate setting for that expand node from the mapping file. The attribute value can be

expanded into multiple tags incrementally to add multiple entries.

Each expanded tag of an attribute is assumed to be an Object ID and a separate

association is created in the output for each expanded tag value.
Notes:

· The Expand mapping pattern must contain the expansion character to consider

the attribute value for the expansion.

· If configured, the MultiValue separator mapping is applied first on the attribute
value. When these multiple values are generated, the Expand mappings are

applied to generate interpolated values. This does not depend on the ordering of

other mapping entries under the TargetID element.

Examples:

· If a matched tag with the pattern is P-101A/D and if the interpolate setting is
true and the expansion character is '/' then the following four tags will be

created: P- 101A, P-101B, P-101C and P-101D.

If interpolate setting is false and expansion character is '/' then the following two

tags will be created: P-101A and P-101D.

Release 5.0.10

Page 57

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

· If the attribute value is 50-A-D23-A/D and there are two expand entries '/'  and
'-', then the first expand will produce the following tags 50-A-D23-A, 50-A-

D23-B, 50-A-D23-C and 50-A-D23-D. The second expand will be applied on

top of these four expanded tags and produces the final tags: 50-A23-A, 50-B23-

A, 50-C23-A, 50-D23-A, 50-A23-B, 50-B23-B, 50-C23-B, 50-D23-B, 50-

A23-C, 50-B23-C, 50-C23-C, 50-D23-C, 50-A23-D, 50-B23-D, 50-C23-D

and 50-D23-D.

Mapping Configuration:

<Association Attribute="object_name" AttributePattern="^[A-Z]{3}$" >
      <AssociationType Value="is part of" />
      <ContextID Value="IPE" />
      <TargetID Value="07001-QE/QT-003;07001-FE-004A/B/C" >
         <MultiValue Separator=";" />
         <Expand Interpolate = "false">
            <Pattern>[A-Z][A-Z]/[A-Z][A-Z]</Pattern>
            <Char>/</Char>
          </Expand>
          <Expand Interpolate = "false">
            <Pattern>[A-Z]/[A-Z]/[A-Z]</Pattern>
            <Char>/</Char>
           </Expand>
       </TargetID>
    </Association>

7.7

Template Mapping
Template Mapping determines the ID of the template in an output EIWM file. Each
mapping entry in the configuration is examined in turn and the first matching entry is used
to generate the template ID.

Template Mapping uses the attributes of the first object in the EIWM file.

The example below appends the current date and time to the ‘name’ attribute from the
source to generate a unique template ID.

<Template>

<TemplateID Value="[name] [DateAndTime]"/>

</Template>

A simple example taking multiple attributes with its corresponding patterns to match

specific format of its attribute value:

<Template>

<Conditions>
      <Attribute Name="object_name" />
      <Attribute Name="object_id" />
</Conditions>
<TemplateID Value="[object_id] [object_name] Template" />

</Template>

Release 5.0.10

Page 58

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

Note: All conditions must be met in order for the mapping to be applied.

You can use transforms to generate a template ID. The example below inserts the value
‘Template’ after the name attribute to generate a new ID:

<Template>

<TemplateID Value="[name]" >

<Transforms>

<InsertAfter Pattern="^.*$" Value=" Template" />

</Transforms>

</TemplateID>

</Template>
You can use Lookups with or without transforms, to look up a value for the Template ID:

<Template Attribute="code" AttributePattern="^[a-z]{3}" >

<TemplateID Value="[code]" >

<Lookup Id="TemplateID Map">

<FailAction Action = "FixedValue" Value="[DateAndTime]"/>

</Lookup>
</TemplateID>

</Template>

7.8

Rendition Mapping

Rendition Mapping is used to configure the settings that will be used to determine which

document rendition needs to be fetched from the DMS server.

The document rendition to fetch is based upon the class assigned to the document in the

output. The following settings define the behaviour:

· RenditionType attribute determines the rendition metadata that needs to be

fetched from DMS server and written into the EIWM File.

· ExpectedRendition attribute is considered only when the RenditionType
attribute is set to either 'Download', 'Link' or 'Download + Link'. This

attribute is used to identify the rendition, either as the primary or if its a

secondary then by its file extension (MIME type).

Rendition mapping maintains a Lookup table. It defines the file formats present in the

DMS server and their corresponding file extensions. You can add new file formats and

their file extensions in this Lookup table. ExpectedRendition attribute uses this Lookup

table to identify the file format to be downloaded from the DMS server.

Release 5.0.10

Page 59

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

A class specifier may take a regular expression used to match specific formats of class

value by adding IsRegEx="true". The value of IsRegEx is false by default if the

attribute is not specified. You can configure Rendition mapping entries as shown in the

examples below:

· Rendition Type: Download (Download file to disk)

Example 1: <Add ClassID = "Equipment” Rendition Type = "Download"

ExpectedRendition =".zgl" />

Example 2: <Add ClassID = "Pipe"  RenditionType = "Download"

ExpectedRendition ="primary"  />

Example 3: <Add ClassID = "*"  RenditionType = "Download"   ExpectedRendition

 ="primary"  />

· Rendition Type: Download+LinkToPrimary (Download the expected file
rendition to disk and add a Link to the primary rendition in the EIWM.

Example 1: <Add ClassID = "*" RenditionType = "Download+LinkToPrimary"

ExpectedRendition = ".dwg" />

Example 2: <Add ClassID = "Equipment" RenditionType =

"Download+LinkToPrimary" ExpectedRendition = "primary" />

· Rendition Type: Link (Add a Link to the expected rendition in the EIWM)

Example 1: <Add ClassID = "*" RenditionType = "Link" ExpectedRendition

="primary" />

Example 2: <Add ClassID = "*" RenditionType = "Link " ExpectedRendition

=".dwg" />

· Rendition Type: Download+Link (Add a Link to the expected rendition in the

EIWM and Download the expected rendition file to disk)

Example 1: <Add ClassID = "*" RenditionType = "Download+ Link"

ExpectedRendition =".pdf" />

Example 2: <Add ClassID = "*" RenditionType = "Download+ Link"

ExpectedRendition =" primary " />

When you choose a RenditionType of Download+LinkToPrimary or

Download+Link, it allows the downloaded file to be processed for tags through

other Gateways such as AVEVA NET Document Indexing Gateway or AVEVA NET

Gateway Configuration Tool. This RenditionType allows you to import the tags

that are extracted through the other Gateways and link to the document into

AVEVA NET, without the need to store any copy of the document in AVEVA NET.

Release 5.0.10

Page 60

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

You can configure the Look up entries using the following example:

<! -- .docx Microsoft Office Word 2007 documents -->

Key indicates file extension and value indicates the CMIS Rendition Kind (see CMIS 1.0

specification for details).

<Entry>

<key>.docx</key>
<value>msw12</value>

</Entry>

When the Rendition Type is LinkToPrimary, a link is generated in the EIWM that permits

AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard to open the file directly from the DMS.

If RenditionType is set to Download, the files will be downloaded from the server to

the physical media.

After the download, these files can be processed by other gateways such as AVEVA NET

Gateway Configuration Tool or AVEVA NET Document Indexing Gateway and can be

directly imported to AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard as physical files. A .vnet file is

also generated that links the file to the object in the EIWM.

7.9

Incidental Classification

Incidental classification is often used to state the status of an object. You can define

Incidental classification as part of Class Mapping, as shown in the following example:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>

<ClassMapping>
<Classes>
................
</Classes>

<IncidentalClassifications>

<IncidentalClassification Class="PHYSICAL EQUIPMENT"
Attribute="SYSTEM_STATUS.TEXT" >

<ClassID Value="Deleted" />

</IncidentalClassification>
</IncidentalClassifications>
</ClassMapping>

Configuring an Incidental Classification:

Incidental classification determines the state of an object in AVEVA NET. Each mapping

entry in the configuration is examined in turn and the first entry to match is used to

generate the Incidental classification.

An example verifying the existence of an attribute in the source system:

<IncidentalClassification Attribute="SYSTEM_STATUS.TEXT"
AttributePattern="^[AZ]+$">

<ClassID Value="Deleted" />

</IncidentalClassification>

Release 5.0.10

Page 61

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Appendix A: Mapping Configuration

A class specifier may take a regular expression used to match specific formats of class

value by adding IsRegEx="true". The value of IsRegEx is set to false by default if the

attribute is not specified.

<IncidentalClassification Class="([A-Z])\w+" IsRegEx="true"
Attribute="SYSTEM_STATUS.TEXT" AttributePattern="^[A-Z]+$" >

<ClassID Value="Deleted" />

</IncidentalClassification>

You can also use Lookups and Transforms to obtain the Class ID.

Release 5.0.10

Page 62

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 8 - Appendix B: CMIS Query Language with Examples

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

8

Appendix B: CMIS Query Language with Examples

The following section discusses the CMIS Query Language and provides some related
examples.

8.1

Overview

The CMIS standard defines a query language based on the SQL-92 SELECT statement for

discovering objects that match specified criteria. To support this, a read-only relational

view of the CMIS data model is also defined.

The following are two parts of CMIS query language:

· Relational View model
· Query Language

8.1.1

Relational View

The relational view of a CMIS repository consists of a collection of virtual tables that are

defined on top of the CMIS data model. In this relational view a Virtual Table is implicitly

defined for each queryable Object-Type defined in the repository. In each Virtual Table, a

Virtual Column is implicitly defined for each property for each property defined or

inherited by the object-type.

The following are four base types of CMIS objects:

· A document (cmis:document) object represents a standalone information asset.
Document objects are the elementary entities managed by a CMIS repository.
· A folder (cmis:folder) object represents a logical container for a collection of
“file-able” objects, which include folder objects and document objects. Folder

objects are used to organise file-able objects.

· A relationship object represents an instance of directional relationship between

two objects. The support for relationship objects is optional.

· A policy object represents an administrative policy, which may be “applied” to

one or more “controllablePolicy” objects. Whether or not an object is controllable

is specified in its object-type definition. The support for policy objects is optional.

The object types cmis:document and cmis:folder are queryable in CMIS 1.0

implementation for Documentum and SharePoint. Other types cmis:relationship and

cmis:policy are not queryable.

Note: Content-streams are not exposed through this relational view, so cannot be

queried.

Release 5.0.10

Page 63

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 8 - Appendix B: CMIS Query Language with Examples

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

8.1.2

Query Structure

As with SQL-92, the CMIS Query is made up of the following:

· A mandatory SELECT clause
· A mandatory FROM clause
· An optional WHERE clause
· An optional ORDER BY clause

The CMIS query language does not support GROUP BY or HAVING.

SELECT Clause

The SELECT clause identifies which virtual columns to return in the result set. It can be

either a comma-separated list of one or more properties that are defined by queryable

object types or * for all virtual columns. All columns for an aliased table can be referred to

as ALIAS.*.

Simple SELECT examples:

· To select all properties for all documents:

SELECT * FROM cmis:document

· To select all properties for all folders using a table alias:

SELECT F.* FROM cmis:folder F

· To select specific properties for all documents:

SELECT cmis:name, cmis:objectId FROM cmis:document

· To select specific properties for all folders using a table alias and assigning aliases

for the selected columns:

SELECT F.cmis:name AS name, F.cmis:objectId AS id FROM cmis:folder
AS F

FROM Clause

The FROM clause determines the virtual table(s) to be used to fulfill the query and implies

which virtual columns are available in the SELECT, WHERE and ORDER BY clauses. If

more than one table is specified, table aliases must be used to avoid any ambiguity.

WHERE Clause

Only rows that satisfy all the constraints specified in the WHERE clause are returned from

the query.

CMIS Query supports the following SQL-92 predicates:
· Comparison predicate such as =, <>, <, >, <=, >=

SELECT * FROM cmis:document WHERE cmis:name = 'test'
SELECT * FROM cmis:document WHERE cmis:name <> 'test'
SELECT * FROM cmis:document WHERE cmis:creationDate =

Release 5.0.10

Page 64

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 8 - Appendix B: CMIS Query Language with Examples

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

TIMESTAMP '2010-03-31T12:52:12.123Z'
SELECT * FROM cmis:document WHERE cmis:creationDate <
TIMESTAMP '2010-04-01T00:00:00.000+00:00'

· IN predicate

SELECT cmis:name FROM cmis:document where cmis:name IN ('test',
'tube', 'Company Home')

· LIKE predicate

SELECT cmis:name FROM cmis:document where cmis:name like ‘% .pdf'
SELECT cmis:name FROM cmis:document WHERE cmis:name LIKE
'T__t.txt'
Note: LIKE predicate is not supported in CMIS 1.0 implementation of SharePoint

2010/2013.
· NULL predicate

SELECT * from cmis:document where cmis:name IS NULL
SELECT * from cmis:document where cmis:name IS NOT NULL

· Others

Folder predicate is supported by two predicate functions IN_FOLDER() and

IN_TREE() which have the same arguments. The folder ID must be the id of a

folder in the repository. IN_FOLDER() matches the immediate children of a

folder, IN_TREE matches any object below the folder.

SELECT cmis:name FROM cmis:document WHERE IN_FOLDER('folder
id')
SELECT cmis:name FROM cmis:folder WHERE IN_TREE('folder id')

Additional Functionality Supported by the Gateway:

CMIS query supports IN_FOLDER() and IN_TREE() functions which require folder ID as

an argument. As it is difficult to get the folder ID from the DMS server, the Gateway

supports an option to provide folder path in the CMIS query instead of folder ID by

prefixing "-f:", for example:

SELECT * FROM cmis:document WHERE IN_TREE('-f:/CMISTestRepo/Test')

The folder path is replaced with its respective folder ID internally before making a request

to the DMS server.

Note: A warning is logged if the folder path is invalid.

ORDER BY Clause

The ORDER BY clause is a comma-separated list of one or more column names. Columns

can be referred to directly using their queryName or using their alias as defined in the

query. All the properties specified must be orderable. Only columns that are in the

SELECT clause may be used for ordering.

Note: Collation for properties and property types are repository-specific. You should not

rely on this for portability.

Release 5.0.10

Page 65

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 8 - Appendix B: CMIS Query Language with Examples

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

8.2

CMIS Query Limitations in OpenText Content Server

As OpenText implementation of searching documents by CMIS Query is based on the

OpenText full-text search engine, it has the following CMIS Query restrictions:

· JOIN is not supported.
· ORDER BY is not supported.
· The decimal data type is not supported.
· The query to retrieve a document with its cmis:objectId value

default/V59055/59719 should be SELECT * FROM cmis:document where

cmis:objectId = 59055

Example: Consider two revisions (V1.0 and V1.1) of a document are added to

OpenText repository. cmis:objectId for the document V1.0 is

default/V59055/59055 and cmis:objectId for the document V1.1 is

default/V59055/59719.

Then the query SELECT * FROM cmis:document where cmis:objectId =

59055

· With All Revisions option selected fetches both the document revisions

(V1.0 and V1.1).

· With Latest version option selected fetches only the latest revision (V1.1).

· The DateTime data type is supported to a limited extent:

· Searching for a DateTime value performs the search only for the date part;

the time part is ignored.

· While filtering objects using a DateTime value as criteria, the DateTime

value should have a TIMESTAMP prefix to it, as mentioned below:

SELECT * FROM cmis:document WHERE (cmis:lastModificationDate
>= TIMESTAMP'2016-12-01T00:00:00.000Z') AND
(cmis:lastModificationDate <= TIMESTAMP'2016-12-
13T00:00:00.000Z')

· The LIKE operator acts on the words of a string value rather than on the

complete value.

· The operator <> for string values does not produce the complement of the

=operator. Instead, it behaves like a NOT CONTAINS.

Example: cmis:name <> 'TestDoc' selects all objects whose names do not

contain the word TestDoc.

Release 5.0.10

Page 66

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 8 - Appendix B: CMIS Query Language with Examples

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

8.3

CMIS Query Limitations in SharePoint Server

SharePoint implementation of searching documents by CMIS Query has the following

CMIS Query restrictions:

· Querying documents using the LIKE-predicate is not supported.

Refer https://social.technet.microsoft.com/Forums/sharepoint/en-US/1429000d-

fa87-4ce0-948f-235c2630b926/does-sharepoint-2013-support-contains-and-like-

for-cmis-query-statement?forum=sharepointgeneral for more information.
· Querying against cmis:objectId and cmis:versionSeriesId is not supported.

Refer https://social.technet.microsoft.com/Forums/en-US/43e65c1f-0d96-4853-

bfa0-e49601d6f113/support-for-querying-against-cmisobjectid-and-

cmisversionseriesid-properties?forum=sharepointgeneralprevious for more

information.

· JOIN is not supported in CMIS query as stated in

https://dev.office.com/sharepoint/docs/general-development/content-

management-interoperability-services-cmis-in-sharepoint

· When using IN_FOLDER command to download documents from a specific

folder, it only downloads 2000 files at a time. Refer to the below links for more

information.
· https://social.technet.microsoft.com/Forums/office/en-US/bdd53e37-7be3-

4718-8ed4-2aa57ef76d73/document-count-limit-under-folders?

forum=sharepointgeneral

· https://social.technet.microsoft.com/Forums/office/en-US/07aa7bcf-6201-

4871-981a-00ee944e97b3/2000-item-limit-in-a-list?

forum=sharepointgenerallegacy

Release 5.0.10

Page 67

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 8 - Appendix B: CMIS Query Language with Examples

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

8.4

CMIS Query Limitations in Documentum Content Server

While filtering objects using a DateTime value in criteria, if the DateTime value has a

TIMESTAMP prefixed to it, the Documentum server accepts the following DateTime

format:

"yyyy-dd-MMThh:mm:ss.sss{Z | +hh:mm | -hh:mm}"

For example, correct query is:

SELECT * FROM cmis:document WHERE (cmis:lastModificationDate

>= TIMESTAMP'2018-01-01T00:00:00.000Z') AND (cmis:lastModificationDate

<= TIMESTAMP'2018-31-01T00:00:00.000Z')

Example of an incorrect query (valid range for month is 01 to 12 not 13, highlighted

below):

SELECT * FROM cmis:document WHERE (cmis:lastModificationDate >=
TIMESTAMP'2018-12-01T00:00:00.000Z') AND (cmis:lastModificationDate <=
TIMESTAMP'2018-12-13T00:00:00.000Z')

Release 5.0.10

Page 68

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 9 - Appendix C: Creating Custom Output Folder Structure

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

9

Appendix C: Creating Custom Output Folder Structure

You can customise the Gateway output folder structure by using the Custom output

folder structure functionality.

 Customise the Output Folder Structure:

To create a custom output folder structure:

1. Navigate to the settings in the Project Settings, Output Settings, Custom

output folder structure.

Alternatively, you can modify the project settings file to customise the output folder

structure:

<CustomFolderStructure>true</CustomFolderStructure>
<FolderHierarchy>Document\[eiwm:id]\[Date{dd_MMMM_yy}]
\[DateAndTime]\[Time{hh.mm.ss}]\[eiwm:Context]</FolderHierarchy>

To customise the output folder structure in the Gateway, you need to set attributes or

static values in the Folder Hierarchy box as displayed in the above image. These attributes

are then resolved in real time for each EIWM object.

Release 5.0.10

Page 69

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 9 - Appendix C: Creating Custom Output Folder Structure

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

Expected Values:

The expected values in an output folder hierarchy are of three types:

· Attributes
· System attribute
· Static values

Conditions to be Fulfilled:

The following are the conditions to be fulfilled before you create the custom output folder

structure:

· Every new entry in the folder hierarchy must be delimited by a forward slash "\"

which marks the starting of a new value.

· Every "\" in the folder hierarchy is treated as a delimiter and is resolved as a

subfolder.

· Empty values are skipped.
· The values that are not resolved are skipped and a warning is logged.
· Characters such as  / : * ? " < > |  are not allowed and are skipped in the resolved

attribute values or in the static values and a warning is logged.

· Attributes that need to be resolved must be written within square brackets, for

example, [cmis:name], [eiwm:id], [DateAndTiime] and so on.

Attributes:

Attributes provided by the server and mapped to attributes in the EIWM file are resolved

in real time to get their values.

The following are the two types of attributes:

· CMIS Attributes: Defines the server-defined properties of a CMIS object, for

example, [cmis:objectId], [cmis:name] and so on.

· EIWM Attributes: Defines the values mapped to EIWM objects.

The following are the types of EIWM attributes:

· [eiwm:Id]: Gets the value from the Object Id mapping for the current EIWM

object.

· [eiwm:Name]: Resolves to the value of mapping for current EIWM object's

Name. This attribute gets the value from attribute mapping for current EIWM

object.

Release 5.0.10

Page 70

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 9 - Appendix C: Creating Custom Output Folder Structure

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

· [eiwm:ClassId]: Resolves to the value of mapping for current EIWM object's

Class Id. This attribute gets the value from Class Id mapping for current EIWM

object.

· [eiwm:Context]: Gets the value from context mapping for current EIWM

object.

Note: Multi contexts are resolved as subfolders.

System Attributes:

The system attributes are associated with the current date and current time values:

· [Date]: Provides the value of the current date.

Default format for date is dd-MM-yyyy. This attribute provides a format string,

for example, [Date{d.MMM.yy}].

Note: The [Date] format can be from any of the formats as specified under

MSDN DateTime Formats.

· [Time]: Provides the value of the current time.

Default format for date is hh-mm-ss. This attribute provides a format string, for

example, [Time{H.mm}].

Note: The [Time] format can be from any of the formats as specified under

MSDN DateTime Formats.

· [DateAndTime]: Provides the value of the current date and time.

Default format for date is dd-MM-yyyy hh-mm-ss. This attribute provides a

format string, for example, [Date{d.MMM.yy H:mm}].

Note: The [DateAndTime] format can be from any of the formats as specified

under MSDN DateTime Formats.

Release 5.0.10

Page 71

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 9 - Appendix C: Creating Custom Output Folder Structure

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

Static Values:

The static values are unresolved and are written without any square brackets.

Examples:

A valid example for the output folder structure is as follows:

<FolderHierarchy>Document\[eiwm:id]\[Date{dd_MMMM_yy}]
\[DateAndTime]\[Time{hh.mm.ss}]\[eiwm:Context]</FolderHierarchy>

which resolves the values for each attribute and provides an output folder structure as

follows:

You can also group the attributes together to derive a folder name by combining multiple

attributes, as shown in the following example:

<FolderHierarchy>Document\[eiwm:id]-[eiwm:ClassId]\[Date{dd-MMMM-
yy}]</FolderHierarchy>

Attributes can be from the CMIS server:

<FolderHierarchy>Document\[cmis:name]</FolderHierarchy>

which will resolve in a folder hierarchy as:

which is based on the name property of the CMIS object in the server.

Release 5.0.10

Page 72

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 9 - Appendix C: Creating Custom Output Folder Structure

AVEVA NET Gateway for CMIS User Guide

Note: The default folder hierarchy uses a helper function [cmis:versionID] that is

resolved by the Gateway to the actual version parameters [cmis:versionSeriesId]-

[cmis:versionLabel]. You can use these specific version parameters to customise the

folder hierarchy fully.

Release 5.0.10

Page 73

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Welcome to AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher

Welcome to AVEVA™ Gateway Data
Publisher

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher is a back-end process tool that enables the users of AVEVA™ Gateways to
publish data to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management through AVEVA™ Ingestion Service. In order to do this,
AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher watches a pre-configured staging area to which an AVEVA Gateways user
publishes the data, picks the modified contents of the folder and publishes them to AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management through AVEVA™ Ingestion Service.

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher is capable of the following:

•  Configure an instance of the gateway data publisher for the required AVEVA Gateway.

•  Configure the instance according to your requirements.

•  Add an access token for each instance.

•  Specify the file extensions that need to be published.

•  Specify mappings for creating metadata.

•  Create metadata using the mappings specified in XLS, XLXS, and CSV files.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

What is New in AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher

List of Enhancements

This section lists all the enhancements of AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher 1.3 release.

•  Providing a free list of ingestion parameters so that any parameter can be set. The name and value for

Ingestion API allows the service to validate and respond exactly as it does when validating parameter values.

•  Extended the Gateway Data Publisher so it can read from a staging area and push to an API by having a

pluggable pattern for publishing, where specific connectors can be created for specific API's.

•  Gateway Data Publisher documentation is now available from the following location Gateways

Documentation, and from the start menu

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

Administrator Help

This document introduces AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher, and provides instructions on how to install and use
it. The guide also describes the components and capabilities of AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher.

Preface

This chapter provides an introduction to Administrator help and specifies the intended audience. The chapter
also provides information about product training and how to contact technical support.

Audience

This guide is intended for the administrators of AVEVA Gateways who want to have the data published to a Cloud
application using AVEVA™ Ingestion Service. This guide assumes that the readers have the knowledge of how to
use the AVEVA Gateways, and know how to edit JSON files.

Summary of Chapters

The following table contains a summary of the chapters in this guide.

Lists the system requirements and provides
instructions for installing AVEVA™ Gateway Data
Publisher
