Publisher

Product Training Information

For information about product training courses, see the Product Training section of AVEVA's website, or contact
the nearest AVEVA Regional Support Centre.

Technical Support

If you encounter software issues or have suggestions for software improvements, contact AVEVA Knowledge &
Support Center and register a support incident.

After you register a support incident, we will know your identity (from your customer ID) and the details of the

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 7

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

incident. We will endeavor to fix the software issue or consider the software improvement suggestion as soon as
possible and notify you about the status of the incident.

If you are an accredited user of the AVEVA products, you are entitled to use AVEVA Knowledge & Support Center
for which you should have valid login credentials. For any other queries, contact the nearest AVEVA Regional
Support Center.

Introduction

This chapter introduces AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher and describes its capabilities.

About AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher is a back-end process tool that enables the users of AVEVA Gateways to publish
data to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management through AVEVA™ Ingestion Service. In order to do this, AVEVA™
Gateway Data Publisher watches a pre-configured staging area to which an AVEVA Gateways user publishes the
data, picks the modified contents of the folder and publishes them to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
though AVEVA™ Ingestion Service.

By doing this, AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher enables you to connect your on-premise AVEVA application to
Cloud-based applications that are available through CONNECT. Thus, AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher enables
transfer of data across your organization.

Note: The AVEVA Gateway that is publishing the data to the staging area must generate the trigger.start file.
This file enables the AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher to automatically pick up the changes to the folder and all
its sub folders, and publish them to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management through AVEVA™ Ingestion Service.
For information about how to generate the trigger.start file, refer to the user guide of your gateway at the
installation location of your product.

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher can detect the trigger.start file in any sub folders of the location that is
designated as the staging area. It scans for the changes starting from the location where the trigger.start file
is available and and all its sub folders.

Depending on the number of AVEVA Gateways , you can configure any number of instances of AVEVA™ Gateway
Data Publisher and, as a result, can watch multiple staging area folders. However, one staging area can be
watched by only one instance of AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher.

After it is installed and configured, AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher runs in the background and no manual
interventions are required unless there are interruptions in its functioning. Starting version 1.1, AVEVA™
Gateway Data Publisher can be run as a Windows service. It can be managed through command line using
Windows Service commands and the GDP Service Manager tool that is available after installation of AVEVA™
Gateway Data Publisher.

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher is capable of adjusting to the changes in its configuration dynamically without
needing to be restarted. Information related to the functioning of AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher is logged to
the log files. A log file is created for AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher and for each of its instances.

Capabilities of AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher

You can perform the following actions through AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 8

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

•  Configure an instance of the gateway data publisher for the required AVEVA Gateway

•  Configure the instance according to your requirements

•  Add an access token for each instance

•  Specify the file extensions that need to be published

•  Specify mappings for creating metadata

•  Create metadata using the mappings specified in XLS, XLXS and CSV files

See the sections in Using AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher for information on how to use these capabilities.

•  Any parameter: From Gateway Data Publisher 1.3 onwards any predefined parameter can be passed while
publishing files and Gateway Data Publisher will adapt and set the values as per definition in the each
configuration.

•  Delete: Gateway Data Publisher will pass the action parameter file to delete the files in AIMA. For more

details refer Delete 3D Models

Installing AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher

This chapter provides the system requirements and instructions for installing AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher.
The chapter also describes the components/files are that available after installing the gateway data publisher.

System Requirements and Other Prerequisites

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher requires Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) and .NET
Runtime version 7.0.0 or later to operate. In addition, a valid access token generated using the correct account/
folder must be available for AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher to access the AVEVA™ Ingestion Service end point
URL. The access token must be made available through the GDP Service Manager tool or through the
aveva.gdp.service.exe command line tool , both available in the Start menu after the installation of the
AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher. For more information about how to add access tokens, see Adding Access
Tokens for AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher. For information about the GDP Service Manager tool, see Running
AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher as a Windows Service, and for information about the command line tool, see
Help Commands for AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher.

Note: The aveva.gdp.service.exe command line tool can be run only when the cmd window is opened with
administrator privileges.

From Gateway Data Publisher 1.3 onwards, .NET 8.0 is required to start the service.

The AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher does not require a license to operate.

Adding Access Tokens for AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher

Access tokens are an authentication mechanism adopted by AVEVA. An access token must be set for using an
instance of AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher. AVEVA™ Connect provides two types of access tokens.

•  Account Access Token: Though Account Level Token has the highest level permission, it will still restrict the

user from uploading the homepage and configuration files.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 9

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

•  Service Access Token:This type of access token can be used for uploading the data or configurations

depending on role which was assigned to it. Access is granted according to the following rules:

•  Service Access Token created with the “Data Pipeline User” role grants the user permission to upload all

files except the homepage and config files.

•  Service Access Token created with the “AIM Configuration” role grants the user permission to specifically

upload only the homepage and config files.

Starting version 1.2, AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher enables you to add multiple access tokens for use with
AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher and specify which token must be used for an instance.

To add multiple access tokens to AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher using the command line:

1.  Generate the token in CONNECT with the required folder/role.

2.  Run the RunGdpManagerAddToken.bat file as an administrator from the <installation location>\AVEVA

Gateway Data Publisher\Examples\Startup folder.

3.  On the command window that appears, paste the token you generated and press Enter.

4.  Specify a name for the token or press Enter to accept the name "default". The token is added with the name.

5.  Repeat Steps 1 through 4 for adding more access tokens.

Note: You can edit the name of an access token or delete an access token using commands. For full list of
commands for AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher, see Help Commands for AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher.

To add access tokens using GDP Service Manager:

1.  Generate the token in CONNECT with the required folder/role.

2.  Run the GDP Service Manager from the Start menu.

3.

In the Authentication area, click + to add an access token.

4.  For the Name box, provide a name for the token.

5.  For the Value box, paste the token generated from CONNECT.

6.  Click Save to save the token. The token is added.

7.  Repeat Steps 1 through 6 for adding more access tokens.

Note: You can edit the name of the token or delete a token using the Edit and Delete options on the GDP
Service Manager.

After adding the access token, it can be used for an instance of the AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher. For
information on how to use an access token for an instance, see Configuring AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher for
Your AVEVA Gateway.

If the existing token reaches expiry date, gets compromised, or is deleted by an CONNECT administrator, you
must specify a new access token. For information on how to generate an access token, refer to the
documentation of CONNECT.

Install AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher

To install the gateway data publisher:

1.  Double-click the AVEVAGatewayDataPublisher1.3.msi from your installation media. The AVEVA Gateway

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 10

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

Data Publisher Setup wizard is displayed.

2.  Click Next.

3.  On the End-User License Agreement page, review the agreement, select the I accept check box, and click

Next to proceed with the installation.

4.  On the Custom Setup page, verify the installation location of AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher or click

Browse and select a different location. Click Next to continue.

Note: A warning message is displayed if the minimum required version of .NET Runtime is not available. In
such cases, although the installation process completes successfully, you cannot run AVEVA™ Gateway Data
Publisher.

5.  On the Ready to Install AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher page, click Install. The installation process begins.

6.  After the process is complete, click Finish on the Completed the AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher Setup

Wizard page to exit the wizard.

Components of AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher

After the AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher is installed, the options GDP Documentation, GDP Service Manager,
Run Gdp Manager Example, Run Gdp Manager Add Token, and Run Gdp Manager Shutdown All are available in
the Start menu.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 11

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

Note: The corresponding .bat files are available in the installation location (<install location>\Examples\
Startup).

You can use the options in the following manner:

•  The GDP Documentation option enables you to open the Gateway Data Publisher user documentation on a

webpage.

•  The GDP Service Manager option enables you to run the AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher as a Windows
service. For more information about the GDP Service Manager tool, see Running AVEVA Gateway Data
Publisher as a Windows Service.

•  The Run Gdp Manager Example option enables you to run AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher as a command
line process where you can view live feedback. This is helpful with establishing stable configuration for all
instances before using AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher as a Windows service.

Note: Before you click the Run Gdp Manager Example option, you must make sure that you have completed
the activities mentioned in Before Using AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher.

•  The Run Gdp Manager Add Token option enables you to add the access tokens for AVEVA™ Gateway Data
Publisher. See Adding Access Tokens for AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher for information on how to set the
access token.

•  The Run Gdp Manager Shutdown All option enables you to close all instances of AVEVA™ Gateway Data

Publisher. Information about how to use this option is provided later in this section.

Running the AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher Example

On clicking the Run Gdp Manager Example option, the following consoles are displayed.

•  AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher <Release No>-<date> (Manager): This console is the main console of

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher. It displays if AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher is running and, if yes, how
many instances of it are currently running and the staging areas of each instance.Closing this console closes
all the instances of AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher. The following image provides an example for this
console.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 12

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

•  AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher <Release No>-<date>: <gateway instance, watched directory>: Based on

the number of instances of AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher, you can see more than one of this console. This
console displays the log details of the respective AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher instance. Closing this
console closes the AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher instance. The following image provides an example of
this console.

Note: The above consoles are not displayed if you are running the AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher as a
Windows service. In such cases, you must view the log file available at the logs location path to view the log
messages.

In addition, based on your configuration, few files are available at the installation location of AVEVA™ Gateway
Data Publisher (<install location>\Examples\Configuration). The following image shows an example of
these configuration files.

The files can be modified to suit your requirements. The following table describes the purpose of each file.

Closing All Instances of AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher

On clicking the Run Gdp Manager Shutdown All option, a .bat script is run that closes all the AVEVA™ Gateway
Data Publisher instances, which means that all processes related to AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher are stopped
or shut down.

To start the AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher processes again, you must run the Run Gdp Manager Example
option.

Using AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher

This chapter provides information for configuring and using AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher.

Before Using AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher

Before using AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher, you must make sure that the following tasks are performed.

•  The prerequisites mentioned in System Requirements and Other Prerequisites are met.

•  Access tokens are configured for each instance of the AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher.

•  The staging area for your AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher instance is defined. For information on how to set

the staging area, see Configuring AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher for Your AVEVA Gateway.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 14

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

•  The URL for publishing the data through AVEVA™ Ingestion Service is set in AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher.
For information on how to do this, see Configuring AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher for Your AVEVA Gateway.

Creating AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher Instances

The AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher instances for your AVEVA Gateway are created using the InitGdpCfg.json
file available at the installation location of AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher. A sample location is as follows:
C:\Program Files\AVEVA\AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher\Examples\Configuration

The following image provides an example for the contents of the InitGdpCfg.json file.

To create an instance of AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher for your AVEVA Gateway:

1.  Open the InitGdpCfg.json file from the following location: <installation location of the gateway

data publisher>\Examples\Configuration.

2.  Add an init command to the file in the syntax provided in the image above.

The init command must contain the following parameters:

•  instance-name - Name of the AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher instance.

•  logs-location-path - Location for the log files for the AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher.

If you specify this parameter, it overrides the default logs location for AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 15

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

and its instances.

The default logs location for a user account is C:\Users\{user.name}\AppData\Local\Gdp\Logs

The default logs location for the system account is C:\Windows\System32\config\systemprofile\
AppData\Local\Gdp\Logs

Log files can be accessed anytime while AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher and instances are running. The
format for the name of the log files is as follows: gdp-service_dd-mm-yyyy_HH-MM-SS_RndNo

•  config-file-path - Location of the configuration file for the instance.

•  watched-directory - The location that is currently watched by AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher.

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher can detect the trigger.start file in any of the sub folders of this
location. The changes are picked for publishing from the folder where the trigger.start file is located
and all its sub folders.

You can change the location of the watched directory by changing the location in the InitGdpCfg.json
file. After you make the changes and save the file, the instance is restarted.

•  staging-database-directory - (Optional) Location of the database directory for AVEVA™ Gateway
Data Publisher. If you want to store the database at a custom location, you need to add this new
parameter to the InitGdpCfg.json file as shown in the following image:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 16

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

By default, the staging area for the database directory is located at the same location as the watched-
directory but with the suffix .gdp.

You can dynamically add or remove instances by adding or removing the RunGDP commands to
InitGdpCfg.json file. After you make the desired changes and save the file, the instances are added or
removed accordingly.

Note: In addition, you must also create a file with the name specified in the Value section of the config-file-
path argument. For information on how to create this file and the contents of it, see Configuring AVEVA Gateway
Data Publisher for Your AVEVA Gateway.

You can also specify these settings through the GDP Service Manager tool. For information on how to use the
GDP Service Manager tool, see Running AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher as a Windows Service.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 17

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

Configuring AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher for Your AVEVA Gateway

All configurations for AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher for the required AVEVA Gateways are available in the
<GatewayName>ServiceConfig.json file available at the following location: <install location of the
gateway data publisher>\Examples\Configurations.

Note: If the location does not contain a file for any of your required AVEVA Gateways, then you must create a file
in the same syntax and then create an instance for it using the InitGdpCfg.json file. For information on how to
create an instance, see Creating AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher Instances.

The following image provides an example for an AVEVA™ E3D Design (Model) gateway.

Some Points about Rules

The following points about rules must be noted:

•  A rule is defined by the filter id and set of actions.

•  Each rule will run the defined actions if the filter passes the processed file.

•  The action defines what ingestion parameter should be set on processed file.

•  A filter is defined by its type, id and conditions. There is currently only one filter type available :

•  FileNameFilterCfg - it allows to use the ‘PassFiles’ condition, that can contain regular expressions to

pass the processed file by its name and extension.

Modifying Your Configurations

You can modify the <GatewayName>ServiceConfig.json file anytime, even when the AVEVA™ Gateway Data
Publisher is running. After you make the changes and save the file, the AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
automatically detects the changes and recompiles the rules according to the changes made, without having to be
restarted.

The changes are detected in the following sections: Logger, Tables, Rules, and Publisher. Changes in
StagingAreaWatcher section are not detected, hence they won't be applied.

Each time a section change is detected, a console message is displayed informing you about it. If a change is
made while the publishing process is in progress, AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher waits for the publishing
process to complete before using the updated configuration. If there is no publishing process running while
configuration section change is detected, the updated configuration is applied almost immediately.

The changes are not applied or discarded by the AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher in the following circumstances:

•  If the updated configuration contains a JSON schema error, the change is not detected and the old

configuration will be used.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 23

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

•  If the updated configuration contains a GDP schema error (for example, the entire Logger section is

missing), the old configuration setting will be used for this particular section. This means that other valid
configuration changes will be applied.

•  According to the point above, if the user removes the "Logger" section and restores it with the same settings
as before removal, the configuration change is not detected because the used settings did not change at all.

Specifying Filters

The FiltersConfig.json file is used to specify the file extensions that AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher needs
to consider for publishing the data. The filter can be specified using the following function: FileNameFilterCfg.

The syntax for the function is provided in the FiltersConfig.json file.

The following image provides an example for the FiltersConfig.json file.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 24

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 25

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

Only the files with the specified extensions are considered for publishing according to the filter Id specified on
the <GatewayName>ServiceConfig.json file. Other files are omitted.

Specifying Mappings for Metadata Attributes Using an Excel File

You can specify the mappings metadata attributes using an XLS, XLSX or a CSV file.

To define the mappings for metadata attributes using an XLS, XLSX or a CSV file:

1.  Create a file with the required data.

2.  Define the table name in the <GatewayName>ServiceConfig.json file under Processor > Tables. The

parameters required for defining the table are as follows:

•  Name: Name of the table

•  Path: Location of the file

•  Sheet: (Optional) Sheet name in the file

3.

In the Processor > Rules > Actions area in the the <GatewayName>ServiceConfig.json file, define the
mapping using the Map function.

The following image shows an example of how this can be done.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 26

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

Note: The first row in the file (XLS, XLSX or CSV) must have the column names.

The changes you make to the Excel file are automatically detected without having to restart AVEVA™ Gateway
Data Publisher. Make the changes you want and save the Excel file. The AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher detects
the changes and publishes the data accordingly. If the changes you make are invalid or break the mapping, then
the mapping specified in the previous version of the file are considered.

Running AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher as a Windows Service

Starting version 1.1, AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher is available as a Windows service.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 27

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

You can start AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher as a Windows service using the GDP Service Manager tool or
using the Windows commands.

The following image provides an overview of the tool.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 28

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

The following tasks can be performed from the GDP Service Manager tool:

•  Start/stop the Service

•  Set the following configuration arguments for the Service

Note: You must stop the the AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher Service to set the following arguments.

•  logs-location-path - Location for the log files for the instance.

•  config-file-path - Location of the configuration file for the instance.

•  watched-directory - The location that is currently watched by AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher.

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher can detect the trigger.start file in any of the sub folders of this
location. The changes are picked for publishing from the folder where the trigger.start file is located
and all its sub folders.

•  app-data-directory - (Optional) Location of the application data for the manager and instance. If you
want to store the application data at a custom location, you need to add this new argument. The default
location for application data is %LOCALAPPDATA%\GDP.

Important: If you have changed the location of the application data, you must make sure that an access
token is set once again before running the instance or the GDP Service Manager.

•  Set the token for AVEVA™ Ingestion Service. For information on how to set the access token using the GDP

Service Manager, see Adding Access Tokens for AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher.

Any errors while using the GDP Service Manager are logged to the Event Viewer.

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher also supports the following commands:

Command

sc create

sc delete

sc config

Example

sc create gdp-service binPath="\"C:\Program
Files\AVEVA\AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher\
Aveva.Gdp.Service.exe\"

sc delete gdp-service

sc config gdp-service binPath="\"C:\Program
Files\AVEVA\AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher\
Aveva.Gdp.Service.exe\" --config-filepath
\"Examples\Configuration\InitGdpCfg.json\"
--logs-location-path \"C:\Logs\GDP\""

Disaster Recovery in AVEVA Gateway Data Publisher

In case of a network failure, AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher resumes the publishing activity from where it was
interrupted. For example, if the publishing activity was interrupted while publishing 200 files of 500 files,
AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher goes into the stand-by mode until the network is restored. Then, AVEVA™
Gateway Data Publisher uses the transaction ID of the publishing job and resumes the activity for the remaining
files. The files published before the network failure are not republished.

Note: Access tokens are verified once again to complete the publishing activity.

Security Guidelines

The security recommendations for the administrator of AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher are as follows:

•  Use the principle of least privilege

•  For the users of AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher: Grant Read/Write access to the folders (staging areas)
that you have configured for watching by the AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher instances. Grant Read/
Write access fo the AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher logs folder.

•  For the users of AVEVA Gateways: Change the access to Read/Write only to the specific files and folders
the gateway needs to modify. For example, you can grant Read/Write access to the Staging Area folders.
Grant ReadOnly access to the AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher logs folder.

•  For AVEVA™ Ingestion Service: Grant Read/Write access to target folder (Asset Id) for the specific

CONNECT account user.

•  Please ensure that the system does not have any specific rules implemented for blocking AVEVA™ Gateway

Data Publisher from connecting to AVEVA™ Ingestion Service.

Note: If the above security recommendations are not suitable for your environment, you must investigate
what is the most suitable approach for your environment and apply those practices.

When you run AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher as a Windows service for the first time, the service is started
using the Local Service account. For enhanced security, it is recommended to switch to a dedicated service
account that has required access privileges. To do this, open the Services window, and edit the Log On properties
of the service.

Troubleshooting

Unable to start AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher

If AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher cannot start, it may be because the machine on which it is installed contains
two versions of .NET (x86 and x64). AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher requires the x64 version.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 34

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

To resolve this issue:

1.  Open Edit environment variables from the Start menu.

2.  On the Advanced tab, click Environment Variables.

3.  Find the Path variable in the System variables group and click Edit.

4.  Check if C:\Program Files (x86)\dotnet\ is on the list. If it is, make sure that C:\Program Files\

dotnet\ path is before it.

5.  Try to run AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher again.

Unable to run aveva.gdp.service.exe

If you are unable to run the aveva.gdp.service.exe file in the command prompt, make sure that the
command prompt (cmd window) is opened using the administrator privileges.

AVEVA Ingestion Service API Request Parameters

For orchestration to understand how a particular file will be processed, a set of pre-defined parameters must be
passed from the API. These parameters are passed independently for each file in the API calls. The set of
parameters in an API call either depends on the type of the file or the processing required for the file.

The following are the API parameters that are currently supported by the AIM Ingestion/Orchestration system:

•  target

•  source

•  publishOriginal

•  publishRendition

•  publishReference

•  documentIdentifier

•  context

•  fileName

•  registerIdentifier

•  fileRevisionKey

•  configId

•  Action

Parameter Definitions

target

•  This API parameter specifies which processing unit needs to act on the current file and how it's orchestration

should be carried out.

•  Target is a mandatory API parameter and must be provided for any file.

•  The possible values for this parameter can be:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 35

Value

DE

AC2D

MS2D

AC3D

IFC

NAV

CL

Regi

Model

Custom

source

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

Description

For any 2D file that requires scraping using Data
Extractor Gateway.

For Example: PDF, DOC, DOCX, XLS, XLSX, PPT, and
PPTX.

For any 2D file that requires scraping and conversion
to a rendition using the AutoCAD Gateway.

For Example: DWG, and DXF.

For any 2D file that requires scraping and conversion
to a rendition using the Microstation Gateway.

For Example: DGN

For any AutoCAD 3D file that requires scraping and
conversion to a rendition using the 3D Gateway.

For Example: DWG, and DXF.

For any Industry Foundation Classes 3D file that
requires scraping and conversion to a rendition
using the 3D Gateway.

For Example: IFC

For any Navisworks 3D file that requires scraping
and conversion to a rendition using the 3D Gateway.

For Example: NWD

For an ISM class library. Only XML format for Class
Library is currently supported by Register GW.

For any 1D data file (register) that requires Regi GW
processing.

For Example: CSV, JSON, and XML

For any 3D model file that requires 3DVS processing.

The file is a custom Gateway pattern file, homepage,
or bootstrap XML that needs to be applied to the
AIM-A environment.

This is an optional parameter that denotes the actual source system where the file originated from.

publishOriginal

•  This is a Boolean flag that instructs the orchestration to process the input file as it is. The file will not be

converted into any rendition if the flag is set to true.

•  Default value is false.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 36

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

•  This is a required parameter for every 2D file. Only applicable to files that are supported by the
orchestration's Gateway capabilities. For 3D files, set to false by orchestration in all the cases.

publishRendition

•  This is a Boolean flag that instructs the orchestration to convert the input file into its rendition; for example,

DWG to SVG before publishing the rendition to the AIM-A system. The file will be converted into any
rendition if the flag is set to true.

•  Default value is false.

•  This is a required parameter for every 2D file. Only applicable to files that are supported by the
orchestration's Gateway capabilities. For 3D files, set to true by orchestration in all the cases.

publishReference

•  This is a Boolean flag that instructs the orchestration to scrape references (both tags and documents) from
the input file and publish these references to the AIM-A system. The file will be scraped if the flag is set to
true.

•  Default value is false.

•  This is a required parameter for every 2D and 3D file (that require processing via their respective gateways).

Only applicable to files that are supported by the orchestration's Gateway capabilities.

documentIdentifier

•  This is a unique identifier for the document object in the AIM-A UI. The input file will be associated to the

document object with this identifier.

•  This is a required parameter for every 2D file.

context

•  If provided, this parameter represents the sub-context under which the file object will be added. This would

represent a sub-system within an asset.

•  More information about how Context Mapping works in AIM-A can be found here.

fileName

•  The name of the file to be uploaded into the AIM-A system.

registerIdentifier

•  This is the ISM Class Library Register path to be used for data processing through Register Gateway.

•  Every 1D file requires some instructions on how the information must be translated to an EIWM object and
imported into AIM-A. RegisterIdentifier allows orchestration to look at the Class Library and find the rules it
needs to apply to transform a 1D register into meaningful EIWMs for import.

•  For example, if you InfoInterface hierarchy is System >> Register1, your registerIdentifier would be System/

Register1.

fileRevisionKey

•  This parameter helps orchestration maintain a consistent data model when more than one revisions of a file
are loaded from the API or the document is updated. To make sure that we only show the latest revision of
any file for any document, this value is used.

•  When provided, fileRevisionKey will override documentIdentifier value and add an extra (unclassified)

identifier for the document. Orchestration will then create an association between the document object and
the fileRevisionKey.

configId

•  This parameter is used to specify what type of customization is being performed by the target "Custom".

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 37

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

•  Any customization to target AIM environments can only be performed by tokens with the "AIM

Configuration" role assigned to them. This is an administrative task so cannot be performed by Account
access tokens or Service to service tokens. The role is available in the new Data Pipeline/Load reporting
solution in AVEVA Connect. Documentation on how to use and enable this solution can be found here.

•  This property is used in conjunction with the target Custom and the possible values are listed below.

•  This parameter is used exclusively for deleting 3D models.

•  For the 3D model deletion to function without error, two additional parameters must be specified:

•  fileName must be a CSV file (e.g., "fileName": "DeleteModels.csv")Known Types

•  target must be set to "Model".

•  The only accepted value for this parameter is:

•  delete: Exclusively for deleting 3D model files. Use lower case as this parameter is case sensitive.

•  More information about how 3D Model Deletion works, and the CSV file formatting, can be found here:

Ingestion API 3D Model Deletion

•  The orchestration has a fallback mechanism to decide the target for any input file if it is already added into

the system as a known type.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 38

AVEVA™ Gateway Data Publisher
Administrator Help

•  For any file, if target is not specified by mistake, known types have been configured to default a specific type
of processing. For example, if an upload process accidentally forgets to pass Target parameter for a pdf file,
the system will try to scrape and process the pdf file using DE gateway.

•  For certain file types, there can be multiple valid processing units. Out of these, one is configured by default
as a known type. If you want a different type of processing to be done for this file type, you need to make
sure the target parameter is accordingly passed.

•  For example, XLSX is a file type that may require Register GW or DE GW processing. It defaults to Register
GW, so if you want an XLSX file to be scraped using DE, you need to specify target as DE in the API request.

•  It is a recommended practice to always provide a target.

The following section provides sample sets of request parameters that must be provided for the specified file
for orchestration to successfully process it.

•  Custom Gateway Configs - AC3D

•  Custom Gateway Configs - IFC

•  Custom Gateway Configs - NAV

Video Files

Video files in .mp4, .mov, .avi, and .wmv formats must use the PublishOriginal flag for 2D Import. Other video
formats are not currently supported.

The below examples are the required values for video files:

•  publishOriginal = false

•  publishRendition = false

•  publishReference = true

When uploaded to AIM, the video formats will have different behaviors :

•  .mp4 files will render and can be playable within AIM

•  .avi, .mov, .wmv will download from AIM and can be playable in a separate program.

[{

"PublishOriginal":true,

"PublishRendition": false,

"PublishReference": false,

"DocumentIdentifier":"video",

"FileRevisionKey":"2669133",

"FileName":"large_video_file.mp4",

"RegisterIdentifier":"samplevideos/mp4"

}]

Page 7

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Welcome to AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises

Welcome to AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-
Premises

AVEVA BI Gateway On-Premises is a product that synthesizes data from across the plant, revealing relationships
that would be all but invisible otherwise. It models big data into manageable chunks, so that industrial teams can
track performance against the metrics that matter most – in time enough to make a difference. By providing a
single version of the truth and contextualizing data, AVEVA BI Gateway helps you make strategic sense out of
your production data.

To learn about BI Gateway, refer to the readme for the release, and review legal and copyright information.

Page 9

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
What's New in AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises?

What's New in AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-
Premises?

Last revision: 4/9/2024

About this ReadMe

This document contains important information about AVEVA BI Gateway 2021 Service Pack 1 (SP1). Review this
document for Product enhancements and new features, system requirements and compatibility, resolved issues
and known issues in this version, and the BI Gateway software documentation.

Release Summary

With the release of AVEVA BI Gateway, the following list of new features and enhancements are introduced.

•  Data Adapters:

•  BI Gateway now includes a PI System Asset Framework data adapter.

•  BI Gateway now supports additional enhancements for the AVEVA Insight data adapter.

•  Enhanced Model Builder user experience.

•  Roll-out of resolved issues including performance improvements since the AVEVA BI Gateway 2021 release

and/or reported on AVEVA Insight BI Gateway (Cloud offering).

•  Browser support: You can now access AVEVA BI Gateway using the Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, and

Mozilla Firefox browsers.

To access the AVEVA BI Gateway Model Builder using the Firefox browser, you have to make some changes in
the browser settings. For more information, refer to the BI Gateway Installation Guide.

•  Upgrade support: Possible to upgrade from Intelligence 2017 Update 1 and AVEVA BI Gateway 2021 to

AVEVA BI Gateway 2021 SP1.

•  AVEVA BI Gateway Analytics Client 2021.4.2 (OEM version of Tableau Desktop) is bundled along with the

installation package.

Note: AVEVA BI Gateway Dashboard Server can be downloaded from AVEVA Knowledge and Support Center.
For more information on the installation process, refer to the Deploy section of the Tableau Help.

•  AVEVA BI Gateway Dashboard Server 2021.4.2 supports the following Connectors.

•  Microsoft Analysis Services (MSAS)

•  Microsoft Azure SQL Data Warehouse

•  Microsoft Excel

•  Microsoft SQL Server

•  OData

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 10

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
What's New in AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises?

•  Spatial Data

•  Tableau Data Extracts

•  Text

Limitations for this release:

•  Due to the Apache log4j2 vulnerability, only the BI Gateway Dashboard Server 2021.4.2 and later versions

are supported.

•  The concurrent licensing model for BI Gateway Dashboard Server is available only for the existing

Intelligence/BI Gateway customers. For all new installs of BI Gateway, the concurrent licensing model for BI
Gateway Dashboard Server is not supported.

System Requirements and Product Compatibility

For important information about AVEVA BI Gateway 2021 SP1 product release, see the Technology Matrix for this
release. Information on the AVEVA Support site includes product requirements and compatibility, and system
requirements to install the BI Gateway software. You need to have valid credentials to access the site. You can
search for the information by product name and the release/version.

In addition, the system requirements are also available in the BI Gateway Installation Guide.

Licensing

AVEVA BI Gateway Server Licensing

This release of BI Gateway enforces licensing through the AVEVA Enterprise Licensing system for the BI Gateway
Server add-in.

For more information regarding Licensing, see the AVEVA Enterprise License Server Guide available at the
following location in the system where AVEVA Enterprise Licensing is installed: All Programs > AVEVA >
Enterprise License Manager User Guide.

AVEVA BI Gateway Dashboard Server and Analytics Client Licensing (Tableau Components)

The BI Gateway Analytics Client versions shipped with BI Gateway are allowed to access data only from the BI
Gateway datastore and data sources used in the BI Gateway model (Drill-down scenario, for example). Using the
Analytics Client outside of those boundaries is a violation of the license agreement.

The BI Gateway software enforces licensing for the Analytics Client through license keys provided by AVEVA on
your license certificate. When you first start the Analytics Client, a software activation window prompts you to
enter the license key.

The BI Gateway Dashboard Server is downloadable from AVEVA Knowledge and Support Center. For more
information on the installation process, refer to the Deploy section of the Tableau Help. You can activate the
Dashboard Server License through the Tableau Service Manager (TSM) portal.

NOTE:
- If you already have permanent activation codes for Tableau software, you can use the same activation codes

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Software Documentation

The AVEVA BI Gateway software documentation set includes the following:

•  BI Gateway Installation Guide - Provides information about installing and configuring the BI Gateway

software features.

•  BI Gateway Web Configuration Guide - Provides information about configuring and mapping various data

sources; managing BI Gateway objects such as dimensions, measures, data sources, and their deployment;
and security.

•  BI Gateway Event API Guide - Provides information about using the BI Gateway Event API.

•  BI Gateway Analytics Client Data Source Generator Guide - Provides information about using the Analytics
Client Data Source Generator to generate Analytics Client Data Source files compatible with BI Gateway
Analytics Client. These files are also known as Tableau Data Source (.tds) files.

•  ReadMe (This document) - Provides information about software and hardware requirements, known issues,

licensing, and documentation.

Requirements and Recommendations

Accessing the documentation

•  The BI Gateway software installation instructions are located in the root folder of the BI Gateway software
installation media as a PDF file. You can view these instructions by clicking the View Install Guide button as
you start the BI Gateway software installation procedure.

•  After installing AVEVA BI Gateway, you can access the BI Gateway software documentation as follows:

About AVEVA BI Gateway

Get Started

Activating License for BI Gateway Model Builder

About AVEVA BI Gateway

About AVEVA BI Gateway

About AVEVA BI Gateway Model

Basic Concepts

About AVEVA BI Gateway

AVEVA BI Gateway is a web application, which allows you to build a BI Gateway Model by creating data sources,
dimensions and measures, and configuring each of those objects. The application also displays the relationships
between the objects using a dynamic and interactive graphical representation of all the objects in the model. You
can export the BI Gateway model to a file; similarly, you can import a model from a previously generated model
file. Finally, you can deploy your BI Gateway model so that the Service starts executing your configured model to
save data into the BI Gateway Datastore.

About AVEVA BI Gateway Model

A BI Gateway model is a formal representation of the Data Sources, Dimensions, and Measures that are
collectively called BI Gateway "objects." The BI Gateway model also represents the relationships and
configurations of the BI Gateway objects.

A BI Gateway model is independent of any database schema and is optimized for manufacturing intelligence. The
BI Gateway model:

•  Consists of three types of objects: Data Source, Dimension, and Measure.

•  Contains measures typically representing quantitative aggregate operational data.

•  Contains dimensions typically representing context for the measures.

•  Defines relationships between the dimensions and the aggregated measures and how they map to the data

sources.

You can create and maintain a BI Gateway Model with the BI Gateway Model Builder. This is a web application
that allows you to build a BI Gateway Model by creating data sources, dimensions and measures, and configuring
each of those objects. Model Builder also displays the relationships between the objects using a dynamic and

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 15

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Get Started with AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises

interactive graphical representation of all the objects in the model. You can export the BI Gateway model to a
file; similarly, you can import a model from a previously generated model file. Finally, you can deploy your BI
Gateway model so that the Intelligence Service starts executing your configured model to save data into the BI
Gateway Data Store.

NOTE: The BI Gateway Model Builder replaces the use of the IDE objects and eliminates the need to use
ArchestrA IDE.

The BI Gateway model defines the dimensions and measures from one or more data sources as per mapping
rules.

For more information about the Data Source, see "Data Sources".

For more information about the Dimensions, see "Dimensions".

For more information about the Measures, see "Measures".

Basic Concepts

BI Gateway Model includes the following basic concepts:

Backfill

While configuring a refresh rate for a measure, you can provide a start date and time to start collecting data from
the data source and populate the measure. You can provide a start date and time prior to the measure
deployment date. The process of populating the measure for a period prior to the deployment date is called as
back-filling.

BI Gateway model

A BI Gateway model is a formal representation of the data sources, dimensions, and measures that are
collectively called BI Gateway objects. The BI Gateway model also represents the relationships and configurations
of the BI Gateway objects.

Context

A context is established by relating a measure to a dimension. A context link provides a context for time-slicing.
Time-slices obtained from time defining dimensions can have an associated context.

Dimension

A dimension is a structure that provides context to measures. Each dimension stores related items of data. A
dimension stores contextual data. Dimension allows you to filter, group, and label measure data.

Data source

Data sources are the external source systems to which you can map the dimensions and measures in the BI
Gateway model. Data sources are the BI Gateway objects in the BI Gateway model. You configure the data source
objects to provide connection information to the external data source systems.

Measure

A measure is a group of calculations that shares the same context. A measure consists of calculations for each
source system, time period, and for each unique combination of dimension value.

Starting the BI Gateway Model Builder

Logging Out of the BI Gateway Model Builder

Navigating the BI Gateway Model Builder Application

The BI Gateway Model Builder application page is automatically created after installing the BI Gateway Server
feature and after completing the post-installation configuration. You can access the BI Gateway Model Builder by
typing the URL. The URL for the BI Gateway Model Builder is:

https://<hostname>: <port number>

where

<hostname> is the name of the web server on which BI Gateway Server is installed.

<port number> is the port number previously configured after installation for the Model Builder.

For example: https://localhost:61075. Here, 61075 is the default port number.

Access to BI Gateway Model Builder is granted to authorized users only based on their Windows credentials.

If you have not activated the BI Gateway Model Builder license, the Deployment version of BI Gateway Model
Builder appears. You cannot create any objects in the Deployment version. In the Deployment version mode, you
can modify only the connection details and data adapter details (in data source). To activate the license, refer to
Activating License for BI Gateway Model Builder section.

NOTE: You can also access the BI Gateway Model Builder remotely from another computer. For more
information, refer to Accessing BI Gateway Model Builder from a Remote Computer.

Enabling Firefox Compatibility Settings

You must make the following changes in your Firefox browser settings so that there is a smooth and continuous
access to the BI Gateway Model Builder.

If you are using Firefox, do the following:

1.  Open Firefox and type about:config in the address bar. A warning message appears.

2.  Click the I accept the risk button.

3.  Use the Filter field to search for the ‘security.enterprise_roots.enabled' configuration parameter.

4.  Double-click the name of the configuration parameter and update the value from false to true.

5.  Next, you must add Exceptions to the Security Certificates. To do this:

a.  Click the Menu button and then click Options.

b.

In the Options page, click Privacy and Security on the left pane.

c.  Under Certificates, click View Certificates.

d.

In the Certificate Manager dialog box, click the Servers tab.

e.  Click Add Exception. The Add Security Exception dialog box appears.

f.  Add the URL to access the BI Gateway Model API.

For example, https://localhost:61076

g.  Add the URL to access the BI Gateway Model Builder.

For example, https://localhost:61075

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 17

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Get Started with AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises

h.  Click Confirm Security Exception.

You can now access the BI Gateway Model Builder using the Firefox browser.

Adding Users to the aaIntelligenceConfigAdmin Group

You can access the BI Gateway Model Builder only when you are part of the Windows group
"aaIntelligenceConfigAdmin" that allows the user read-write access to the BI Gateway Model Builder application.
Authorized users will see their Windows user name appear at the top right of BI Gateway. If you are not
authorized to access BI Gateway, you will not see a user name at the top right, and Model Explorer will remain
blank. You can add and grant access to the user by adding them to the Windows groups manually.

NOTE: Make sure you restart the computer after you add any user to the "aaIntelligenceConfigAdmin" Windows
group.

To add users to the aaIntelligenceConfigAdmin group:

1.  Open Service Manager > Configuration > Local Users and Groups > Groups.

2.  Click Groups. A list of group names and descriptions appears.

3.  Click aaIntelligenceConfigAdmin. The aaIntelligenceConfigAdmin Properties dialog box appears.

4.  Click Add. The Select Users dialog box appears.

5.

In Enter the object names to select, type the name of the user, group, or computer that you want to add to
the group, and then click OK.

Starting the BI Gateway Model Builder

AVEVA BI Gateway is used to visually configure information models that are multidimensional data models to
represent business logic and relationship between various, unstructured source data items. The explorer view of
the BI Gateway Model Builder enables multidimensional data model visualization in a compact, single screen
format, depicting all of the many-to-many, dynamic relationships between different types of objects. In the
explorer view of the BI Gateway Model Builder, you can view all the objects (Data Sources, Dimensions, and
Measures) of a model in a single view, and also the relationships that they share. For example, if you click a data
source in the BI Gateway Model, you can see the Linked Dimensions and Measures that are being used for the
selected data source. You can also view the dynamics and the linking between objects of the same type. For
example, if one of the Dimension is leveraging another Dimension, you can see the linking when you click the
Dimensions.

The explorer view also allows you to view the deployment status of the various objects in the Model using the
three different pre-defined color codes as displayed in the Legends of the application.

The full model visualization with all the rich context greatly aids in authoring of the Information model and
creation of business dashboards showing key performance indicators based on the Models.

The differentiating details of the BI Gateway Model Builder include:

•  A single place to clearly and fully visualize the entire Information model

•  All relationship between objects (many-to-many) fully captured and rendered

•  A single object in context, showing all dependencies within the full model

Logging Out of the BI Gateway Model Builder

You can log off from BI Gateway by closing the Browser. This will close the session. Make sure that you save your
work before closing the Browser.

Activating License for BI Gateway Model Builder

The BI Gateway product uses the AVEVA Enterprise Licensing component and activation. You must acquire and
activate a valid license to work seamlessly with the BI Gateway Model Builder. You can acquire license based on
your requirement. You must activate the license using the AVEVA Enterprise License Manager before you actually
start building your BI Gateway Model.

NOTE: License is enforced for the Model Builder and Data Source count. If a Model Builder feature license is not
acquired, the model builder will start in Deployment version mode. In the case of Data Source count, the system
will prevent deployment of additional data sources when the data source count license limit is reached.

If not already installed on a node on the network, install and configure the AVEVA Enterprise License Manager
and License Manager. The AVEVA Enterprise License Server and License Manager installation is available as a
component of the BI Gateway installation.

Once installed, for information about how to activate the license, refer to the AVEVA Enterprise Licensing
documentation available in .\Program Files (x86)\Common Files\ArchestrA\Licensing Framework\License
Manager.

To activate the BI Gateway Model Builder licenses

1.  Make sure you have already configured the AVEVA Enterprise License Manager component. If not, use the
post-installation Configurator tool to configure it. For more information, refer to the Configurator Utility

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 21

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Get Started with AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises

section of the BI Gateway Installation Guide.

2.  Use the AVEVA Enterprise License Manager to activate your BI Gateway Model Builder licenses.

The AVEVA Enterprise License Manager also allows you to:

•  View the BI Gateway Model Builder license usage.

•  For an environment in which BI Gateway Model Builder is being used on multiple devices, you can reserve

licenses for a specific BI Gateway Model Builder computer.

•  Deactivate existing licenses.

The following topics cover general information for performing these tasks. For more detailed information about
using the License Manager, see the AVEVA Enterprise License Manager Guide and the online help.

Activating the Product Licenses

To activate the BI Gateway Model Builder license

1.  Do one of the following:

•  On the Start menu, click the AVEVA Enterprise License Manager tile.

•  In a web browser, enter the URL https://localhost/AELicenseManager.

The AVEVA Enterprise License Manager opens and the following License Server tile appears.

2.  Click the License Server tile.

The server's license page appears with the License Summary tab selected.

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Data Sources

Data Sources

The BI Gateway Server retrieves data from external data sources and provides this data to the reporting, analysis,
and other visualization tools at the enterprise level.

Data sources are the external source systems to which you can map the dimensions and measures fields in the BI
Gateway model. At run time, the data from the external source systems populates the dimensions and measures
as per the mappings.

Data Sources can include the following:

•  AVEVA™ Historian

•  AVEVA™ Enterprise Data Management

•  AVEVA™ Insight

•  AVEVA Insight Historian

•  AVEVA Insight MES

•  Microsoft SQL Server Database and Microsoft Azure SQL Server Database

•  PI System

•  PI System OLEDB Provider

•  PI System Asset Framework

•  Oracle Database

•  Text File

You must configure the Data Source objects to provide connection information to the external data source
systems. Only deployed data sources are available at run time to the dependent dimensions and measures for
data collection.

The connection information for a data source varies depending on the data source type. The connection
information is stored in an encrypted format so your credentials are protected by the BI Gateway software.

Data adapters are plug-in components that provide connectivity and retrieval from the data sources.

Managing Data Sources

You can manage a data source using the Data Source editor.

The Data Source editor allows you to:

•  Add a Data Source

•  Edit a Data Source

•  Deploy and Undeploy a Data Source

•  Delete a Data Source

•  Add Custom Source Data Items using Custom Queries

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Adding a Data Source

You can add a data source that allows you to map the dimensions and measures fields in the BI Gateway model.

To add a data source

1.  Open the BI Gateway Model Builder.

2.  Click the Add (

) icon. A
new data source is added in the BI Gateway model with a default name and the Data Source editor appears
in the right pane.

) icon in the Floating Action Buttons menu and then click the Data Source (

3.  Click the General tab, and then do the following:

a.  In the Name box, type a new name for the data source. The data source name must be unique and can

have a maximum length of 32 characters.

b.

In the Description box, type a description for the data source. The description must be less than 1024
characters.

4.

In the Data Adapter Service area, do the following:

a.  In the Server name box, type the name of the server where the BI Gateway Data Adapter service is

installed and configured.

b.

c.

In the DataAdapter Type box, select the required data adapter.

5.

In the Data Source Configuration section, configure the settings for the required data source. For more

NOTE: The data source configuration options may vary depending of the DataAdapter type selected.

minutes to retrieve the schema from the data source.

7.  Click Done and then click Save.

Data Sources

Configuring the Microsoft SQL Server Database Connection

You can configure the native SQL Server database name to connect to a Microsoft SQL Server database and
retrieve the database details.

You must provide the native SQL Server database name to configure the connection information for the
Microsoft SQL Server database.

To configure the Microsoft SQL Server connection

1.

In the Data Adapter Type box, select Microsoft SQL Server Database.

2.

In the Data Source Configuration section, do the following:

a.  In the Data Source time zone list, click the time zone of the database that the external data source

system is referencing.

The default time zone is the time zone of your computer. You can select the (UTC) Coordinated Universal
Time option if the external data source system being referenced uses UTC for its time fields.
You can click the Automatically adjust time for Daylight Savings check box if the time zone that you
select is affected by daylight saving time (DST). For more information about time zone handling, see Time
Zone for a Data Source.

b.

In the Server name box, type the name of the server where the SQL Server database is installed and
configured.

c.

In the Database name box, type the database name that you want to connect.

d.  Select the authentication mode.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 28

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Data Sources

a.  By default, the authentication mode shows the default Windows Authentication option and the user

name and password boxes are not available.

b.  Select the SQL Server authentication option if you want to connect using the SQL server credentials.

You must specify the user name and password for SQL Server authentication.

c.  Select the Azure Active Directory authentication option if you want to connect using the Azure
Active Directory credentials. You must specify the user name and password for the Azure Active
Directory authentication.

3.  Click Connect. If the connection succeeds, the Getting schema notification message appears. It can take few

minutes to retrieve the schema from the data source. For more information about retrieving the schema, see
Retrieve the schema.

4.  Click Done and then click Save.

Configuring the Text File Connection

The Text file connection information provides the functionality to retrieve the names of the available source data
items from a .csv data source file. The .csv file must have a valid header line. Be sure that the Microsoft Office
2007 Data Connectivity components (Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0 Provider) are installed on the BI Gateway data
adapter computer to connect to or retrieve the schema depending on the Operating System. The Microsoft
Office 2016 data connectivity components are generally installed with Microsoft Office 2016, but can also be
installed separately using the installation program available at:

•  For a 32 -bit system, click the link to download.

•  For a 64-bit system, click the link to download.

You must provide the .csv directory name to configure the connection information for the .csv data source file.

A schema.ini file can be used to specify how the text is formatted. The delimiter character specified in this file
can be different from the default decimal symbol of the operating system. For example, on a US-English
operating system, the default decimal symbol is a dot (.). On French and German operating systems, among
others, the default decimal separator is a comma (,). Therefore, on such a system, using a format such as .csv and
Delimited (,) in the schema.ini file requires a different decimal separator to be specified. You can do this by
adding an entry "DecimalSymbol" to the schema.ini file. The format of this schema.ini file is documented at:

http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms709353(VS.85).aspx

For example:

Format=CSVDelimited
DecimalSymbol=,

For more information about the schema.ini file, see the Microsoft Office documentation for the
Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0 Provider.

To configure the Text File connection

1.

In the Data Adapter Type box, select Text File.

2.

In the Data Source Configuration section, do the following:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 29

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Data Sources

a.  In the Data Source time zone list, click the time zone of the database that the external data source

system is referencing.

The default time zone is the time zone of your computer. You can select the (UTC) Coordinated Universal
Time option if the external data source system being referenced uses UTC for its time fields.
You can click the Automatically adjust time for Daylight Savings check box if the time zone that you
select is affected by daylight saving time (DST). For more information about time zone handling, see Time
Zone for a Data Source.

b.

In the Directory box, type the path to a folder containing text files. You can type the directory in UNC or
local path format. The directory names should be without quotes, even if directory name contains
spaces, and it must be valid from the system hosting the Data Adapter Services.

c.

In the Extended Properties box, type the extended properties for the .csv file.

3.  Click Connect. If the connection succeeds, the Getting schema notification message appears. It can take few

minutes to retrieve the schema from the data source. For more information about retrieving the schema, see
Retrieve the schema.

4.  Click Done and then click Save.

Configuring the AVEVA Historian Connection

You can configure the AVEVA Historian data adapter to connect to the AVEVA Historian Server and retrieve the
names of the available source data items. In the context of this data adapter, a data item typically represents a
Tag name in the AVEVA Historian. The adapter also allows to define and validate custom queries, as well as to
retrieve the Historian tags meta data. The AVEVA Historian adapter retrieves the:

•  Fields and Types for the specified tag based on the tagname. It retrieves tag definitions for analog, string,

and discrete type tags.

•  Data for the specified tag depending on the filter string. The filter string can have single or multiple fields.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 30

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Data Sources

•  Tag data values for the specified calculation field in measure objects. The aggregates are valid for both

analog and discrete tag types.

•  Metadata for all tags. This is done through four source data items available through the Historian data

adapter. The four source data items are: All Tags, Analog Tags, Discrete Tags, and String Tags. These source
data items are available in the BI Gateway Source Data item Picker under a group called Metadata.

•  Metadata along with the data for the specified custom query.

All Historian analog and state summary tags and tag groups are excluded from the Source Data item Picker.

The maximum number of source data items that is recommended to be used for optimal performance is 25,000.
If the data source contains more than 25,000 tags, apply a filter to limit the amount of data items returned.

You must provide the Historian server name to configure the connection information for the Historian. The
Historian data adapter uses the Windows authentication mode and the Intelligence Data Adapter Service
credentials to connect to the Historian.

The minimum and maximum aggregate calculations are not supported for string tags. However, you can use a
custom query to work around this.

The following custom query casts the string value to a decimal if the string value is numeric; otherwise, it sets
the value to null. When you use this query, you can perform the minimum and maximum aggregate calculations
on the DecimalValueOfSysString field. For more information, see Using Custom Queries to Create Custom Source
Data Items.

SELECT TagName, DateTime,

CASE WHEN (ISNumeric(vValue) = 1)

THEN

CAST (vValue as decimal)

ELSE

NULL

END) AS DecimalValueOfSysString
FROM History
WHERE TagName = 'StringUDO_000.TagValue'

AND DateTime >= [[RequestedStartTime]]
AND DateTime < [[RequestedEndTime]]

If you are using a custom query for a Historian data adapter and you are using column aliases, be sure that you
either do not create an alias for the DateTime column or, if you use an alias, that you duplicate the DateTime
column in the SELECT statement. The DateTime column is a reserved word for the time filtering. For example, if
the DateTime column has an alias called "TimeStamp" in the custom query, an error occurs and no measure
results are returned. However, the following query, which specifies the duplicate DateTime column, works:

SELECT DateTime AS TimeStamp, DateTime, Tagname AS Tagname, Value AS Value FROM history

WHERE tagname LIKE 'SysTimeHour'

AND DateTime > '10/03/2009 06:30'
AND DateTime < '10/03/2011 08:30'

If you use Historian tag data instead of metadata as a source for dimension data, there is a possibility that the
AVEVA Historian returns multiple records with the same date/time and value, but with a differing QualityDetail
value. If one or both of these fields (date/time and value) are being used as a key for the dimension, the
dimension will not be populated, as the dimension expects the key to be unique. To avoid this, configure date/
time, value, and quality detail to be dimension keys. Or, you could use a custom query on the AVEVA Historian
data source to filter the data based on the quality detail you expect, so that unwanted duplicate records are not
passed to the BI Gateway system.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 31

To configure the AVEVA Historian connection

1.

In the Data Adapter Type box, select AVEVA Historian.

2.

In the Data Source Configuration section, do the following:

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Data Sources

a.  In the Data Source time zone list, click the time zone of the database that the external data source

system is referencing.

The default time zone is the time zone of your computer. You can select the (UTC) Coordinated Universal
Time option if the external data source system being referenced uses UTC for its time fields.
You can click the Automatically adjust time for Daylight Savings check box if the time zone that you
select is affected by daylight saving time (DST). For more information, see Time Zone for a Data Source.

b.

In the Server name box, type the name of the AVEVA Historian server.

The Authentication box shows the default Windows Authentication mode. The AVEVA Historian data
adapter uses the Intelligence Data Adapter Service credentials.

c.

In the Tag Name Filter box, type a tag name pattern to limit the number of tags to be retrieved from the
AVEVA Historian depending on the filter string. The filter syntax is the same as using the Transact-SQL
LIKE comparison operator. For example, to retrieve only the tags starting with "Sys", enter Sys%.

3.  Click Connect. If the connection succeeds, the Getting schema notification message appears. It can take few

minutes to retrieve the schema from the data source. For more information about retrieving the schema, see
Retrieve the schema.

4.  Click Done and then click Save.

NOTE: For the Historian Data Adapter compatibility with Historian 2012 R2, the Hot Fix CR L00136281 is
needed on the Historian Server machine and for compatibility with Historian 2014 P01, the Hot Fix CR
L00136283 is required. Contact your distributor or the AVEVA Technical Support to obtain these hot fixes.

Configuring the AVEVA Enterprise Data Management Connection

You can configure the AVEVA Enterprise Data Management (EDM) data adapter to connect to the EDM Server
and retrieve the available eDNA history services.

You must have the EDM client installed on your local computer.

NOTE: It is expected that you are familiar with AVEVA Enterprise Data Management.

Before using the EDM data adapter, note the following:

•  EDM uses different terminology for certain items, including “Tag Names” similar to Point IDs. “Metadata” is

similar to Point Configuration Information.

•  Custom Queries are not supported.

•  Tag Groups are not supported.

•  SQL Server authentication is not required, all configuration is done in DNASYS.ini.

•  The EDM data adapter is compatible with systems containing 25,000 points or less.

To configure the AVEVA Enterprise Data Management connection

1.

In the Data Adapter Type box, select AVEVA Enterprise Data Management.

2.

In the Data Source Configuration section, do the following:

3.  Click Load eDNA history services and select the eDNA History Service you want to use with BI Gateway from

the list.

4.  Click Connect. If the connection succeeds, the Getting schema notification message appears.

5.  Click Continue to retrieve the schema from the data source. For more information about retrieving the

schema, see Retrieve the schema.

6.  Click Done and then click Save.

Configuring the AVEVA Insight Connection

The AVEVA Insight data adapter allows BI Gateway data source to use an AVEVA Insight as a source to which you
can map the dimensions and measures fields in the BI Gateway model.

Configuring the AVEVA Insight data adapter helps you interact with the Azure functions available within the
AVEVA Insight source. By default, the AVEVA Insight data adapter points to the Azure function available in the
“development” environment. You can use a custom Azure function by updating URL available inside the
<AzureFunctionUrl> </AzureFunctionUrl> tag within the OnlineInsight.DataAdapter.config file. The
OnlineInsight.DataAdapter.config file is located at C:\Program Files (x86)\Wonderware\Intelligence\Bin.

To configure the AVEVA Insight connection

1.

In the Data Adapter Type box, select AVEVA Insight.

2.

In the Data Source Configuration section, do the following:

a.  In the Data Source time zone list, the (UTC) Coordinated Universal Time option is by default selected.

You cannot edit the time zone for the AVEVA Insight data adapter.

b.

In the URL box, specify the AVEVA Insight source URL. The default URL is
“https://online.wonderware.com/apis/Historian/v2”. You can update the default URL by editing the URL
inside the <OnlineInsightUrl> </OnlineInsightUrl> tag in the OnlineInsight.DataAdapter.config file.

c.

In the Key box, specify the authentication key for the registered AVEVA Insight URL.

3.  Click Connect. A confirmation message appears.

4.  Click Done and then click Save.

NOTE: The AVEVA Insight adapter supports only custom queries. You can add or modify custom queries using
the Custom Query Expression editor. For more information, see Using Custom Queries to Create Custom
Source Data Items.

Configuring the PI System OLEDB Provider Connection

The PI System OLEDB Provider connection provides the functionality to retrieve the tag names, tag definitions,
and tag data from the PI System Server data source. This connection also retrieves tag data by applying
aggregates and grouping on the fields of a tag from the PI System Server data source for a specified duration. The
supported aggregates are: Average (Time Weighted), Count, Minimum, Maximum, and Sum.

Ensure that the required version of the PI System OLEDB Provider is installed on the computer where the
Intelligence Data Adapter Service is configured. For information on the required versions, see the Global
Customer Support web site.

For more information about PI System OLEDB Provider, refer to the OSIsoft Documentation.

To configure the PI System OLEDB Provider connection

1.

In the Data Adapter Type box, select PI System OLEDB Provider.

2.

In the Data Source Configuration section, do the following:

Editing a Data Source

You can edit any deployed or undeployed data source.

To edit a data source

1.  Open the BI Gateway Model Builder.

2.  Under the Data Sources column, click the data source that you want to edit. The data source editor appears

in the right pane with the selected data source details.

3.  Do the required modifications.

4.  Click Save.

Deploying and undeploying a Data Source

You deploy the data source to the BI Gateway Server. Data from deployed data sources is available to deployed
dependent dimensions and measures at run time. Be sure that you have defined the data source with the BI
Gateway server node name before deploying the data source.

To deploy a data source

1.  Open the BI Gateway Model Builder.

2.  Under the Data Sources column, click the data source that you want to deploy. The Data source editor

appears in the right pane with the selected data source details.

3.  On the Action menu, click the Deploy (

) button. After the successful deployment, a notification message

appears and the deploy status next to the data source name changes from yellow to green.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 39

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Data Sources

To undeploy a data source

1.  Open the BI Gateway Model Builder.

2.  Under the Data Sources column, click the data source that you want to undeploy. The Data source editor

appears in the right pane with the selected data source details.

3.  On the Action menu, click the Undeploy (

) button. After the successful undeployment, a notification

message appears and the deploy status next to the data source name changes from green to yellow.

Deleting a Data Source

You can delete a data source that is not deployed or is not referenced by a dependent dimension or measure.

To delete a data source

1.  Open the BI Gateway Model Builder.

2.  Under the Data Sources column, click the data source that you want to delete.

3.  On the Action menu, click the Delete (

) button. A confirmation message appears.

NOTE: The Delete button in the Data Source editor is available only when the selected data source is
undeployed.

4.  Click Delete.
