AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Measures

Configuring Data Collection Details for a Measure

You have to provide a data collection start date and time and optionally an offset time for a measure. You must
provide a refresh rate and update period for the measure. The measure is first populated on the start date and
time that you specify. Thereafter, the measure data is refreshed as per the configured refresh rate and the
update period.

You must provide an update period to define a time interval for which measure data has to be refreshed during
the measure refresh.

For example, if you specify a refresh rate of one hour and an update period of one day, then the system refreshes
the measure data for the past day after every one hour.

The system also triggers a check on the configured refresh rate to verify if there is a measure period or
dimension time-slices identified since the last refresh. New measure values are calculated for each time-slice
defined by measure period and time-slicing dimensions and the values are stored in the measure table.

For more information about measure period and dimension time-slicing, see Configuring a Measure Period and
"Configuring Time-Slicing for a Dimension".

You have to provide a measure data collection start date and time that is before or equal to the measure
deployment date. Upon deployment, the process of computing measure calculations for each time-slice between
the data collection start time and the current time is called measure backfilling. Thereafter, new measure values
are calculated as per the configured refresh rate and update period.

You can also provide an offset time to delay the refresh execution. Using an offset time can be useful to ensure
that transactions at the data source have completed prior to the extraction of data for the measure.

To configure a data collection start date, refresh rate, update period, and offset time for a measure

1.

In the Refresh rate area, specify a refresh rate and offset time for the measure. Do the following:

a.  In the Interval list, click a refresh rate for the measure.

b.

c.

In the Unit list, click a unit of time for the refresh rate. You can provide a minimum refresh rate of one
minute and a maximum refresh rate of 366 days. The default refresh rate is one day.

In the Offset (minutes) list, click an offset time in minutes. The offset time is used to delay the scheduled
refresh of the measure. You can provide an offset time of maximum 59 minutes. The default offset time
is zero minutes.

2.

In the Update period area, do the following:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 99

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Measures

a.  In the Interval list, click the required update period for the measure.

b.

In the Unit list, click the required unit of time for the update period. You can provide a minimum update
period of one millisecond and a maximum update period of 366 days. The default update period is one
day.Be sure that the update period is equal to or more than the refresh rate.

3.

In the Data backfill area, specify a data collection start date and time. In the Data collection start list, click
the start date and time to collect data from the data sources and populate the measure. You must provide a
start date and time prior to the measure deployment date. The default start date and time is 12:00:000 A.M.
(local time of the BI Gateway server) on the date of measure creation.

4.  Click Save.

Configuring a Measure Period

You can configure a measure period, which will force the computation of the measure calculations at the
specified period. For example, a Measure object configured with a measure period of one hour stores
calculations in the measure table for every hour since the data collection start time.

For each measure calculation, you must provide a source data item field mapping that is used to calculate period
for measure calculation.

Because measure records are described by a time-slice, a Measure object must at least have one time context
defined either by a measure period, and/or time-slicing dimensions. If you do not provide a measure period or
do not relate the measure to a time-slicing dimension, then after saving the measure the state of the measure
changes to "Warning" and measure values are not calculated.

To configure a measure period

1.  Select the Measure period check box.

2.

In the Interval list, type or click a period for measure calculation.

3.

In the Unit list, click the unit of time for the period.

You can provide a minimum period of one millisecond and a maximum period of 366 days. The default
measure period is one hour.

4.  Click Save.

Assigning Measure to a File group

To assign the measure to a pre-configured file group, do the following:

1.  Select the Override SQL Server default file group check box.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 100

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Measures

2.

In the File group name box, type the name of the pre-configured file group to which you want to associate
the measure. The file group name can contain a maximum of 128 characters.

•  File groups help support database growth and performance by storing data in different files.

•  You can associate a measure to a SQL Server file group. The measure table is created and the measure

data is stored on the server file group that you specify.

•  Before associating a measure to a file group, you must create and configure the file group in the SQL

Server where the data store resides.

•  If you do not associate a measure to a file group, then the measure table is created and the measure

data is stored in the default file group called "Primary".

•  Associating measures to different file groups helps distribute the data, thereby enhancing the

performance.

For more information about creating file groups, see the Microsoft SQL Server documentation.

NOTE: If the file group that you specify is not available in the SQL Server, then the system assigns the
measure to the default file group.

3.  To associate the measure to the default file group, clear the Override SQL Server default file group check

box.

Manage measure calculations

You can add, clone, rename, configure, and delete measure calculations.

There are two types of measure calculations:

•  Aggregates

You can configure aggregate calculations to execute a single operation on a single field. Aggregate
calculations serve as the base variables within a measure. You can use aggregate calculations as input for
expressions.

An aggregate calculates a summary function, such as average, minimum, and sum on the data set retrieved
from the source.

•  Expression

You can build an arithmetic expression using the configured aggregates.

An example for a simple calculation created from an expression is (AggregateA + AggregateB)/AggregateC.

Measure calculations are each represented by separate columns in the resulting measure table.

The column names are the same as the calculation names defined in the measure object.

You must define at least one measure calculation in a measure.

Adding a Measure Calculation

You can add a maximum of 75 calculations in a measure.

To add a measure calculation

1.  Open the BI Gateway Model Builder.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 101

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Measures

2.  Click the Add (

) icon in the Floating Action Buttons menu and then click the Measure (
new measure is added in the BI Gateway model with a default name and the Measure editor appears in the
right pane.

) button. A

3.  Click the Edit Calculations button. The Context & Calculations page appears.

4.  Click Add (+) to add a measure calculation. A measure calculation with a default name is added in the

Calculations pane.

5.  Type a new name for the measure calculation.

6.  Click Save.

Note: To delete a measure calculation, select the measure calculation in the Calculations pane, and then
click Delete.

Configuring an Aggregate Calculation

The BI Gateway service extracts data from data sources, determines time-slicing, computes aggregates, adds the
associated context, and stores this data in the datastore.

You can configure an aggregate calculation by providing a function, return data type, and source data item
mapping for calculating the time period and values (using the function).

You can set a refresh rate to specify the frequency at which the measure data will be refreshed at run time. You

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 102

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Measures

can also define the update period that allows you to define the time period for which data will be retrieved every
time the measure refreshes. The minimal update period you can select is 1 minute and the maximum is 366
days.

For example, you have a measure configured with an update period of 1 hour, and a refresh rate of 5 minutes.
Every 5 minutes, the BI Gateway services will query the last hour of data from the source and update the target
records in the datastore.

However, if a source record from the previous day is modified, it is not captured by the update period of 1 hour.
If the data source records the date or time at which records are modified, you can use the modification period
function. When you configure the Modification Period, the BI Gateway services will handle the records at the
source that have been modified since the last measure refresh, in addition to the records handled by the update
period.

To configure an aggregate calculation

1.  Click the Edit Calculations button. The Context & Calculations page appears.

2.

In the Calculations pane, click a calculation that you want to configure.

3.

In the Calculation type list, click Aggregate.

4.  Configure the aggregate calculation. In the Aggregate calculation area, do the following:

a.  Click Source data items to select a source data item in the Source data item box. The Select source data

items dialog box appears. Select a source data item that you want to use in the calculation. The

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 103

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Measures

Calculation field and Period field boxes show the source data item fields of the source data item that
you select.

b.

In the Function list, click a function for the calculation.

c.

In the Return type list, click a return data type for the function. The default return data type is Double.

d.

e.

In the Calculation field list, click the source data item field that you want use for the measure calculation
based on the function. This is the field which will be used for the data Aggregation.

In the Period field list, click the source data item field that will be used to select the source records for a
time-slice. For example, the source system stores data values with a timestamp. The Time Stamp field
would be used here to query the appropriate records for each of the time-slices.

5.

In the Modification Period area, do the following:

a.  In the Modification field list, click the source data item field that you want to use for the calculation. The
default value is set as "None". This default value indicates that the measure data update using a record
modification date function is disabled.

The Modification field, selected from the available fields of the source data item, is used as an indication
for the source record modification date or time. When the Modification field is specified, the BI Gateway
service retrieves the measure source records that have been modified since the last update, in addition
to the records already covered by the update period interval. The default value (set as "None") indicates
that the measure data update using a record modification date feature is disabled.

b.

In the Interval list, click the required time interval of the Modification period.

You can enter a time interval value only if you specify a Modification field. You cannot enter a decimal
value for time interval. You can use the modification interval to limit the number of source records that
are scanned for record modification dates. This function is useful when there is no index on the record
modification date in the source database but only on the Period field. The BI Gateway service scans only
the source records that have the Period field within the modification interval period. The modification
interval is not used if the source database has an index on the Modification field. When the modification
interval is not used, you can set its value to zero.

c.

In the Unit list, click the required unit of time for the Modification period.

The default value for Unit is Minute. You can select a time interval unit after the Modification field is
populated.

6.  You must define relationships for the measure in the Context Relationships grid.

7.  Click Save.

Configuring an Expression Calculation

An expression allows you to create a simple calculation for which you must use aggregates.

An example for a simple calculation created from an expression is (AggregateA + AggregateB)/AggregateC.

You can build an expression calculation by using the SQL arithmetic operators and aggregate calculations.

When you create an expression, you must ensure that the execution of the expression will not trigger SQL
exceptions. For example, the expression should include logic to catch a potential division by zero. If such
exceptions occur, the object reports an error at each scheduled execution and record insertion will stop at the
time of the error.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 104

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Measures

To configure an expression calculation

1.  Click the Edit Calculations button.

2.

In the Context & Calculations pane, click a calculation that you want to configure.

3.

In the Calculation type list, click Expression.

4.

In the Return type list, click a return data type for the expression. The default return data type is Double.

5.

In the Expression area, build an expression calculation. Do the following:

a.  In the Available calculations area, double-click an aggregate calculation that you want to use in

expression calculation. The Available calculations area shows the aggregate calculations of the current
measure that have numeric return type.

b.

In the Operators area, click an operator to use in expression calculation.

The expression built in the SQL Expression editor must be a valid Microsoft SQL Server expression.

6.  Click Save.

Selecting Dimensions to Define the Context for a Measure

A measure is an aggregated value. You often need to provide a context to the aggregated value. To do this, you
relate a measure to a dimension.

You can select dimensions that define the context of the measure. For example, you can select the Product and
Shift dimensions and relate them to a Production measure. The Product and Shift dimensions define the context
for the Production measure and allow reporting production by Product and Shift.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 105

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Measures

You can select the same dimension more than once. For example, you can select a Material dimension twice to
define the Material Input and Material Output context for a measure.

By default, the system adds an alias name to the dimension that you select. Aliases must be unique within the
context definition of a measure. For more information, see "Defining Relationships for a Measure".

The measure table contains a column for the reference value for each dimension that the measure relates to.
The column name is the defined alias for the dimension.

To select dimensions for a measure

1.  Under the Measures column, click the measure for which you want to select the dimensions. The measure

editor appears.

2.  Click the Context tab.

3.  Click Add Dimensions. You can select a maximum of 30 dimensions.

4.

In the Changing dimensions area, select a dimension that you want to map to the measure. An alias with a
default name is added in the Changing dimensions pane under the Alias column. The Link option appears
unselected by default. For more information about renaming a dimension alias, see Rename a dimension
alias. For more information about configuring a context link for a dimension, see Select a Context Link
Dimension.

NOTE: You can click the Delete icon to remove a dimension. If you remove the dimension, the system deletes
all the relationships that reference the dimension alias in calculations within the measure. For more
information about measure calculations, see "Managing Measure Calculations".

Rename a dimension alias

You can rename a dimension alias. The alias name:

•  Must be unique within the measure.

•  Can contain a maximum of 100 characters and cannot start with the "aa" character.

Rename a dimension alias

1.  Under the Measures column, click the measure for which you want to rename the dimension alias. The

measure editor appears.

2.  Click the Context tab.

3.

In the Changing dimensions area, under the Alias column, double-click an alias, and type a new alias name.

4.  Click Add Dimensions.

Select a Context Link Dimension

A context is established by relating a measure to a dimension. Some of these dimensions can be defined as time
defining, and as such contribute to the definition of the time periods for which measures are aggregated. Time-
slices obtained from time defining dimensions can have an associated context. This enables you to reduce the
total number of time intervals for measure records.

A context link identifies a dimension by which time-slicing will be computed. For example, if equipment is
selected as the context link dimension, time-slices identified by the time defining dimension will be grouped by

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 106

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Measures

equipment, and stored as such in the measure table. A time-slice for equipment A will not be considered when
calculating the aggregates for equipment B.

For example, a measure has two selected dimensions in the context pane. The two selected dimensions are the
time defining dimension called Work Order and the Equipment dimension. The Equipment dimension link can be
used to provide context-based time-slicing. To enable this, drag the Link option for the Equipment dimension.
The Work Order dimension must have a dimension link to the Equipment dimension, since it is the context-link
dimension. Any other time defining dimension used in the context of the measure and having a dimension link to
Equipment will also be sliced according to equipment at run time.

For more information about time-slicing dimensions, see Configuring Time-Slicing for a Dimension.

You can select only one dimension as a context link dimension.

To select a context link dimension

1.  Under the Measures column, click the measure for which you want to select the dimensions. The measure

editor appears.

2.  Click the Context tab.

3.

In the Changing dimensions area, under the Link column, drag the Link option to enable.

4.  Click Add Dimensions.

Defining Relationships for a Measure

After you select dimensions for a measure, you must define relationships. Relationships help define context for
the measure.

There are three types of relationships:

•  Key relationship - You can relate to a dimension key field using a source data item field. This establishes the

relationship between the measure record and the selected dimension.

•  Dimension link relationship - You can relate a dimension using a linked dimension. This adds the linked

dimension reference in the measure table. For example, your source data defines values with respect to
equipment. However, you want to have the Site definition with your measure data. The Equipment
dimension contains a dimension link to Site. This allows you to add the Site dimension to the measure data
and use the linked dimension for site defined in the Equipment dimension.

•  Time-based relationship - You can provide a time-based relation for a dimension that has a time-slice

configured. The system automatically maps to the dimension using the data source DateTime field that you
define for aggregate calculation.

For example, you want to have Shift context on an Historian aggregate value. If Shift is defined as a time-slicing
dimension, you can use a time relationship to relate the timestamp of the Historian data to the time-slices
defined by the Shift dimension.

You can either provide a dimension link relationship or you must provide a relationship for all the key fields of a
dimension. For example, if you select a dimension that has four key fields that form a composite key, then you
must provide a relationship for all the four key fields. However, you can provide only one time-based relationship
for a key field out of the four key fields.

If you are providing a dimension link relationship, then you do not need to provide a mapping for all the other
key fields of the dimension.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 107

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Measures

To define a relationship for a measure

1.  Open the BI Gateway Model Builder.

2.  Click the Add (

) icon in the Fast Action Buttons menu and then click the Measure (

) button. A new

measure is added in the BI Gateway model with a default name and the Measure editor appears in the right
pane.

3.  Click the Edit Calculations button. The Context & Calculations page appears.

4.

In the Context & Calculations page, click a required aggregate calculation.

5.  Click the Context Relationships button. The Context page appears.

6.

In the Relationships grid, do the following:

The Dimensions column shows the alias names of dimensions that you select in the Context tab. One row is
added for each key field of a dimension.

The References column shows:

•  All the key fields (represented by a Key icon) of the current dimension.

•  Dimension links (represented by a Link icon) of the dimensions that you select in the Context tab, that
reference the current dimension. If you select the dimension link option for a dimension, then all other
rows for the dimension are unavailable.

•  If the corresponding dimension has time-slicing configured, then the dimension column also shows the

"TIME" option (represented by a Clock icon).

a.  Under the References column, click the required option.

b.  The Mappings column shows any one of the following depending on the option you select under the

References column:

•  If you select a dimension key, then it shows the fields of the source data item that is used for measure
calculation. You must select the field from the source data item which maps to the selected key field of
the dimension.

•  If you select a dimension link, then it shows the dimension of the selected dimension link.

•  If you select the TIME option, then the Mappings field is unavailable. The system automatically maps the

dimension to the data source DateTime field that you define for aggregate calculation.

•  Click the required option.

NOTE: If you select a dimension key field under the References column, then you can map the dimension key
field to a constant value. Be sure that the dimension key field type is compatible with the constant type.

7.  Click Save.

Cloning a Measure Calculation

You can create a new calculation by copying or cloning the attributes of an existing one. For example, if you want
to copy the attributes of a maximum value calculation to create a minimum value calculation, select the
maximum calculation in the calculations list and click the clone calculation button. You can then modify the
required attributes of the maximum calculation to define it as a minimum calculation. You can clone attributes
for aggregate, expression, and advanced calculations.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 108

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Measures

To clone a measure calculation

1.  Click the Calculations tab.

2.

In the Calculations pane, click the calculation that you want to clone.

3.  Click Clone Calculation

 to copy the attributes of the measure calculation that you have selected. A

duplicate calculation is created and displayed under the original calculation in the Calculations pane.

4.  Click Save.

NOTE: To delete a measure calculation, you can select the measure calculation in the Calculations pane, and
then click Delete.

Selecting Multiple Source Data Items for a Measure Calculation

You can select multiple source data items from any of the configured data sources to configure measure
calculations.

To select multiple source data items for a measure calculation

1.  Click the Edit Calculations button.

2.

In the Context & Calculations editor, click the required aggregate calculation.

3.  Click Select source. The Select source data items dialog box appears.

The Source data items area shows the available filtered schema of the data sources.

4.  Click Expand to expand the data source and view the source data items within the data source.

Note: You can use the Find box to search a source data item.

5.  Select a source data item from which you want to expose the fields to add the source data item. Deselect a

source data item to move an item back to the Source data items area.

The names in a data source table should not contain the special character, semicolon (;). The names that
contain special characters like semicolon (;) and (#) do not appear in Source data items pane.

The bottom grid of the dialog box shows the fields of the source data item that you select in the Source data
items pane. The first source (from the top of the list) providing a value for the field will be used in the
resulting dimension table.

6.  Drag to enable the Preview source data option to view sample data from the data source.

7.  Click OK.

The source data items and source data item fields are exposed to the measure. You can use the exposed
source data items and source data item fields to configure measure calculations.

For more information, see Adding a Measure Calculation.

Configuring a Measure Filter

You can build a filter expression for the aggregate calculations to select a subset of the transformed data from
external data sources and populate the measure table in the data store. You can configure only one filter per
aggregate calculation.

For more information about aggregate calculations, see Configuring an Aggregate Calculation.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 109

You can build a filter expression by using the dimension fields and the expression operators.

To configure a measure filter

1.  Open the BI Gateway Model Builder.

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Measures

2.  Click the Add (

) icon in the Floating Action Buttons menu and then click the Measure (
new measure is added in the BI Gateway model with a default name and the Measure editor appears in the
right pane.

) button. A

3.  Click the Edit Calculations button. The Context & Calculations page appears.

4.

In the Context & Calculations page, click a required aggregate calculation.

5.

In the Calculations area, click the required aggregate calculation.

6.  Click Expand to expand the Filter expression editor.

NOTE: You can click Collapse to collapse the Filter expression editor.

7.

In the Available aggregates area, click the aggregate calculation for which you want to configure a filter.

8.  Build a filter expression. Do the following:

•  In the Available context Filter Expression, specify an expression operator to add to the Filter Expression

editor.

You can use the open and close parenthesis () to define sub-comparison expressions and single quotes
'abc' as string delimiters. The filter expression must be a valid SQL WHERE clause.

For more information about SQL WHERE clauses, see the Microsoft SQL Server documentation.

9.  Click the Validate button to manually validate the expression syntax.

NOTE: Alternatively, the system automatically validates the expression syntax after you save the measure.

10.  Click Save.

Edit a measure

You can modify the measure configuration. If you edit and check in a deployed measure, then the state of the
measure changes to "Pending Changes". Editing a measure does not impact other BI Gateway objects.

Edit a measure

1.  Open the BI Gateway Model Builder.

2.  Under the Measures column, click the measure that you want to edit. The Measure editor appears with the

selected measure details.

3.  Do the required modifications.

4.  Click Save.

Deleting a Measure

If you delete a measure, then the configuration, mappings, and relationships of the measure are deleted.
However, the measure table and data are not removed from the datastore.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 110

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Measures

To delete a measure

1.  Open the BI Gateway Model Builder.

2.  Under the Measures column, click the measure that you want to delete.

3.  On the Action menu, click the Delete (

) button. A confirmation message appears.

NOTE: The Delete button in the Measures editor is available only when the selected measure is undeployed.

4.  Click Delete.

Deploying and undeploying a Measure

You deploy the measure to the BI Gateway.

To deploy a measure

1.  Open the BI Gateway Model Builder.

2.  Under the Measures column, click the measure that you want to deploy. The Measures editor appears in the

right pane with the selected measure details.

3.  On the Action menu, click the Deploy (

) button. A notification message appears in the green color.

4.  Click Yes. After the successful deployment the the deploy status next to the measure name changes from

yellow to green.

To undeploy a measure

1.  Open the BI Gateway Model Builder.

2.  Under the Measures column, click the dimension that you want to undeploy. The Measures editor appears in

the right pane with the selected measure details.

3.  On the Action menu, click the Undeploy (

) button. A notification message appears in the green color.

4.  Click Yes. After the successful undeployment, the deploy status next to the measure name changes from

green to yellow.

Models

Asset Model Concept

Export and import models

Deploying, Undeploying, and Deleting the BI Gateway Model

Asset Model Concept

This chapter provides an example on how to implement the Asset Model concept leveraging the AVEVA Insight
Data Adapter. These implementation strategies and rules can be extended to the on-premises data sources
(Historian, MES, and so on).

The following objectives should be considered while creating an asset model:

•  BI Gateway operates as an abstraction layer by processing the dimensional model regardless of the distinct

assets and FQN/Tagnames to consider.

•  BI Gateway needs to resolve

•  “what to do” (e.g. populate a dimension, compute a measure calculation, execute backfill processing,
run a scheduled refreshed, etc.) from the dimensional model and its internal mechanism (custom
queries, scheduler, etc.).

•  “How to do” (e.g. which FQN to consider for populating batch dimension, which retrieval mode to

consider, does it require dataset manipulation, which asset and asset’s behavior to consider, etc.) from
the asset model capability of AVEVA Insight.

Asset model structure

Asset model concept can be configured using the BI Gateway Insight Data Adapter for:

•  Managed Historian – Tagname Dictionary (built-in and Tag extended properties)

•  MES - Equipment Efficiency Model

•  CIM – Common Intelligent Model

It can be combined in such a way as:

1.  Basic asset model

•  Managed Historian only

2.

Intermediate asset model

•  Managed Historian

•  MES Efficiency

3.  Advanced asset model (Future Release)

•  Common Intelligent Model

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 116

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Models

REST APIs

AVEVA Insight contents is mainly exposed through REST APIs which allow interacting with the solution such as:

•  Historian Data REST API (Managed Historian API)

•  Equipment Efficiency REST API (MES API)

•  Common Intelligent Model REST API (CIM API)

For more information about available API endpoints, see Using Custom Queries to Create Custom Source Data
Items.

Each of the above APIs allow upload or retrieval requests using a web browser or a client-side applications such
as:

•  Microsoft Excel (2013, 2016, or Office 365)

•  Business Intelligence (BI) systems

•  Tableau

•  Microsoft Power BI

•  Postman

Basic Asset Model

Basic asset model only leverages Tagname Dictionary of Managed Historian (Historian API) and its capability of
configuring extended properties for grouping Tagname per a field value expected by BI Gateway model objects.

Scenario

Scope of this scenario is to define 2 dimensions and 1 measure calculation such as:

•  Asset dimension reflects all asset and plant available as part of the Tagname Dictionary leveraging either the

built-in Location field or any custom Tag extended properties.

•  Batch dimension as type of Time Defining Dimension reflects all batches that have been operated against

existing assets. This dimension retrieves tagname historical data and returns all batches (batch ID, Start Time,
End Time, etc.) per key fields such as Asset and Plant.

•  Power measure’s calculation computes the energy spent by the context of Asset, Plant, and Batch.

The Asset dimension is populated by retrieving all distinct value of the custom Tag extended properties named
“BIGateway_Asset” and “BIGateway_Plant” across the whole Tagname Dictionary.

The Batch dimension retrieves all tagnames matching the criteria BIGateway_Dimension eq ‘Batch’ and
subsequently returns all historical events associated to this tag list.

The Power calculation retrieves all tagnames matching the criteria BIGateway_MeasureCalculation eq ‘Power’
and subsequently returns aggregated historical data associated to per requested time slice.

Note: BI Gateway datasource object uses Custom Query expression which facilitates the configuration of the
query statement (See Query Expression section) for retrieving and manipulating result set accordingly to the
expected output schema.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 117

Configuring Tagname Extended Property

Following is an example of a JSON file, which is used to create Tagname Extended Properties leveraging basic
asset model concept.

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Models

Sample:

{
"metadata": [
{
"TagName": "Asset0001.Batch",
"BIGateway_Asset": {"DataType":"String", "Value":"Asset0001"},
"BIGateway_Dimension": {"DataType":"String", "Value":"Batch"},
"BIGateway_MeasureCalculation": {"DataType":"String", "Value":""}
},
{
"TagName": "Asset0001.Power",
"BIGateway_Asset": {"DataType":"String", "Value":"Asset0001"},
"BIGateway_Dimension": {"DataType":"String", "Value":""},
"BIGateway_MeasureCalculation": {"DataType":"String", "Value":"Power"}
}
]
}

For more information about defining extended tag properties, see the Define extended tag properties section in
the AVEVA Insight Help.

Intermediate Asset Model

Intermediate asset model leverages both Tagname Dictionary (Historian API) and MES Efficiency (MES API) parts
and their capabilities of configuring and hosting asset model information.

For more information, see the Manage the efficiency model section in the AVEVA Insight Help.

Scenario

Scope of this scenario is to define 3 dimensions and 1 measure calculation such as:

•  Asset dimension reflects all Asset and Plant available as part of the Equipment Efficiency Model.

•  Batch dimension as type of Time Defining Dimension reflects all batches that have been operated against

existing assets. This dimension returns all batches (batch ID, Start Time, End Time, etc.) per key fields such as
Asset and Plant.

•  UtilizationEvent dimension as type of Time Defining Dimension reflects all utilization events and their reason
that assets have been exposed to. This dimension returns all utilization event (Event ID, Start Time, End Time,
etc.) per key fields such as Asset and Plant.

•  Power measure’s calculation computes the energy spent by the context of Asset, Plant, Batch, and

UtilizationEvent.

The Asset dimension is populated by retrieving all equipment and location (hierarchy) from the Equipment
Efficiency Model (MES API).

The Batch dimension retrieves all tagnames matching the criteria BIGateway_Dimension eq ‘Batch’ and

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 118

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Models

subsequently returns all historical events associated to this tag list.

The UtilizationEvent dimension retrieves all utilization events (MES API).

The Power calculation retrieves all tagnames matching the criteria BIGateway_MeasureCalculation eq ‘Power’
and subsequently returns aggregated historical data associated to per requested time slice.

Configuring Tagname Extended Property

Here is an example of JSON file for creating Tagname Extended Properties leveraging Intermediate asset model
concept.

Sample:

{
"metadata": [
{
"TagName": "Asset0001.Batch",
"BIGateway_Asset": {"DataType":"String", "Value":"Asset0001"},
"BIGateway_Dimension": {"DataType":"String", "Value":"Batch"},
"BIGateway_MeasureCalculation": {"DataType":"String", "Value":""}
},
{
"TagName": "Asset0001.Power",
"BIGateway_Asset": {"DataType":"String", "Value":"Asset0001"},
"BIGateway_Dimension": {"DataType":"String", "Value":""},
"BIGateway_MeasureCalculation": {"DataType":"String", "Value":"Power"}
}
]
}

For more information about defining extended tag properties, see the Define extended tag properties section in
the AVEVA Insight Help.

Export and import models

To help you create and manage items more efficiently, you can export BI Gateway Models from an existing
system to a .json file and then import the .json file when you create a new BI Gateway Model at a later date. You
can also use the import functionality to update an existing model.

Export a BI Gateway Model

Import a BI Gateway Model

Exporting a BI Gateway Model

You can export an existing BI Gateway Model to a .json file. The export considers the latest version of objects.

To export a BI Gateway Model

1.  From the BI Gateway Model Home page, click Export All from the main menu. A confirmation message

appears.

2.  Click Done to finish exporting the Model. The Exported Model is save at the default location with a file name

Data.json.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 121

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Models

Importing a BI Gateway Model

You can import an existing BI Gateway Model of the same version using the exported Data.json file.

The following import rules are followed while performing the import of BI Gateway Model:

•  All the objects are imported from Export File.

•  If any objects are existing, you can choose to either 'Overwrite' or 'Create New' objects.

•  After import, all newly created objects are in an undeployed state, irrelevant of the state they were in at the

export time.

•  If any objects are existing and are in the deployed state, they are in 'Deployed with pending changes' state (a

new version is created).

•  If any objects are existing and are in an undeployed state, they are overwritten.

•  If any objects are existing, and are in 'Deployed with pending changes' state, this version is overwritten.

•  All relationships between objects are preserved.

To import a BI Gateway Model of the same version

1.  From the BI Gateway Model Home page, click Import from the main menu. The Import Model dialog box

appears.

2.

In the Import Model dialog box, click CHOOSE A FILE. The Open dialog box appears.

3.  Select the JSON file that you want to import and then click Open.

4.

In the Import Model dialog box, select any of the following

•  Overwrite: If you wan to overwrite an existing object or BI Gateway Model.

•  Create New: If you want to create a new object or BI Gateway Model.

5.  Specify the Override Data Backfill date if you want to set the data collection date which the system would

consider for pulling the data for measure or dimension during the import process.

6.  Click Import. The import process starts importing the model and a successful import message appears.

7.  Click Done.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 122

Deploying, Undeploying, and Deleting the BI Gateway Model

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Models

About BI Gateway Model Deployment

Deploying the BI Gateway Model

Undeploying the BI Gateway Model

Deleting the BI Gateway Model

About BI Gateway Model Deployment

You deploy the BI Gateway model, which includes the DataSource, Dimension, and Measure objects to the BI
Gateway. The deployment provides the BI Gateway model to the BI Gateway.

BI Gateway objects with invalid configurations are not deployed. A BI Gateway object configuration is considered
invalid if the configuration has changed from a previously deployed state, or if the representation is not created
in the datastore during run time.

The Explorer view shows an icon indicating the deployment status of the BI Gateway objects. When a new BI
Gateway object is created, it is shown as "not deployed" and is updated to "deployed" after deployment.

Once an Object is deployed to the Runtime, you cannot make changes to the deployed object. A copy of the
object is created if it detects any requested changes to allow users to continue working with the object, even
though it is deployed.

When you open an object in the BI Gateway, it references and displays as applicable, the latest version of all of
its dependee objects, regardless of the deployment state.

Dependent Object

Data Source

Dimension

Measure

Dependee Object

None

Data Source, Dimension

Data Source, Dimension

This is based on the assumption that only one Object can be loaded at a time by the BI Gateway. When a
dependee object that is deployed, is further modified and saved, and a dependent object is opened, the
dependent object will automatically load the dependencies based on the latest version of the dependee object
and not the deployed version. This implies that there is not a way to view the dependent object along with its
dependencies from a version of the dependee object that is not the latest.

Any saved modifications done to the dependee object as seen from within a dependent object, will need to be
reconciled with a dependent object as required. Further, a dependent object cannot be deployed, when a
corresponding dependee object is in an undeployed state or is in deployed with pending changes state. A
message is shown to the user indicating the dependee objects that should be in deployed state. Similarly, an
object cannot be deleted if it has one or more dependent objects. The dependencies will first need to be
removed to delete the object.

Even if a dependee object has a change that is not directly related to changes done in a dependent object, a
dependee object cannot be in an undeployed or deployed with pending changes state when a dependent object
is deployed.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 123

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Models

Deploying the BI Gateway Model

You deploy a BI Gateway model or BI Gateway objects to BI Gateway. You can:

•  Deploy the entire BI Gateway model.

•  Deploy individual BI Gateway objects or deploy BI Gateway objects with their parent dependents. Measure

to Dimensions (and Dimension links) and Measure / Dimensions to Data Sources together.

•  Deploy BI Gateway objects of the same group together.

Data from deployed data sources is available to deployed dependent dimensions and measures at run time. If
you deploy objects of different groups together, then the order of deployment is: Data Sources, Dimensions, and
then Measures. If you deploy the parents objects, all the dependent objects will also be updated.

To deploy the BI Gateway Model

1.  Open the BI Gateway Home page.

2.  Click Deploy All from the main menu to deploy the entire BI Gateway model. The Deploy All Objects dialog

box appears.

3.  Click any of the following:

•  Cancel

•  Keep Data: if you want to retain the existing data in the datastore. You must rename and redeploy the

new dimension or measure.

NOTE: 'Keep Data' may still result in a complete drop of the relevant dimension or measure data if the
structure changed.

•  Remove Data: if you want to remove the existing data in the datastore. You must rename and redeploy

the new dimension or measure.

NOTE: To deploy from an individual object, click the Deploy

 icon.

Undeploying the BI Gateway Model

You undeploy a BI Gateway model from BI Gateway. You can:

•  Undeploy the BI Gateway model.

•  Undeploy individual BI Gateway objects.

If you undeploy:

•  Multiple objects together, then the order of undeployment is: measures, dimensions, and then data sources.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 124

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Models

•  A data source only, then the dimensions and measures cannot access the data source. The on-going data

collection process from the data source fails and reports errors in the logger.

•  A dimension or measure, then the on-going data collection process for the dimension or measure is

terminated.

•  A dimension or measure, then the system prompts you to retain or remove the dimension or measure table

in the datastore. If you choose to retain the table on undeployment, then the table is retained but not
refreshed. If you choose to delete the table from the datastore, then the table is deleted from the datastore.

To undeploy BI Gateway Model

1.  Open the BI Gateway Home page.

2.  Click Undeploy All from the main menu to deploy the entire BI Gateway model. The Undeploy All Objects

dialog box appears.

3.  Click any of the following:

•  Cancel

•  Keep Data: if you want to retain the dimension or measure data in the datastore on undeployment.

•  Remove Data: if you want to delete the dimension or measure data from the datastore on

undeployment.

NOTE: To undeploy individual objects, click the Undeploy (

) icon in the Object Editor pane.

Deleting the BI Gateway Model

1.

If the model that you want to delete has been deployed, then undeploy the model. For more information
about undeploying a model, see Undeploying the BI Gateway Model.

2.  Delete objects based on the object dependencies.

a.  To delete a dimension with a dependent measure, delete the measure first and then delete the

dimension. For more information about deleting measures and dimensions, see Deleting a Measure and
Deleting a Dimension, respectively.

b.  To delete a data source with a dependent dimension or measure, delete the measure first, then the
dimension, and finally delete the data source. For more information about deleting measures,
dimensions, and data sources, see Deleting a Measure, Deleting a Dimension, and Deleting a Data
Source, respectively.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 125

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Datastores

Datastores

The BI Gateway datastore contains dimension and measure data that is stored based on the deployed BI
Gateway Model configuration.

Managing the Datastore

About the Datastore

Purging Data from the Datastore

Copying Data from the Datastore

About the Datastore

You can delete (purge) or copy data from the BI Gateway datastore.

Purging the data deletes the dimension or measure data from the datastore permanently, thereby increasing the
available disk space in the computer where the datastore resides.

You can copy the measure or dimension data from the BI Gateway datastore to a pre-configured offline database
in a local or remote server. You can use the copied data for analysis or reporting.

Purging Data from the Datastore

You can purge the dimension or measure data to permanently delete the data from the datastore. You can
manually purge the data or schedule an event using the SQL Server Management Studio. It is recommended that
you backup the datastore before purging any data.

You can specify a data purge execution date and time to manually purge the data. Alternatively, you can schedule
a purge job and a data purge execution event. The purge job triggers a check to verify whether there was any
data purge execution event since the last purge event.

If you manually specify a data purge execution date, then the data before the execution date is deleted from the
datastore.

If you schedule a purge job and an execution event, for example, a purge job of one month, then at the end of
each month the system triggers a check to identify if there is any data purge execution date during the last one
month. The identified date is the Period End date. The data before the Period End date is deleted. The data with
End Time values (aaValuePeriodEndTime) older than the Period End date and time that you specify is deleted
from the datastore.

You can also specify the purge duration in days if you want to purge data for specific days. For example, if you
schedule a purge job of one month and the purge job identifies a data purge execution date as August 31, 2020,
then the Period End date is August 31, 2020 minus one month, that is, July 31, 2020. If you do not specify the
duration, then the data before July 31, 2020 is purged. If you specify a duration, for example, 15 days, then the
data before 15 days from July 31, 2020 is purged. That is, the data between July 16, 2020 and July 31, 2020 is
purged.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 126

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Datastores

The system purges:

•  The measure data that has End Time values (aaValuePeriodEndTime) older than the Period Enddate and time

that you specify.

•  For a dimension that has a time-slice configured, the dimension records where the End Time

values(aaValuePeriodEndTime) are older than the Period End date and time that you specify and
thedimension records are not referenced by any other record in the datastore.

When you attempt to purge data, there is always a latency of 24 hrs. For example, the current date and time is
August 31, 2020, 09:00:000 A.M. and the data purge Period End date and time is August 31, 2020, 09:00:000
A.M. The system purges the data before to August 30, 2020, 00:00:000 A.M. and does not delete the data
between August 30, 2020, 00:00:000 A.M. and August 31, 2020 09:00:000 A.M.

Managing the Datastore For more information on configuring the data purge job provided by BI Gateway using
SQL Server

Management Studio, see the Microsoft SQL Server documentation.

Note: To get the archive/purge jobs, contact technical support and request for Hotfix #2891262.

Copying Data from the Datastore

You can copy the measure or dimension data from the BI Gateway datastore to a pre-configured offline database
in a local or remote server. Data copying is configured using the Microsoft SQL Server Management Studio. You
must specify the server name and the database name to which you want to copy the measure or dimension
data.

You can specify a data copy start and end date to manually copy the data. Alternatively, you can schedule a copy
job and a data copy execution date. The copy job triggers a check to verify whether there was any data copy
execution date during the given data copy period.

You can specify a start and end date and time, also called Absolute time. The data between the start and end
date and time is copied to the pre-configured database.

Alternatively, you can schedule an event by specifying a duration in days to copy the data. You can also specify
the offset days. For example, if you schedule a copy job of one month and specify the duration as 15 days, and
the copy job identifies a data copy execution date as August 31, 2020, then the data for 15 days prior to August
31, 2020 is copied. If you specify a duration of five days, then the data for 15 days prior to five days before
August 31, 2020 is copied. That is, data for 15 days prior to August 27, 2020 is copied.

The system copies the measure data along with the related dimension data for the specified date range.

The system merges the copied data with an existing data that was copied previously in the specified database.
The data store database schema is retained in the specified database.

For more information on configuring the data copy job provided by the BI Gateway using SQL Server
Management Studio, see the Microsoft SQL Server documentation.

How is the Datastore Updated at Runtime?

The datastore is updated at run time if you modify the configuration and redeployed a dimension or measure.

If you redeploy a dimension after modifying the dimension configuration and the modified configuration affects
the dependent measures, then the dimension triggers an alert to the dependent measure.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 127

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Datastores

Similarly, if you redeploy a measure after modifying the measure configuration, then the measure values are
recalculated if required.

Datastore Updates Resulting from Modifications to Deployed Dimensions

The following table lists the scenarios when data in the datastore is updated after a dimension has been
modified and redeployed.

Modification done to a deployed dimension

Run time handling of the datastore

A dimension is undeployed and the option to delete
the dimension table in the datastore is selected.
Thereafter, the dimension configuration is modified
and the dimension is redeployed.

A dimension is renamed.

A new dimension key field is selected.

The time-defining fields in a dimension are modified,
inserted, or deleted. Thereafter, the dimension is
redeployed.

Deletes the existing dimension table and creates a
new dimension table based on the new configuration.

Renames the dimension table in the datastore.
Updates the dependent measures for dimension name
related dependencies.

Deletes existing rows, starts dimension processing
afresh. Deletes the data in the measure tables of the
dependent measures and starts measure processing
afresh.

Reprocesses and updates the dependent measure
records.

Page 130

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Analytics Client Data Source Files

Analytics Client Data Source Files

You can use the Analytics Client Data Source Generator to generate Analytics Client Data Source files that are
compatible with BI Gateway Analytics Client. These files are also known as Tableau Data Source (.tds) files.

About the Analytics Client Data Source Generator

The BI Gateway Analytics Client enables you to analyze data contained in the BI Gateway datastore, and publish
dashboards to the Dashboard Server. The Analytics Client needs information about the location of the data store,
the measures, and their relationships to dimensions. This information is available in a Tableau Data Source (.tds)
file. The Analytics Client Data Source Generator creates a .tds file for each measure table in the BI Gateway
datastore. You can then analyze data in the BI Gateway datastore by opening the appropriate .tds file in the BI
Gateway Analytics Client.

The Analytics Client Data Source Generator generates the .tds files automatically every hour. You can also
manually trigger the generation of the .tds files using the Analytics Client Data Source Generator.

The Analytics Client Data Source Generator is installed as a component of the Analytics Client feature in the BI
Gateway installation program.

About the Analytics Client Data Source Files

The Analytics Client Data Source Generator generates the Analytics Client Data Source files. These files contain:

•  Data store connection information.

•  Relationship definitions between the measure and dimension tables.

•  Correct identification of dimension and measure fields. For example, a Numeric Dimension field is correctly

identified as a dimension and not a measure in the Analytics Client.

•  Internal table columns (primary and foreign keys, dimension table and measure table columns) that are

marked as hidden columns.

The generator creates a .tds file for each of the measures in the model.

The generated files are named after the measures in the client model. For example, if a model has 3
measures—measure001, measure002 and measure003—the generated files will be called measure001.tds,
measure002.tds and measure003.tds respectively.

Starting the Analytics Client Data Source Generator

When you install the BI Gateway Analytics Client on your system, the Analytics Client Data Source Generator is
also automatically installed. Only one instance of Analytics Client Data Source Generator can run on the system.
When you start the generator, no window appears. The program starts directly in the system tray.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 131

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Analytics Client Data Source Files

To start the Analytics Client Data Source Generator

•  On the Windows Start menu, click All Programs, AVEVA BI Gateway and then click Analytics Client Data

Source Generator.

NOTE:
- If you are using Windows Server 2012 R2, click Start and then on the Start menu, type Analytics Client Data
Source Generator in the Search box. Click Analytics Client Data Source Generator when displayed in search
results.

- After the system restarts after this feature is installed, the Analytics Client Data Source Generator starts
automatically and is visible in the system tray.

Exiting the Analytics Client Data Source Generator

When you click Exit, the icon is removed from the system tray. You can start the application again from the
Windows Start menu. If you need to remove Analytics Client Data Source Generator, you must uninstall the BI
Gateway Analytics Client feature from BI Gateway.

To exit the Analytics Client Data Source Generator

•  Right-click the Analytics Client Data Source Generator icon and click Exit to close the Analytics Client Data

Source Generator window.

Configuring the Analytics Client Data Source Generator

You must enter a valid BI Gateway Server name and the target data source folder name to configure the Analytics
Client Data Source Generator.

To configure Analytics Client Data Source Generator

1.  Right-click the Analytics Client Data Source Generator icon on the system tray, and click Configure. The

Analytics Client Data Source Generator dialog box appears.

2.

In the BI Gateway Server box, type a valid BI Gateway Server name that is available. The default server name
is "localhost".

If the changes are applied for a server name that is invalid or unavailable, a warning message appears. Until
you specify a valid server name that is available, no changes are made to the system.

3.

In the Data Source Folder box, browse to select the data source folder name where the Analytics Client Data
Source file will be generated. By default, the box displays C:\Documents and Settings\User1\My Documents\
My Tableau Repository\Datasources, where "User1" represents the currently logged-in user.

If the default folder <... My Tableau Repository\Datasources> cannot be found, then the default folder shown
in the box is the user’s <My Documents> folder.

If you apply the changes on an invalid data source folder name, a warning message appears. No changes to
the system occur until you specify a valid data source folder name.

NOTE: The <... My Tableau Repository...> folder structure is created in the <My Documents> only when the
BI Gateway Analytics Client is started the first time.

4.  Click Apply. The configuration is saved. If the configuration is successful, then the Apply button becomes

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 132

AVEVA™ BI Gateway On-Premises
Analytics Client Data Source Files

unavailable, implying that the configuration is validated and saved.

5.  Click Generate to create the data source files in the target folder.

6.

If you need to discard the configuration changes or re-configure the Analytics Client Data Source Generator,
click Cancel.

7.  Click OK to close the Analytics Client Data Source Generator dialog box. This also saves the current

configuration.

Generating the Analytics Client Data Source File

Before generating the files, you must configure the BI Gateway Server name and data source folder name where
the generated .tds files will be stored. For more information, see Configuring the Analytics Client Data Source
Generator.

The application automatically generates or updates the files every one hour after the application starts, and the
first file is generated immediately after the start up. You can also see the last generation time of the data source
files. If you move the pointer over the system tray icon, you can view the last action performed by the
application.

Every time a new data source file is generated, it automatically overwrites the existing file.

Note: Dashboards that consume an older version of .tds files are not affected by any change to those .tds files.

To generate Analytics Client data source files

•  Do any of the following:

•  Right-click the Analytics Client Data Source Generator icon on the system tray, and click Generate.

•  In the Analytics Client Data Source Generator dialog box, click Generate.

The .tds file is generated and stored in the data source folder that you have specified while configuring Analytics
Client Data Source Generator.

When a .tds file is being generated, the Analytics Client Data Source Generator icon changes, and the Configure
and Generate options on the context menu are unavailable.

Accessing Program Information

You can get information on the product name, copyright, and version number.

To access program information

•  Right-click the Analytics Client Data Source Generator icon and click About.

Accessing the Help File

You can access the Help file for information.

To access the Help file

•  In the Analytics Client Data Source Generator dialog box, click Help.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Guide.

You open Tableau Data Source files using the BI Gateway Analytics client.

To open a Tableau data source file

1.  On the Windows Start menu, click AVEVA BI Gateway > BI Gateway Analytics Client.

Or

Double-click the BI Gateway Analytics Client shortcut icon on the desktop.

Or

Search for BI Gateway Analytics Client in the Search box. Click BI Gateway Analytics Client when displayed
in search results.

Note: If you are using Windows Server 2012 R2, click Start and then on the Start menu, type Analytics Client
in the Search box. Click Analytics Client when displayed in search results.

The BI Gateway Analytics Client dialog box appears.

2.  On the File menu, click Open. The Open dialog box appears.

3.  Select the .tds file you want to open.

4.  Click OK.
